## ἔα (id: 001458000000000)

ἔα
==

ἔα (G1436)

ptcl

88\.191 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Anger, Be Indignant With

ah an exclamatory particle indicating surprise, indignation, or anger If another language does not have a fully satisfactory particle expressing surprise, indignation, or anger, it is probably best to leave this particle untranslated, as, for example, in a number of translations into English which make no attempt to introduce a corresponding emotive particle.

[LUK 4:34](https://ref.ly/Mark4:34)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 4:34

## ἐάνπερ (id: 001460000000000)

ἐάνπερ
======

ἐάνπερ (G1437a)

cj

89\.68 · Relations · Condition

if indeed, if surely an emphatic marker of condition, with the implication of reduced probability

[HEB 3:14](https://ref.ly/Phlm3:14) · [HEB 6:3](https://ref.ly/Phlm6:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 3:14; Hebrews 6:3


## ἑαυτοῦ (id: 001461000000000)

ἑαυτοῦ
======

ἑαυτοῦ (G1438) — ἑαυτῶν

refl.pron · αὐτός

92\.25 · Discourse Referentials · Whom or What Spoken or Written About

himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves in the singular, a reflexive reference to a person or thing spoken or written about,1 and in the plural, a reflexive reference to any and all persons or things involved as subjects of the clause (including first, second and third persons)

## ἐάω (id: 001462000000000)

ἐάω
===

ἐάω (G1439)

προσεάω

13\.138 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Happen

to allow, to let, to permit to allow someone to do something

[MAT 24:43](https://ref.ly/Matt24:43) · [LUK 4:41](https://ref.ly/Mark4:41) · [ACT 14:16](https://ref.ly/John14:16) · [ACT 16:7](https://ref.ly/John16:7) · [ACT 19:30](https://ref.ly/John19:30) · [ACT 23:32](https://ref.ly/John23:32) · [ACT 27:32](https://ref.ly/John27:32) · [ACT 27:40](https://ref.ly/John27:40) · [ACT 28:4](https://ref.ly/John28:4) · [1CO 10:13](https://ref.ly/Rom10:13)

68\.35 · Aspect · Cease, Stop

**Collocation:** ἐάω ἕως

stop, quit, cease (an idiom, literally: to leave off until) to cease from what one is doing, with the implication of strong admonition

[LUK 22:51](https://ref.ly/Mark22:51)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 24:43; Luke 4:41; Acts 14:16; Acts 16:7; Acts 19:30; Acts 23:32; Acts 27:32; Acts 27:40; Acts 28:4; 1 Corinthians 10:13; Luke 22:51


## ἑβδομήκοντα (id: 001463000000000)

ἑβδομήκοντα
===========

ἑβδομήκοντα (G1440)

adj · ἑπτά

60\.30 · Number · One, Two, Three, Etc. \[Cardinals]

seventy cardinal number, where there are seventy instances of a countable entity

[LUK 10:1](https://ref.ly/Mark10:1) · [LUK 10:17](https://ref.ly/Mark10:17) · [ACT 7:14](https://ref.ly/John7:14) · [ACT 23:23](https://ref.ly/John23:23) · [ACT 27:37](https://ref.ly/John27:37)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 10:1; Luke 10:17; Acts 7:14; Acts 23:23; Acts 27:37


## ἑβδομηκοντάκις (id: 001464000000000)

ἑβδομηκοντάκις
==============

ἑβδομηκοντάκις (G1441)

adv · ἑπτά

60\.74 · Number · Once, Twice, Three Times, Etc. \[Cardinals of Time]

seventy times seventy occurrences For another interpretation of ἑβδομηκοντάκις in [MAT 18:22](https://ref.ly/Matt18:22), see [60\.77](#60.77).

[MAT 18:22](https://ref.ly/Matt18:22)1

60\.77 · Number · Double, Four Times As Much, Etc. \[Multiples]

seventy times seventy multiples of a quantity One should not, however, interpret ἑβδομηκοντάκις ἑπτά as referring to a specific number, such as 490, but simply an unusually large number with symbolic significance of being totally adequate or complete. For another interpretation of ἑβδομηκοντάκις in [MAT 18:22](https://ref.ly/Matt18:22), see [60\.74](#60.74).

[MAT 18:22](https://ref.ly/Matt18:22)1

1 MAT 18:22 It is also possible that ἑβδομηκοντάκις ἑπτά in [MAT 18:22](https://ref.ly/Matt18:22) is a shortened form for ἑβδομηκοντάκις ἑπτάκις, which would mean ‘seventy times seven times,’ but this is, of course, essentially equivalent to ἑβδομηκοντάκις ἑπτά.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 18:22


## ἕβδομος (id: 001465000000000)

ἕβδομος
=======

ἕβδομος (G1442)

adj · ἑπτά

60\.54 · Number · First, Second, Third, Etc. \[Ordinals]

seventh seventh in a series involving either time, space, or set

[JHN 4:52](https://ref.ly/Luke4:52) · [HEB 4:4](https://ref.ly/Phlm4:4) · [HEB 4:4](https://ref.ly/Phlm4:4) · [JUD 1:14](https://ref.ly/3John1:14)1 · [REV 8:1](https://ref.ly/Jude8:1) · [REV 10:7](https://ref.ly/Jude10:7) · [REV 11:15](https://ref.ly/Jude11:15) · [REV 16:17](https://ref.ly/Jude16:17) · [REV 21:20](https://ref.ly/Jude21:20)

1 JUD 1:14 In the more general manner of counting an ordinal series, ἕβδομος in [JUD 1:14](https://ref.ly/3John1:14) would really refer to the sixth descendant from Adam. In the Hebrew system of counting, Adam himself would be counted as part of the series, and hence the use in [JUD 1:14](https://ref.ly/3John1:14) of ἕβδομος rather than ἕκτος.

* **Associated Passages:** John 4:52; Hebrews 4:4; Jude 1:14; Revelation 8:1; Revelation 10:7; Revelation 11:15; Revelation 16:17; Revelation 21:20


## Ἔβερ (id: 001466000000000)

Ἔβερ
====

Ἔβερ (G1443)

noun nom

93\.103 · Names of Persons and Places · Persons

Eber a person in the genealogy of Jesus

[LUK 3:35](https://ref.ly/Mark3:35)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 3:35


## Ἑβραϊκός (id: 001467000000000)

Ἑβραϊκός
========

Ἑβραϊκός

adj

93\.104 · Names of Persons and Places · Persons

Hebrew (derivative of Εβραῖος ‘a Hebrew,’ [93\.105](#93.105)) pertaining to the Hebrews ([LUK 23:38](https://ref.ly/Mark23:38), apparatus) The addition of γράμμασιν in some manuscripts literally applies to Hebrew letters, though the reference seems to be to the language, probably Aramaic. In [ACT 21:40](https://ref.ly/John21:40) [ACT 22:2](https://ref.ly/John22:2) [ACT 26:14](https://ref.ly/John26:14) a peculiar form of Ἑβραϊκός occurs, namely, Ἑβραΐδι. Scholars, however, explain it as from Ἑβραΐς, relating it to Εβραῖος ([93\.105](#93.105)).



## Ἑβραῖος (id: 001468000000000)

Ἑβραῖος
=======

Ἑβραῖος (G1445)

noun nom

93\.105 · Names of Persons and Places · Persons

a Hebrew the oldest ethnic name for a Jew or the Jewish people

[ACT 6:1](https://ref.ly/John6:1) · [2CO 11:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:22) · [PHP 3:5](https://ref.ly/Eph3:5) · [PHP 3:5](https://ref.ly/Eph3:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 6:1; 2 Corinthians 11:22; Philippians 3:5


## Ἑβραΐς (id: 001469000000000)

Ἑβραΐς
======

Ἑβραΐς (G1446)

adj

93\.104 · Names of Persons and Places · Persons

Hebrew (derivative of Εβραῖος ‘a Hebrew,’ [93\.105](#93.105)) pertaining to the Hebrews The addition of γράμμασιν in some manuscripts literally applies to Hebrew letters, though the reference seems to be to the language, probably Aramaic. In [ACT 21:40](https://ref.ly/John21:40) [ACT 22:2](https://ref.ly/John22:2) [ACT 26:14](https://ref.ly/John26:14) a peculiar form of Ἑβραϊκός occurs, namely, Ἑβραΐδι. Scholars, however, explain it as from Ἑβραΐς, relating it to Εβραῖος ([93\.105](#93.105)).

[ACT 21:40](https://ref.ly/John21:40) · [ACT 22:2](https://ref.ly/John22:2) · [ACT 26:14](https://ref.ly/John26:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 21:40; Acts 22:2; Acts 26:14

## ἐγγίζω (id: 001471000000000)

ἐγγίζω
======

ἐγγίζω (G1448)

ἐγγύς · ἐγγύτερον · προσεγγίζω

15\.75 · Linear Movement · Come Near, Approach

to draw near, to come near, to approach to move nearer to a reference point In a number of languages it is not possible to merely say ‘to come near,’ for it is necessary to specify the reference point, in other words, ‘near to what?’ One may accordingly translate this expression in [LUK 18:40](https://ref.ly/Mark18:40) as ‘when he came near to Jesus, Jesus asked him.’

[MAT 21:1](https://ref.ly/Matt21:1) · [MAT 26:46](https://ref.ly/Matt26:46) · [MRK 11:1](https://ref.ly/Matt11:1) · [MRK 14:42](https://ref.ly/Matt14:42) · [LUK 7:12](https://ref.ly/Mark7:12) · [LUK 12:33](https://ref.ly/Mark12:33) · [LUK 15:1](https://ref.ly/Mark15:1) · [LUK 15:25](https://ref.ly/Mark15:25) · [LUK 18:35](https://ref.ly/Mark18:35) · [LUK 18:40](https://ref.ly/Mark18:40) · [LUK 19:29](https://ref.ly/Mark19:29) · [LUK 19:37](https://ref.ly/Mark19:37) · [LUK 19:41](https://ref.ly/Mark19:41) · [LUK 21:8](https://ref.ly/Mark21:8) · [LUK 22:47](https://ref.ly/Mark22:47) · [LUK 24:15](https://ref.ly/Mark24:15) · [LUK 24:28](https://ref.ly/Mark24:28) · [ACT 9:3](https://ref.ly/John9:3) · [ACT 10:9](https://ref.ly/John10:9) · [ACT 21:33](https://ref.ly/John21:33) · [ACT 22:6](https://ref.ly/John22:6) · [ACT 23:15](https://ref.ly/John23:15) · [PHP 2:30](https://ref.ly/Eph2:30) · [HEB 7:19](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:19) · [JAS 4:8](https://ref.ly/Heb4:8) · [JAS 4:8](https://ref.ly/Heb4:8)

67\.21 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

to approach, to come near, to approximate the occurrence of a point of time close to a subsequent point of time Though in many languages time may be expressed in terms of space, as in speaking of ‘time coming near,’ in other languages this may not be at all possible. Accordingly, this clause in [MAT 21:34](https://ref.ly/Matt21:34) may need to be translated as ‘it was about time to harvest’ or ‘when it was about time to gather the grapes.’

[MAT 3:2](https://ref.ly/Matt3:2) · [MAT 4:17](https://ref.ly/Matt4:17) · [MAT 10:7](https://ref.ly/Matt10:7) · [MAT 21:34](https://ref.ly/Matt21:34) · [MAT 26:45](https://ref.ly/Matt26:45) · [MRK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Matt1:15) · [LUK 10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:9) · [LUK 10:11](https://ref.ly/Mark10:11) · [LUK 21:20](https://ref.ly/Mark21:20) · [LUK 21:28](https://ref.ly/Mark21:28) · [LUK 22:1](https://ref.ly/Mark22:1) · [ACT 7:17](https://ref.ly/John7:17) · [ROM 13:12](https://ref.ly/Acts13:12) · [HEB 10:25](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:25) · [JAS 5:8](https://ref.ly/Heb5:8) · [1PE 4:7](https://ref.ly/Jas4:7)

## ἔγερσις (id: 001476000000000)

ἔγερσις
=======

ἔγερσις (G1454)

noun · ἐγείρω

23\.93 · Physiological Processes and States · Live, Die

to come back to life, to live again, to be resurrected, resurrection to come back to life after having once died In a number of languages there is a difficulty involved in formulating some expression for ‘resurrection’ or ‘living again,’ since such a phrase may refer to what is technically known as metempsychosis, that is to say, the rebirth of the soul in another existence, a belief which is widely held in a number of areas of south Asia. This problem may be avoided in some languages by speaking of ‘his body will live again’ or ‘his body will come back to life’ or ‘he will be the same person when he lives again.’

[MAT 27:53](https://ref.ly/Matt27:53)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 27:53

spy, secret agent (derivative of ἐγκάθημαι ‘to lie in a crouched position,’ not occurring in the NT) one whose task it is to obtain information surreptitiously

[LUK 20:20](https://ref.ly/Mark20:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 20:20


## ἐγκαίνια (id: 001478000000000)

ἐγκαίνια
========

ἐγκαίνια (G1456)

noun · καινός

51\.9 · Festivals

the festival of dedication of the Temple a Jewish festival commemorating the rededication of the Temple in the time of Judas Maccabaeus in 165 B.C. It may be possible to speak of this Festival of Dedication as ‘the festival to celebrate the time when the Temple was again made pure and ready for worship.’

[JHN 10:22](https://ref.ly/Luke10:22)

* **Associated Passages:** John 10:22

=======

κακόω

to become discouraged, to lose heart, to give up to lose one’s motivation to accomplish some valid goal In place of a negative expression such as ‘not to give up,’ it may be better in some languages to use a positive equivalent, for example, ‘to keep on’ or ‘to continue.’

===========

καυχάομαι

33\.368 · Communication · Boast

to boast to express an unusually high degree of confidence in someone or something being exceptionally noteworthy1 Whether in any particular context the boasting is legitimate or not depends upon what is boasted about. In a number of languages, however, quite different terms are employed, depending upon the differing degrees of justification for such boasting.

[2TH 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:4)

1 There are undoubtedly certain connotative differences in these terms for boasting, but this cannot be determined from existing contexts.

==========

κέντρον

43\.10 · Agriculture

to graft, to bud to insert a shoot or bud into a growing plant Since grafting is not known in some cultures, it may be important to employ a descriptive phrase, for example, ‘to cause a small twig of one tree to grow in another,’ but such a descriptive phrase must almost always be accompanied by a descriptive marginal note, for example, ‘to take a bud or twig from one tree and insert it under the bark of another tree so that it will grow.’

[ROM 11:17](https://ref.ly/Acts11:17) · [ROM 11:19](https://ref.ly/Acts11:19) · [ROM 11:23](https://ref.ly/Acts11:23) · [ROM 11:23](https://ref.ly/Acts11:23) · [ROM 11:24](https://ref.ly/Acts11:24) · [ROM 11:24](https://ref.ly/Acts11:24)

## ἐγκράτεια (id: 001490000000000)

ἐγκράτεια
=========

ἐγκράτεια (G1466)

noun · κραταιόομαι

88\.83 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Self\-Control, Lack of Self\-Control

to control oneself, to exercise self\-control, self\-control to exercise complete control over one’s desires and actions An adequate rendering of the expression ‘to exercise self\-control’ may require an idiomatic equivalent, for example, ‘to hold oneself in,’ ‘to command oneself,’ ‘to be a chief of oneself,’ ‘to make one’s heart be obedient,’ ‘to command one’s own desires,’ ‘to be the master of what one wants,’ or ‘to say No to one’s body.’ (Compare ἀκρασία ‘lack of self\-control,’ [88\.91](#88.91), and ἀκρατής ‘lacking in self\-control,’ [88\.92](#88.92).)

[ACT 24:25](https://ref.ly/John24:25) · [GAL 5:23](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:23) · [2PE 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:6) · [2PE 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 24:25; Galatians 5:23; 2 Peter 1:6

## ἐγώ (id: 001498000000000)

ἐγώ
===

ἐγώ (G2257) — ἡμεῖς

pers.pron

92\.1 · Discourse Referentials · Speaker

**Collocation:** ἐγώ · ἐμέ · ἐμοί · ἐμοῦ · με · μοι · μου

I, I indeed a reference to the speaker (with an added feature of emphasis in the form ἐγώ) The terminology ‘first, second, and third persons’ reflects the normal order in Greek in which the so\-called first person always occurred first in any listing of participants. The second person would be in the second position, and similarly, the third person would be in a third relative position. To preserve that order in English, however, could imply impoliteness, and it is for that reason that it is preferable to translate [JHN 10:30](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30) as ‘the Father and I are one.’

92\.4 · Discourse Referentials · Speaker and Those Associated with the Speaker \[exclusive and inclusive]

**Collocation:** ἡμᾶς · ἡμεῖς · ἡμῖν · ἡμῶν

we, us a reference to the speaker or writer and those associated with him or her, either including or excluding the audience (with an added feature of emphasis in the form ἡμεῖς)

## ἐδαφίζω (id: 001499000000000)

ἐδαφίζω
=======

ἐδαφίζω (G1474)

ἔδαφος

20\.57 · Violence, Harm, Destroy, Kill · Destroy

to raze, to tear down, to destroy (derivative of ἔδαφος ‘ground,’ [1\.44](#1.44)) to destroy or tear down, by causing something to be brought down to the level of the ground In [LUK 19:44](https://ref.ly/Mark19:44) the pronoun σε ‘you’ refers to the city of Jerusalem.

[LUK 19:44](https://ref.ly/Mark19:44)

20\.64 · Violence, Harm, Destroy, Kill · Kill

to kill (a figurative extension of meaning of ἐδαφίζω\[a] ‘to raze, to tear down,’ [20\.57](#20.57)) to destroy or kill a population The verb ἐδαφίζω in relationship to the pronoun σε (referring to Jerusalem) means ‘to raze to the ground’ (see [20\.57](#20.57)), but in reference to τὰ τέκνα σου ἐν σοί the meaning is ‘to kill.’ Accordingly, ἐδαφίζω must be regarded as having two different meanings within the same context: (1\) ‘to raze to the ground’ and (2\) ‘to kill.’

[LUK 19:44](https://ref.ly/Mark19:44)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 19:44

ground the surface of the earth

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 22:7

## ἐθνικός (id: 001507000000000)

ἐθνικός
=======

ἐθνικός (G1482)

adj · ἔθνος

11\.38 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · Socio\-Religious

Gentile, heathen, pagan (derivative of ἔθνος ‘nation,’ [11\.55](#11.55)) pertaining to one who is not a Jew

[MAT 5:47](https://ref.ly/Matt5:47) · [MAT 6:7](https://ref.ly/Matt6:7) · [MAT 18:17](https://ref.ly/Matt18:17) · [3JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/2John1:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 5:47; Matthew 6:7; Matthew 18:17; 3 John 1:7


## ἐθνικῶς (id: 001508000000000)

ἐθνικῶς
=======

ἐθνικῶς (G1483)

adv · ἔθνος

11\.39 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · Socio\-Religious

like a Gentile, as a heathen, similar to a pagan (derivative of ἔθνος ‘nation,’ [11\.55](#11.55)) pertaining to being like or similar to one who is not a Jew In [GAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14) one cannot use some such expression as ‘like those who do not believe in God’ (compare τὰ ἔθνη, [11\.37](#11.37)), since here the contrast is between two types of believers, Jews and non\-Jews. Frequently there is no specific term for ‘Gentile’ in contrast with Jews, and therefore it may be important to speak of ‘Gentiles’ as ‘those who are not Jews.’ [GAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14) may then be rendered as ‘you are a Jew, but you have been living like those who are not Jews.’

[GAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Galatians 2:14


## ἔθνος (id: 001509000000000)

ἔθνος
=====

ἔθνος (G1484) — τὰ ἔθνη

noun · ἐθνάρχης · ἐθνικός · ἐθνικῶς

11\.55 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · Socio\-Political

nation, people the largest unit into which the people of the world are divided on the basis of their constituting a socio\-political community In a number of languages a term meaning basically ‘tribe’ has been extended in meaning to identify ‘nations.’ In other instances different nations are spoken of simply as ‘different peoples.’ In certain cases distinct nations are classified primarily in terms of their diverse languages, for example, ‘those people who speak different languages.’ Such an expression should not, however, be used if it only refers to multilingual persons.

[MAT 21:43](https://ref.ly/Matt21:43) · [MAT 24:7](https://ref.ly/Matt24:7) · [MAT 24:7](https://ref.ly/Matt24:7) · [MAT 24:9](https://ref.ly/Matt24:9) · [MAT 24:14](https://ref.ly/Matt24:14) · [MAT 20:3](https://ref.ly/Jude20:3) · [REV 20:8](https://ref.ly/Jude20:8) · [REV 21:24](https://ref.ly/Jude21:24) · [REV 21:26](https://ref.ly/Jude21:26) · [REV 22:2](https://ref.ly/Jude22:2)

11\.37 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · Socio\-Religious

**Collocation:** τὰ ἔθνη

heathen, pagans, Gentiles (an extended meaning of ἔθνος ‘nation,’ [11\.55](#11.55)) those who do not belong to the Jewish or Christian faith Though in a number of instances τὰ ἔθνη may be rendered as ‘those who do not believe in God,’ it is often more appropriate to render τὰ ἔθνη in terms of belief in other gods or in false gods. For example, [EPH 4:17](https://ref.ly/Gal4:17) may be rendered as ‘do not any longer live like those who believe in idols’ or ‘... in gods who are not really God.’

## ἔθος (id: 001510000000000)

ἔθος
====

ἔθος (G1485)

noun · ἐθίζω

41\.25 · Behavior and Related States · Custom, Tradition

custom, habit a pattern of behavior more or less fixed by tradition and generally sanctioned by the society In a number of languages there is no noun for ‘custom’ or ‘habit,’ but the same meaning is communicated by verbal aspects or adverbial phrases indicating habitual action. For example, in the case of [JHN 19:40](https://ref.ly/Luke19:40) one may translate in some languages ‘as is the way in which Jews always bury people,’ and for [1CO 11:16](https://ref.ly/Rom11:16) one may translate ‘this is not what we constantly do.’

[LUK 1:9](https://ref.ly/Mark1:9) · [LUK 2:42](https://ref.ly/Mark2:42) · [LUK 22:39](https://ref.ly/Mark22:39) · [JHN 19:40](https://ref.ly/Luke19:40) · [ACT 6:14](https://ref.ly/John6:14) · [ACT 15:1](https://ref.ly/John15:1) · [ACT 16:21](https://ref.ly/John16:21) · [ACT 21:21](https://ref.ly/John21:21) · [ACT 25:16](https://ref.ly/John25:16) · [ACT 26:3](https://ref.ly/John26:3) · [ACT 28:17](https://ref.ly/John28:17) · [HEB 10:25](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:25)

2:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:19) · [1JN 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:13) · [2JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/1John1:10)

89\.30 · Relations · Cause and/or Reason

since, because a marker of cause or reason on the basis that an actual case is regarded formally as a supposition In English it is possible to translate εἰ in passages such as [ROM 6:8](https://ref.ly/Acts6:8) and [MAT 6:30](https://ref.ly/Matt6:30) as either ‘if’ or ‘since,’ for the conjunction ‘if’ may also refer to an actual event as a supposition. In a number of languages, however, it is impossible to translate ‘if God so clothes the grass of the field,’ for this would imply serious doubt as to whether God actually does perform such an activity. It may therefore be necessary in such languages to translate quite specifically ‘because’ or ‘since.’

[MAT 6:30](https://ref.ly/Matt6:30) · [LUK 12:26](https://ref.ly/Mark12:26) · [LUK 12:28](https://ref.ly/Mark12:28) · [JHN 7:23](https://ref.ly/Luke7:23) · [JHN 13:14](https://ref.ly/Luke13:14) · [JHN 13:32](https://ref.ly/Luke13:32) · [ROM 6:5](https://ref.ly/Acts6:5) · [ROM 6:8](https://ref.ly/Acts6:8) · [ROM 8:31](https://ref.ly/Acts8:31) · [ROM 15:27](https://ref.ly/Acts15:27) · [1CO 11:6](https://ref.ly/Rom11:6) · [2CO 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:3) · [GAL 3:29](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:29) · [GAL 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:7) · [COL 2:20](https://ref.ly/Phil2:20) · [COL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Phil3:1) · [1TH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Col4:14) · [HEB 7:15](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:15) · [JAS 3:3](https://ref.ly/Heb3:3) · [1PE 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jas1:17) · [1PE 2:3](https://ref.ly/Jas2:3) · [1JN 4:11](https://ref.ly/2Pet4:11) · [1JN 5:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet5:9)

90\.26 · Case · Content

whether, if, that a marker of an indirect question as content

## εἰδέα (id: 001512000000000)

εἰδέα
=====

εἰδέα (G2397a)

noun · εἶδος

58\.14 · Nature, Class, Example · Appearance as an Outward Manifestation of Form

form, appearance appearance as the form of that which is seen In some languages a strictly literal translation of [MAT 28:3](https://ref.ly/Matt28:3) might be misinterpreted to mean that there was something jagged about the outline of Jesus’ appearance. The focus, of course, is upon the brightness, and therefore it may be important to translate ‘his appearance was bright like lightning.’

[MAT 28:3](https://ref.ly/Matt28:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 28:3


## εἶδος (id: 001513000000000)

εἶδος
=====

εἶδος (G1491)

noun · γραώδης · εἰδέα · εἰδωλεῖον · εἰδωλόθυτον · εἰδωλολάτρης · εἰδωλολατρία · εἴδωλον · κατείδωλος · συνείδησις

58\.14 · Nature, Class, Example · Appearance as an Outward Manifestation of Form

form, appearance appearance as the form of that which is seen One may also translate this expression in [LUK 9:29](https://ref.ly/Mark9:29) as ‘while he was praying, his face became different’ or ‘... his face was seen as different’ or ‘... he didn’t look the same.’

[LUK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Mark3:22) · [LUK 9:29](https://ref.ly/Mark9:29) · [JHN 5:37](https://ref.ly/Luke5:37) · [1TH 5:22](https://ref.ly/Col5:22)

24\.1 · Sensory Events and States · See

sight, seeing seeing1 It is also possible to interpret εἶδος in [2CO 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:7) as meaning ‘what is seen’ (see [24\.2](#24.2)).

[LUK 9:29](https://ref.ly/Mark9:29) · [2CO 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:7)

1 As in the case of other so\-called semantic primitives, there seems to be no reason for setting up an artificial kind of definition, since the corresponding glosses serve quite satisfactorily to indicate the semantic content.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 3:22; Luke 9:29; John 5:37; 1 Thessalonians 5:22; 2 Corinthians 5:7


## εἰδωλεῖον (id: 001514000000000)

εἰδωλεῖον
=========

εἰδωλεῖον (G1493)

noun · εἶδος

7\.19 · Constructions · Buildings

temple of an idol a temple or sanctuary which houses an idol The term εἰδωλεῖον may be rendered as ‘the building in which an idol is placed’ or ‘a large house in which an idol is worshiped.’ Since ‘idol’ is often rendered as ‘the statue of a god,’ εἰδωλεῖον may be rendered as ‘the building in which there is a statue of a god’ or ‘a building in which a statue of a god is worshiped.’

[1CO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Rom8:10)1

1 1CO 8:10 It is possible that the statement about banqueting in the temple of an idol should be understood as a process by which a person would symbolically identify himself with the deity in question. This would accordingly provide a problem for an individual who was weak in his conscience.

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 8:10


## εἰδωλόθυτος (id: 001515000000000)

εἰδωλόθυτος
===========

εἰδωλόθυτος (G1494) — εἰδωλόθυτον

adj

5\.15 · Foods and Condiments · Food

sacrificial meat, meat of animals sacrificed to an idol the meat of animals which have been sacrificed to idols εἰδωλόθυτον is a semantically complex word meaning literally ‘that which has been sacrificed to idols.’ There is no specific element meaning ‘meat,’ but the stem meaning ‘sacrifice’ implies ‘meat.’ Part of the sacrifice was normally burned on the altar, part was eaten during a ritual meal in a temple, and part was sold in the public market. According to Jewish tradition this meat was unclean and therefore forbidden. εἰδωλόθυτον is normally translated by a phrase, for example, ‘meat which had been offered to idols’ or ‘meat of an animal which had been sacrificed to idols’ or ‘meat of animals killed in honor of false gods.’

[ACT 15:29](https://ref.ly/John15:29) · [ACT 21:25](https://ref.ly/John21:25) · [1CO 8:1](https://ref.ly/Rom8:1) · [1CO 8:4](https://ref.ly/Rom8:4) · [1CO 8:7](https://ref.ly/Rom8:7) · [1CO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Rom8:10) · [1CO 10:19](https://ref.ly/Rom10:19) · [REV 2:14](https://ref.ly/Jude2:14) · [REV 2:20](https://ref.ly/Jude2:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 15:29; Acts 21:25; 1 Corinthians 8:1; 1 Corinthians 8:4; 1 Corinthians 8:7; 1 Corinthians 8:10; 1 Corinthians 10:19; Revelation 2:14; Revelation 2:20

idolater, worshiper of idols a person who worships idols

============

idolatry the worship of idols

## εἴδωλον (id: 001518000000000)

εἴδωλον
=======

εἴδωλον (G1497)

noun · εἶδος

6\.97 · Artifacts · Images and Idols

idol, (image) an object which resembles a person, animal, god, etc. and which is an object of worship1 The technical distinction between an image and an idol is that an image may merely represent a supernatural being, while an idol not only represents such a being but is believed to possess certain inherent supernatural powers. Images often become idols when they are assumed to possess such powers in and of themselves rather than being mere representations of some supernatural entity. If, for example, various images of a particular supernatural being are supposed to have different healing powers, then what began merely as images or representations of a supernatural power have become idols, in that the different images themselves have acquired special efficacy.\|Though the existence of idols and corresponding terms for them are widespread, idols are by no means universal, and therefore it may be necessary in some languages to employ some type of descriptive equivalent, for example, ‘objects that are made to look like gods’ or ‘carved statues that are considered to be gods.’

[ACT 7:41](https://ref.ly/John7:41) · [ROM 2:22](https://ref.ly/Acts2:22) · [1CO 8:4](https://ref.ly/Rom8:4) · [1CO 8:7](https://ref.ly/Rom8:7) · [1CO 10:19](https://ref.ly/Rom10:19) · [1CO 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:2) · [2CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:16) · [1TH 1:9](https://ref.ly/Col1:9) · [REV 9:20](https://ref.ly/Jude9:20)

12\.23 · Supernatural Beings and Powers · Supernatural Beings

false god (a figurative extension of meaning of εἴδωλον\[a] ‘idol,’ [6\.97](#6.97)) an unreal supernatural being A ‘false god’ may be rendered as ‘that which seems to be a god.’ In [1JN 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Pet5:21) one may speak of ‘those that seem to be gods but really are not.’\|It is also possible, however, to interpret εἴδωλον in [1JN 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Pet5:21) as being simply an idol.

[ACT 15:20](https://ref.ly/John15:20) · [1TH 1:9](https://ref.ly/Col1:9) · [1JN 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Pet5:21)

1 In the NT εἴδωλον\[a] is restricted in meaning to idol in contrast with image, as noted in the discussion of [6\.97](#6.97).

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 7:41; Romans 2:22; 1 Corinthians 8:4; 1 Corinthians 8:7; 1 Corinthians 10:19; 1 Corinthians 12:2; 2 Corinthians 6:16; 1 Thessalonians 1:9; Revelation 9:20; Acts 15:20; 1 John 5:21

in vain, to no avail, with no result pertaining to being without any result In a number of languages an expression such as ‘to be without results’ may be rendered as ‘will accomplish nothing’ or ‘will be as though it had never happened.’

[GAL 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:5)

## εἰκών (id: 001522000000000)

εἰκών
=====

εἰκών (G1504)

noun · ἐπιείκεια · ἐπιεικής

6\.96 · Artifacts · Images and Idols

likeness, image an object (not necessarily three\-dimensional) which has been formed to resemble a person, god, animal, etc. In certain contexts the referents of εἰκών\[a], τύπος\[b], and χάραγμα\[b] may have special religious significance in that they may refer to idols, but the focal component for the translator is that of a likeness or resemblance (see [58\.35](#58.35)). In contexts such as [MAT 22:20](https://ref.ly/Matt22:20) an equivalent translation may be simply ‘a picture,’ but in [ROM 1:23](https://ref.ly/Acts1:23) and [ACT 7:43](https://ref.ly/John7:43) one may use the same expression as is employed to render εἴδωλον\[a] ‘idol’ ([6\.97](#6.97)).

[MAT 22:20](https://ref.ly/Matt22:20) · [LUK 20:24](https://ref.ly/Mark20:24) · [ROM 1:23](https://ref.ly/Acts1:23) · [REV 13:14](https://ref.ly/Jude13:14) · [REV 13:15](https://ref.ly/Jude13:15) · [REV 13:15](https://ref.ly/Jude13:15) · [REV 13:15](https://ref.ly/Jude13:15) · [REV 14:9](https://ref.ly/Jude14:9) · [REV 14:11](https://ref.ly/Jude14:11) · [REV 15:2](https://ref.ly/Jude15:2) · [REV 16:2](https://ref.ly/Jude16:2) · [REV 19:20](https://ref.ly/Jude19:20) · [REV 20:4](https://ref.ly/Jude20:4)

58\.35 · Nature, Class, Example · Same or Equivalent Kind or Class

same form, likeness that which has the same form as something else Since the expressions τοῦ χοϊκοῦ and τοῦ ἐπουρανίου refer to individuals, it is much more satisfactory to translate [1CO 15:49](https://ref.ly/Rom15:49) as ‘just as we have the likeness of the man made of earth, so we will have the likeness of the Man from heaven.’\|In [1CO 15:49](https://ref.ly/Rom15:49) εἰκών designates similarity in class or kind, while in [ROM 1:23](https://ref.ly/Acts1:23) εἰκών indicates a fashioned object (see [6\.96](#6.96)).

[MRK 12:16](https://ref.ly/Matt12:16) · [ROM 8:29](https://ref.ly/Acts8:29) · [1CO 11:7](https://ref.ly/Rom11:7) · [1CO 15:49](https://ref.ly/Rom15:49) · [1CO 15:49](https://ref.ly/Rom15:49) · [2CO 3:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:18) · [2CO 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:4) · [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Phil1:15) · [COL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Phil3:10)

58\.61 · Nature, Class, Example · Pattern, Model, Example, and Corresponding Representation

representation, pattern that which represents something else in terms of basic form and features

[HEB 10:1](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 22:20; Luke 20:24; Romans 1:23; Revelation 13:14; Revelation 13:15; Revelation 14:9; Revelation 14:11; Revelation 15:2; Revelation 16:2; Revelation 19:20; Revelation 20:4; Mark 12:16; Romans 8:29; 1 Corinthians 11:7; 1 Corinthians 15:49; 2 Corinthians 3:18; 2 Corinthians 4:4; Colossians 1:15; Colossians 3:10; Hebrews 10:1


## εἰλικρίνεια (id: 001523000000000)

εἰλικρίνεια
===========

εἰλικρίνεια (G1505)

noun

88\.42 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Honesty, Sincerity

sincerity, purity of motives the quality of sincerity as an expression of pure or unadulterated motives

[1CO 5:8](https://ref.ly/Rom5:8) · [2CO 1:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:12) · [2CO 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:17)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 5:8; 2 Corinthians 1:12; 2 Corinthians 2:17


## εἰλικρινής (id: 001524000000000)

εἰλικρινής
==========

εἰλικρινής (G1506)

adj

88\.41 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Honesty, Sincerity

sincere, without hidden motives pertaining to being sincere in the sense of having pure motivation

[PHP 1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:10)1 · [2PE 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1)

1 PHP 1:10 It is possible that in [PHP 1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:10) εἰλικρινής is to be understood in the sense of moral purity and hence ‘with clean lives’ or ‘without fault.’

* **Associated Passages:** Philippians 1:10; 2 Peter 3:1


## εἰμί (id: 001525000000000)

εἰμί
====

εἰμί (G2258)

ἄπειμι · ἀπουσία · ἔνειμι · ἔξεστι · ἐξουσία · ἐξουσιάζω · ἐξουσιαστικός · ἐπιοῦσα · ἐπιούσιος · κατεξουσιάζω · ὄντως · οὐσία · πάρειμι · παρουσία · περιούσιος · συμπάρειμι · σύνειμι

13\.1 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · State

to be to possess certain characteristics, whether inherent or transitory In some expressions, the verb εἰμί is absent, e.g. μακάριοι οἱ δοῦλοι ἐκεῖνοι ‘truly fortunate are those servants’ [LUK 12:37](https://ref.ly/Mark12:37).1

71\.1 · Mode · Possible, Impossible

to be possible to mark an event as being possible in a highly generic sense In [HEB 9:5](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:5) one cannot determine precisely those factors which make it impossible for the author to be more precise. It may be simply the urgency of other matters or the limitations of space or even the inappropriateness of such considerations for the topic under consideration. In some languages, however, one must be somewhat more specific, for a strictly literal translation might imply that the author of the Epistle to the Hebrews was simply incapable of producing a detailed analysis. This would, of course, be quite contradictory to the implication of the statement in [HEB 9:5](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:5) and the scope of the wider context.

[JHN 1:46](https://ref.ly/Luke1:46) · [ACT 1:7](https://ref.ly/John1:7) · [ACT 4:12](https://ref.ly/John4:12) · [HEB 9:5](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:5)

58\.67 · Nature, Class, Example · Archetype, Corresponding Type \[Antitype]

to be to belong to a particular class In [JHN 1:1](https://ref.ly/Luke1:1) θεός obviously is a unique member of a class, and therefore syntactically this would appear to be a case of complete identification. One can, however, translate ‘the Word was God’ but not ‘God was the Word.’

58\.68 · Nature, Class, Example · Archetype, Corresponding Type \[Antitype]

to correspond to, to stand for, to be a figure of, to represent to correspond to something else in certain significant features

[MAT 13:38](https://ref.ly/Matt13:38) · [MAT 13:38](https://ref.ly/Matt13:38) · [MAT 13:39](https://ref.ly/Matt13:39) · [MAT 13:39](https://ref.ly/Matt13:39) · [LUK 22:19](https://ref.ly/Mark22:19) · [JHN 10:6](https://ref.ly/Luke10:6) · [ACT 10:17](https://ref.ly/John10:17) · [1CO 10:4](https://ref.ly/Rom10:4) · [1CO 10:16](https://ref.ly/Rom10:16) · [1CO 10:16](https://ref.ly/Rom10:16) · [GAL 4:25](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:25)

13\.51 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Change of State

**Collocation:** εἰμὶ εἰς

to change, to become to change from one state to another

[LUK 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mark3:5) · [ACT 8:20](https://ref.ly/John8:20) · [1CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/Rom6:16) · [EPH 1:12](https://ref.ly/Gal1:12) · [EPH 5:31](https://ref.ly/Gal5:31) · [COL 2:22](https://ref.ly/Phil2:22)

34\.18 · Association · Associate

**Collocation:** εἰμὶ εἰς τὸν κόλπον

to be closely involved with, to be close beside (an idiom, literally: to be in the bosom of) to be closely and intimately associated, with the implication of strong affection for In [JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18) one may speak of the Son as ‘being at the Father’s side’ or ‘being in closest communion with the Father.’

[JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18) · [JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18) · [JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18) · [JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18)

68\.20 · Aspect · Continue

**Collocation:** εἰμὶ ἐν

to practice, to continue to do, to cultivate (an idiom, literally: to be in) to continue to perform certain activities with care and concern The construction in [1TI 4:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess4:15), in which ταῦτα μελέτα and ἐν τούτοις ἴσθι appear to be essentially synonymous reinforcements one of the other, would appear to fully justify combining these expressions as essentially equivalent in meaning, but for another interpretation of μελετάω in [1TI 4:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess4:15), see [30\.20](#30.20).

[2CO 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:5) · [1TI 4:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess4:15)

23\.117 · Physiological Processes and States · Live, Die

**Collocation:** εἰμὶ ἐν θανάτοις

to face death, to be likely to die (an idiom, literally: to be in death) to be in imminent danger of dying

[2CO 11:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:23)

87\.49 · Status · High Status or Rank \[including persons of high status]

**Collocation:** εἰμί τις

to be great, to be important (an idiom, literally: to be someone) to be an important person

[ACT 5:36](https://ref.ly/John5:36) · [ACT 5:36](https://ref.ly/John5:36) · [GAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:6) · [GAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:6)

87\.80 · Status · Slave, Free

**Collocation:** εἰμὶ ὑπὸ ζυγόν

to be a slave (an idiom, literally: to be under a yoke) to be in a state of slavery In [1TI 6:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:1) ὑπὸ ζυγὸν is redundant in that it simply emphasizes the status of being a slave.

[1TI 6:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:1) · [1TI 6:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:1) · [1TI 6:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:1)

89\.106 · Relations · Equivalence

**Collocation:** ὅ ἐστιν · τοῦτ᾽ ἔστιν

that is, that means a marker of an explanation or a clarification in the same or a different language In some languages one must make more specific the interpretation of a term in another language. For example, in [MRK 3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:17) it may be necessary to translate ‘Boanerges, which in another language means Men of Thunder’ or ‘Boanerges, for which we would say, Men of Thunder.’

[MAT 1:23](https://ref.ly/Matt1:23) · [MAT 27:33](https://ref.ly/Matt27:33) · [MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46) · [MRK 3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:17) · [MRK 5:41](https://ref.ly/Matt5:41) · [MRK 7:2](https://ref.ly/Matt7:2) · [MRK 7:11](https://ref.ly/Matt7:11) · [MRK 7:34](https://ref.ly/Matt7:34) · [MRK 12:42](https://ref.ly/Matt12:42) · [MRK 15:16](https://ref.ly/Matt15:16) · [MRK 15:22](https://ref.ly/Matt15:22) · [MRK 15:34](https://ref.ly/Matt15:34) · [MRK 15:42](https://ref.ly/Matt15:42) · [LUK 2:11](https://ref.ly/Mark2:11) · [ACT 1:19](https://ref.ly/John1:19) · [ACT 2:12](https://ref.ly/John2:12) · [ACT 4:36](https://ref.ly/John4:36) · [ACT 19:4](https://ref.ly/John19:4) · [ROM 1:12](https://ref.ly/Acts1:12) · [ROM 7:18](https://ref.ly/Acts7:18) · [ROM 9:8](https://ref.ly/Acts9:8) · [ROM 10:6](https://ref.ly/Acts10:6) · [ROM 10:7](https://ref.ly/Acts10:7) · [ROM 10:8](https://ref.ly/Acts10:8) · [EPH 5:5](https://ref.ly/Gal5:5) · [COL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Phil3:14)

23\.91 · Physiological Processes and States · Live, Die

**Collocation:** εἶναι ἐν σκηνώματι

to be alive (an idiom, literally: to be in a dwelling, see [7\.8](#7.8)) to be alive, with special emphasis upon physical existence on earth Most languages have rather generic expressions for ‘life’ or ‘to live,’ but sometimes there are idiomatic expressions which may readily fit the types of contexts illustrated by [2CO 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:6) and [2PE 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:13), for example, ‘to have strength,’ ‘to have one’s eyes,’ and ‘to walk about on the earth.’

9:4](https://ref.ly/Jude9:4) · [REV 17:7](https://ref.ly/Jude17:7) · [REV 19:3](https://ref.ly/Jude19:3)

33\.131 · Communication · Name

to call, to name to use an attribution in speaking of a person It is not always possible to determine from the context whether the act involved is an initial naming of a person or simply calling a person by a particular name. Likewise in the case of attributions or titles, it is not always possible to determine whether it is a matter of giving an attribution or title to a person or simply a matter of speaking of or to a person by means of such an attribution or title. In some instances the giving of a title may be made indirect. For example, in [MAT 1:23](https://ref.ly/Matt1:23) the statement ‘and they will call his name Emmanuel’ suggests the giving of a title, but the process is stated in terms of how people will speak of this person.

[JHN 15:15](https://ref.ly/Luke15:15)

33\.140 · Communication · Interpret, Mean, Explain

to mean, to imply to say something in order to explain more fully the implications or intent of what has been said

[ROM 4:1](https://ref.ly/Acts4:1) · [ROM 6:1](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1)

31\.5 · Hold a View, Believe, Trust · Have an Opinion, Hold a View

**Collocation:** εἶπον ἐν ἑαυτῷ

to think to oneself, to say to oneself (often used to introduce a direct quotation of one’s thoughts) (an idiom, literally: to speak to oneself) to think about something without communicating the content to others

[MAT 9:3](https://ref.ly/Matt9:3)

## εἰρηνεύω (id: 001528000000000)

εἰρηνεύω
========

εἰρηνεύω (G1514)

εἰρήνη · εἰρηνικός · εἰρηνοποιέω · εἰρηνοποιός

88\.102 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Peaceful Behavior

to behave peacefully, to live in peace to live in peace with others In some languages the equivalent of ‘live in peace’ is a negation of fighting, for example, ‘do not fight’ or ‘do not constantly quarrel.’

[MRK 9:50](https://ref.ly/Matt9:50) · [ROM 12:18](https://ref.ly/Acts12:18) · [2CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:11) · [1TH 5:13](https://ref.ly/Col5:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 9:50; Romans 12:18; 2 Corinthians 13:11; 1 Thessalonians 5:13


## εἰρήνη (id: 001529000000000)

εἰρήνη
======

εἰρήνη (G1515)

noun · εἰρηνεύω

22\.42 · Trouble, Hardship, Relief, Favorable Circumstances · Favorable Circumstances or State

peace, tranquility a set of favorable circumstances involving peace and tranquility The meaning of ‘peace’ or ‘tranquility’ may be expressed in some languages in a negative form, for example, ‘to be without trouble’ or ‘to have no worries’ or ‘to sit down in one’s heart.’

25\.248 · Attitudes and Emotions · Worry, Anxiety, Distress, Peace

peace, freedom from worry a state of freedom from anxiety and inner turmoil1 ‘Peace’ in the sense of ‘freedom from worry’ is often expressed by means of an idiom, for example, ‘to sit down in the heart,’ ‘to rest in the liver,’ or ‘to be quiet in one’s inner self.’

[LUK 2:14](https://ref.ly/Mark2:14) · [LUK 2:29](https://ref.ly/Mark2:29) · [LUK 19:38](https://ref.ly/Mark19:38) · [JHN 14:27](https://ref.ly/Luke14:27) · [JHN 14:27](https://ref.ly/Luke14:27) · [JHN 16:33](https://ref.ly/Luke16:33) · [JHN 20:19](https://ref.ly/Luke20:19) · [JHN 20:21](https://ref.ly/Luke20:21) · [JHN 20:26](https://ref.ly/Luke20:26) · [ACT 10:36](https://ref.ly/John10:36) · [ROM 1:7](https://ref.ly/Acts1:7) · [ROM 2:10](https://ref.ly/Acts2:10) · [ROM 3:17](https://ref.ly/Acts3:17) · [ROM 5:1](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1) · [ROM 8:6](https://ref.ly/Acts8:6) · [ROM 14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:17) · [ROM 14:19](https://ref.ly/Acts14:19) · [ROM 15:13](https://ref.ly/Acts15:13) · [ROM 15:33](https://ref.ly/Acts15:33) · [ROM 16:20](https://ref.ly/Acts16:20) · [1CO 1:3](https://ref.ly/Rom1:3) · [2CO 1:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:2) · [GAL 1:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3) · [GAL 5:22](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:22) · [GAL 6:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:16) · [EPH 4:3](https://ref.ly/Gal4:3) · [EPH 6:15](https://ref.ly/Gal6:15) · [PHP 4:7](https://ref.ly/Eph4:7) · [PHP 4:9](https://ref.ly/Eph4:9) · [COL 1:2](https://ref.ly/Phil1:2) · [COL 3:15](https://ref.ly/Phil3:15) · [1TH 1:1](https://ref.ly/Col1:1) · [1TH 5:3](https://ref.ly/Col5:3) · [1TH 5:23](https://ref.ly/Col5:23) · [2TH 1:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:2) · [2TH 3:16](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:16) · [2TH 3:16](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:16) · [1TI 1:2](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:2) · [HEB 13:20](https://ref.ly/Phlm13:20) · [JAS 2:16](https://ref.ly/Heb2:16) · [1PE 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jas5:14) · [2PE 1:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:2) · [2PE 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:14) · [JUD 1:2](https://ref.ly/3John1:2) · [REV 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4)

1 εἰρήνη in Domain 25 pertains to a psychological state and not to a set of circumstances which may lead to peace and tranquility (see [22\.42](#22.42)).

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 10:13; Matthew 10:34; Mark 5:34; Luke 1:79; Luke 7:50; Luke 8:48; Luke 10:5; Luke 10:6; Luke 11:21; Luke 12:51; Luke 14:32; Luke 19:42; Acts 7:26; Acts 9:31; Acts 10:36; Acts 12:20; Acts 15:33; Acts 16:36; Acts 24:2; Romans 14:19; 1 Corinthians 1:3; 1 Corinthians 7:15; 1 Corinthians 14:33; 1 Corinthians 16:11; 2 Corinthians 1:2; 2 Corinthians 13:11; Galatians 6:16; Ephesians 1:2; Ephesians 2:14; Ephesians 2:15; Ephesians 2:17; Ephesians 6:23; Philippians 1:2; 1 Thessalonians 1:1; 1 Thessalonians 5:3; 2 Thessalonians 3:16; 2 Timothy 1:2; 2 Timothy 2:22; Titus 1:4; Philemon 1:3; Hebrews 7:2; Hebrews 11:31; Hebrews 12:14; James 2:16; James 3:18; 1 Peter 1:2; 1 Peter 3:11; 2 John 1:3; 3 John 1:15; Revelation 6:4; Luke 2:14; Luke 2:29; Luke 19:38; John 14:27; John 16:33; John 20:19; John 20:21; John 20:26; Romans 1:7; Romans 2:10; Romans 3:17; Romans 5:1; Romans 8:6; Romans 14:17; Romans 15:13; Romans 15:33; Romans 16:20; Galatians 1:3; Galatians 5:22; Ephesians 4:3; Ephesians 6:15; Philippians 4:7; Philippians 4:9; Colossians 1:2; Colossians 3:15; 1 Thessalonians 5:23; 2 Thessalonians 1:2; 1 Timothy 1:2; Hebrews 13:20; 1 Peter 5:14; 2 Peter 1:2; 2 Peter 3:14; Jude 1:2; Revelation 1:4


## εἰρηνικός (id: 001530000000000)

εἰρηνικός
=========

εἰρηνικός (G1516)

adj · εἰρηνεύω

25\.249 · Attitudes and Emotions · Worry, Anxiety, Distress, Peace

peaceful, free from worry pertaining to freedom from anxiety and inner turmoil

[HEB 12:11](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:11) · [JAS 3:17](https://ref.ly/Heb3:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 12:11; James 3:17


## εἰρηνοποιέω (id: 001531000000000)

εἰρηνοποιέω
===========

εἰρηνοποιέω (G1517)

40\.4 · Reconciliation, Forgiveness · Reconciliation

to make peace, to make things right to cause a state of peace or reconciliation between persons εἰρηνοποιέω is closely related in meaning to the series in [40\.1](#40.1) in that the making of peace or reestablishing peace between persons is a distinctive feature of reconciliation, but the focus in εἰρηνοποιέω seems to be upon the resulting state rather than upon the process.

[COL 1:20](https://ref.ly/Phil1:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Colossians 1:20


## εἰρηνοποιός (id: 001532000000000)

εἰρηνοποιός
===========

εἰρηνοποιός (G1518)

adj

40\.5 · Reconciliation, Forgiveness · Reconciliation

peacemaker, one who works for peace (derivative of εἰρηνοποιέω ‘to make peace,’ [40\.4](#40.4)) a person who restores peace between people Though in [MAT 5:9](https://ref.ly/Matt5:9) the cessation of war is by no means excluded, the focus of meaning of εἰρηνοποιός is reconciliation between persons and not primarily to cause wars to cease.

[MAT 5:9](https://ref.ly/Matt5:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 5:9


## εἰς (id: 001533000000000)

εἰς
===

εἰς (G1519)

prep · ἔσω · ἔσωθεν · ἐσώτερος

84\.16 · Spacial Extensions · Extension To a Goal

to, toward, in the direction of extension toward a special goal1

83\.47 · Spacial Positions · On, Upon, On the Surface Of

on, at a position on the surface of an area

89\.48 · Relations · Result

with the result that, so that as a result, to cause a marker of result, with the probable implication of a preceding process It is also possible to interpret εἰς in this construction of [2TH 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:5) as purpose (see εἰς\[f], [89\.57](#89.57)).\|It would be possible to interpret εἰς ... ἄγω in [ROM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:4) as being purely an expression of cause since ἄγω\[g] (see [36\.1](#36.1)) also involves a component of cause, and therefore one may interpret the expression εἰς μετάνοιάν σε ἄγει as ‘it causes you to repent.’ In either case, however, εἰς marks a resulting event or state.

89\.76 · Relations · Means

by means of, through, by a marker of the means by which one event makes another event possible4

78\.51 · Degree · Up To, As Much As, To the Degree That \[Marked Extent of Degree]

to the point of, to the extent of, to the degree that, up to a degree extending to a particular point as marked by the context In [2CO 10:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:15) περισσείαν itself indicates a superabundant degree of something (see [78\.31](#78.31)), while εἰς marks a degree up to such a point. One may say, therefore, that semantically εἰς serves to reinforce the meaning of περισσείαν.

90\.59 · Case · Experiencer

to, toward, for a marker of an involved experiencer It is possible to translate [1PE 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jas1:11) as ‘trying to find out when the time would be and how it would come’ by interpreting εἰς in [1PE 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jas1:11) as εἰς\[i] ([90\.23](#90.23)) and ποῖον as ποῖος\[b] ([58\.30](#58.30)).

90\.41 · Case · Benefaction

for, on behalf of (with the accusative) a marker of persons benefited by an event, with the implication of something directed to them In both [1CO 16:1](https://ref.ly/Rom16:1) and [2CO 8:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:4) there is clearly an ellipsis of an event of ‘sending’ such help to and on behalf of God’s people.

90\.30 · Case · Guarantor Participant with Oaths

by (with the dative) a marker of objects which serve as symbolic substitutes for supernatural persons or powers presumed to act as guarantors of compliance with oaths

[MAT 5:35](https://ref.ly/Matt5:35)

67\.117 · Time · Duration of Time without Reference to Points or Units of Time: Time, Spend Time, Always, Eternal, Old, Immediately, Young

for, in, at a marker of an extent of time For another interpretation of εἰς τέλος in [1TH 2:16](https://ref.ly/Col2:16) (as an idiom), see [78\.47](#78.47).

[LUK 1:50](https://ref.ly/Mark1:50) · [LUK 12:19](https://ref.ly/Mark12:19) · [LUK 13:9](https://ref.ly/Mark13:9) · [JHN 12:7](https://ref.ly/Luke12:7) · [1TH 2:16](https://ref.ly/Col2:16) · [HEB 9:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:9)

67\.160 · Time · Indefinite Units of Time: Age, Lifetime, Interval, Period

on, at, for a marker of a unit of time

[MAT 28:1](https://ref.ly/Matt28:1) · [LUK 1:20](https://ref.ly/Mark1:20) · [JHN 12:27](https://ref.ly/Luke12:27) · [ACT 13:42](https://ref.ly/John13:42) · [PHP 1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:10) · [PHP 2:16](https://ref.ly/Eph2:16)

67\.119 · Time · Duration of Time with Reference to Some Point of Time: Until, Delay, Still, From

until, to, at last, at length the continuous extent of time up to a point The phrase εἰ μὴ ὅταν is often translated as ‘until’ (for example, μηδενὶ ἅ εἶδον διηγήσωνται, εἰ μὴ ὅταν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐκ νεκρῶν ἀναστῇ ‘not to tell anybody what they have seen until the Son of Man has risen from death’ [MRK 9:9](https://ref.ly/Matt9:9)), but this phrase may be analyzed as meaning ‘if it is not when,’ an expression which is semantically equivalent to ‘until.’\|For an interpretation of εἰς τέλος in [JHN 13:1](https://ref.ly/Luke13:1) as an idiom, see [78\.47](#78.47).

[MAT 10:22](https://ref.ly/Matt10:22) · [MAT 24:13](https://ref.ly/Matt24:13) · [MRK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Matt13:13) · [JHN 6:27](https://ref.ly/Luke6:27) · [JHN 13:1](https://ref.ly/Luke13:1) · [ACT 2:39](https://ref.ly/John2:39) · [ACT 4:3](https://ref.ly/John4:3) · [GAL 3:23](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:23) · [GAL 3:24](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:24) · [EPH 1:14](https://ref.ly/Gal1:14) · [EPH 4:30](https://ref.ly/Gal4:30) · [1TH 4:15](https://ref.ly/Col4:15) · [2TH 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:6) · [2TI 1:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:12) · [HEB 9:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:9) · [1PE 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jas1:5)

27\.4 · Learn · Learn

**Collocation:** εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἔρχομαι

to learn about, to find out, to come to know (an idiom, literally: to come into knowledge) to acquire information about something, with emphasis upon the process involved It is also possible to treat the phrase εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἔρχομαι as meaning ‘to come to know’ in the sense of ‘to come to understand’ (see [32\.17](#32.17)).

[1TI 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:4)

32\.17 · Understand · Come To Understand

**Collocation:** εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἔρχομαι

to come to understand, to come to know (an idiom, literally: to come into knowledge) to come to understand the meaning of something, with special emphasis upon the process One may also interpret εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἔρχομαι as meaning ‘to come to have knowledge of, to come to know’ (see [27\.4](#27.4)).

[2TI 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:7)

78\.27 · Degree · Much, Little \[Positive\-Negative Degree]

**Collocation:** εἰς τὰ ἄμετρα

excessive, beyond measure (an idiom, literally: into that which is not measured) a point on a scale going beyond what is expected In a number of languages one can perhaps best render this statement in [2CO 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:13) as ‘we will not boast too much’ or ‘we will boast only so much.’

[2CO 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:13) · [2CO 10:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:15)

67\.95 · Time · Duration of Time without Reference to Points or Units of Time: Time, Spend Time, Always, Eternal, Old, Immediately, Young

**Collocation:** εἰς αἰῶνα · εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα · εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας · εἰς ἡμέραν αἰῶνος · εἰς παντὰς τοὺς αἰῶνας · εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα τοῦ αἰῶνος · εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα τῶν αἰώνων · εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων · εἰς τὸ παντελές

always, forever, forever and ever, eternally unlimited duration of time, with particular focus upon the future See also [LUK 1:33](https://ref.ly/Mark1:33); [2PE 3:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:18); [JUD 1:25](https://ref.ly/3John1:25); [HEB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:8). The more elaborate expressions employing αἰών are somewhat more emphatic in meaning and are to be found especially in the solemn style of doxologies.\|For another interpretation of εἰς τὸ παντελές in [HEB 7:25](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:25), see [78\.47](#78.47).

[MAT 21:19](https://ref.ly/Matt21:19) · [MRK 3:29](https://ref.ly/Matt3:29) · [MRK 11:14](https://ref.ly/Matt11:14) · [LUK 1:33](https://ref.ly/Mark1:33) · [LUK 1:55](https://ref.ly/Mark1:55) · [JHN 4:14](https://ref.ly/Luke4:14) · [JHN 6:51](https://ref.ly/Luke6:51) · [JHN 6:58](https://ref.ly/Luke6:58) · [JHN 8:35](https://ref.ly/Luke8:35) · [JHN 8:35](https://ref.ly/Luke8:35) · [JHN 8:51](https://ref.ly/Luke8:51) · [JHN 8:52](https://ref.ly/Luke8:52) · [JHN 10:28](https://ref.ly/Luke10:28) · [JHN 11:26](https://ref.ly/Luke11:26) · [JHN 12:34](https://ref.ly/Luke12:34) · [JHN 13:8](https://ref.ly/Luke13:8) · [JHN 14:16](https://ref.ly/Luke14:16) · [ROM 1:25](https://ref.ly/Acts1:25) · [ROM 9:5](https://ref.ly/Acts9:5) · [ROM 11:36](https://ref.ly/Acts11:36) · [1CO 8:13](https://ref.ly/Rom8:13) · [2CO 9:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:9) · [2CO 11:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:31) · [GAL 1:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:5) · [EPH 3:21](https://ref.ly/Gal3:21) · [PHP 4:20](https://ref.ly/Eph4:20) · [HEB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:8) · [HEB 5:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm5:6) · [HEB 6:20](https://ref.ly/Phlm6:20) · [HEB 7:17](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:17) · [HEB 7:21](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:21) · [HEB 7:24](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:24) · [HEB 7:25](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:25) · [HEB 7:28](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:28) · [HEB 10:12](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:12) · [HEB 10:14](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:14) · [HEB 13:21](https://ref.ly/Phlm13:21) · [1PE 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jas4:11) · [2PE 3:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:18) · [JUD 1:13](https://ref.ly/3John1:13) · [JUD 1:25](https://ref.ly/3John1:25)

28\.73 · Know · Not Able To Be Known, Secret

**Collocation:** εἰς τὸ οὖς

in secret, in private, privately (an idiom, literally: into the ear) pertaining to what is known only by those who hear a particular message in private A phrase such as ‘whatever you hear in secret’ may be rendered as ‘whatever you hear when no one else is listening.’

[MAT 10:27](https://ref.ly/Matt10:27)

23\.150 · Physiological Processes and States · Sickness, Disease, Weakness

**Collocation:** εἰς τὸ χεῖρον ἔρχομαι

to get worse, to become more sick (an idiom, literally: to come to the worse) to become increasingly more sick

[MRK 5:26](https://ref.ly/Matt5:26)

88\.166 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Envy, Jealousy

**Collocation:** εἰς χολὴν πικρίας εἰμί

to be very jealous, to be terribly envious, to be bitterly envious (an idiom, literally: to be in the gall of bitterness) to be particularly envious or resentful of someone

[ACT 8:23](https://ref.ly/John8:23)

1 In the meaning of extension toward a goal, εἰς\[a] ([84\.16](#84.16)) and ἐπί\[e] ([84\.17](#84.17)) overlap considerably. It is, of course, possible to argue that ἐπί implies being on or at a point, while εἰς defines entering into an area related to some point, but such a distinction would seem to be arbitrary. 2 In contexts where an accompanying expression designates movement into an area, ἐν\[a] and εἰς\[d] may be rendered as ‘into,’ 3 There are no doubt certain subtle distinctions in the meanings of the terms in [83\.9](#83.9). For example, πρός\[d] may suggest some kind of interaction or response and παρά\[b] may focus upon association, but it is difficult, if not impossible, on the basis of existing contexts to define precisely the differences in meaning. 4 There are probably certain subtle distinctions between the use of διά\[c] in contrast with εἰς\[h] or ἐν\[p] as markers of means, but this cannot be clearly determined from existing contexts.

## εἷς (id: 001534000000000)

εἷς
===

εἷς (G1520)

adj · ἑνότης

60\.10 · Number · One, Two, Three, Etc. \[Cardinals]

one one, in contrast to more than one; cardinal number, where there is one instance of a countable entity

63\.4 · Whole, Unite, Part, Divide · Whole

one that which is united as one in contrast with being divided or consisting of separate parts This phrase in [GAL 3:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:20) is a reference to the fact that God in the Scriptures is defined as a unit and not as being characterized by numerous manifestations or realizations.

## εἰσάγω (id: 001535000000000)

εἰσάγω
======

εἰσάγω (G1521)

ἄγω

15\.173 · Linear Movement · Lead, Bring, Take

to lead into, to take into to bring or lead into

[LUK 14:21](https://ref.ly/Mark14:21) · [LUK 22:54](https://ref.ly/Mark22:54) · [JHN 18:16](https://ref.ly/Luke18:16) · [ACT 9:8](https://ref.ly/John9:8) · [ACT 21:28](https://ref.ly/John21:28) · [ACT 21:29](https://ref.ly/John21:29) · [ACT 22:24](https://ref.ly/John22:24) · [HEB 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:6)

15\.194 · Linear Movement · Carry, Bear

to bring in, to carry in to carry or bring something into an area or structure

[LUK 2:27](https://ref.ly/Mark2:27) · [ACT 7:45](https://ref.ly/John7:45) · [ACT 21:37](https://ref.ly/John21:37) · [HEB 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:6)

## εἰσακούω (id: 001536000000000)

εἰσακούω
========

εἰσακούω (G1522)

ἀκούω

24\.60 · Sensory Events and States · Hear

to listen to, to heed, to pay attention to what is said to listen to someone, with the implication of heeding and responding to what is heard

[MAT 6:7](https://ref.ly/Matt6:7) · [LUK 1:13](https://ref.ly/Mark1:13) · [ACT 10:31](https://ref.ly/John10:31) · [1CO 14:21](https://ref.ly/Rom14:21) · [HEB 5:7](https://ref.ly/Phlm5:7)

36\.15 · Guide, Discipline, Follow · Obey, Disobey

to obey, obedience to obey on the basis of having paid attention to1 Terms expressing the concept of ‘obedience’ may frequently be rendered in some languages as ‘to do what one says’ or ‘to carry out someone’s orders.’

[1CO 14:21](https://ref.ly/Rom14:21)

1 It is possible that there is a subtle distinction in meaning between ὑπακούω\[a] and εἰσακούω\[b] in that the former may suggest primarily submission, while εἰσακούω\[b] may focus more upon attentive listening with resulting obedience.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 6:7; Luke 1:13; Acts 10:31; 1 Corinthians 14:21; Hebrews 5:7


## εἰσδέχομαι (id: 001537000000000)

εἰσδέχομαι
==========

εἰσδέχομαι (G1523)

δέχομαι

34\.53 · Association · Welcome, Receive

to welcome, to receive, to accept, to have as a guest to accept the presence of a person with friendliness1 In view of the fact that εἰσδέχομαι in [2CO 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:17) refers to God’s receiving people, it may be necessary to use quite a different expression than one would normally employ in referring to welcoming a guest into the home. In some instances one may use a rather generic expression such as ‘to accept,’ and this may be expressed idiomatically in some languages as ‘to extend my hand to you’ or ‘to count you as belonging to me.’ It is the figurative usage of εἰσδέχομαι in this type of context which may lead to a somewhat different type of rendering.

[2CO 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:17)

1 There are no doubt certain contextual constraints involved in the choice of ἀναδέχομαι\[a], ἀποδέχομαι\[a], ἐπιδέχομαι\[a], εἰσδέχομαι, ὑποδέχομαι, προσδέχομαι\[b], and παραδέχομαι\[b] in the sense that the spacial relationships involved in the welcoming of a guest can be reflected in the prepositional prefixes. For example, in [LUK 8:40](https://ref.ly/Mark8:40) the use of ἀποδέχομαι may focus upon the particular source, while ἐπιδέχομαι in [3JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/2John1:10) may be relevant in view of the fact that the believers are coming to a particular point. προσδέχομαι in [LUK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Mark15:2) may suggest a kind of reciprocal relationship which is established with outcasts, and παραδέχομαι in [ACT 15:4](https://ref.ly/John15:4) may simply point to spacial proximity. However, it would be wrong to insist upon clear distinctions in meaning based on the very limited number of contexts involved. Some of these same distinctions in spacial relations may exist for the set προσλαμβάνω\[e], παραλαμβάνω\[d], ἀπολαμβάνω\[d].

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 6:17

εἴσειμι
=======

εἴσειμι (G1524)

εἶμι

15\.93 · Linear Movement · Come/Go Into

to move into, to come into, to go into, to enter to move into a space, either two\-dimensional or three\-dimensional

[ACT 3:3](https://ref.ly/John3:3) · [ACT 21:18](https://ref.ly/John21:18) · [ACT 21:26](https://ref.ly/John21:26) · [HEB 9:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:6)

## εἰσέρχομαι (id: 001539000000000)

εἰσέρχομαι
==========

εἰσέρχομαι (G1525)

έρχομαι

15\.93 · Linear Movement · Come/Go Into

to move into, to come into, to go into, to enter to move into a space, either two\-dimensional or three\-dimensional εἰσέρχομαι\[a] is also used in the NT in speaking of the entrance of demons or Satan into animate beings, for example, λέγοντες, Πέμψον ἡμᾶς εἰς τοὺς χοίρους, ἵνα εἰς αὐτοὺς εἰσέλθωμεν saying, Send us into the swine so we may enter them [MRK 5:12](https://ref.ly/Matt5:12). In a number of languages one cannot speak of such supernatural beings ‘entering’ an object. It may be necessary to use such expressions as ‘to grab’ or ‘to control’ or ‘to command.’

22:14](https://ref.ly/Jude22:14)

13\.110 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Happen

to happen, to come into to happen, with the focus upon the initial aspect In some languages it may be impossible to speak of ‘sin coming’ ([ROM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Acts5:12)), and therefore one may have to restructure the relationships, for example, ‘people began to sin.’

[ROM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Acts5:12) · [ROM 11:25](https://ref.ly/Acts11:25) · [HEB 10:5](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:5)

68\.7 · Aspect · Begin, Start

to begin, to commence to begin, with the focus upon the initial stages of an activity In the first clause of [LUK 9:46](https://ref.ly/Mark9:46), it is clear that the event is to be found in the noun διαλογισμός, while the verb εἰσῆλθεν merely contributes an aspect of the event.

[LUK 9:46](https://ref.ly/Mark9:46)

90\.70 · Case · Experiencer

to begin to experience, to come into an experience, to attain to begin to experience an event or state

41\.24 · Behavior and Related States · Behavior, Conduct

**Collocation:** εἰσέρχομαι καὶ ἐξέρχομαι

to live with, to live among (idiom, literally: to go in and to go out) to live or to conduct oneself in relationship to some community or group The semantic focus of this idiom in [ACT 1:21](https://ref.ly/John1:21) appears to be the manner in which a person conducts himself in relationship to others, but it is possible to imply a certain amount of movement. For example, in [ACT 1:21](https://ref.ly/John1:21), it is possible to translate ‘during the whole time the Lord Jesus travelled about with us.’

[ACT 1:21](https://ref.ly/John1:21)

## εἰσπορεύομαι (id: 001543000000000)

εἰσπορεύομαι
============

εἰσπορεύομαι (G1531)

πορεύομαι

15\.93 · Linear Movement · Come/Go Into

to move into, to come into, to go into, to enter to move into a space, either two\-dimensional or three\-dimensional

[MAT 15:17](https://ref.ly/Matt15:17) · [MRK 1:21](https://ref.ly/Matt1:21) · [MRK 4:19](https://ref.ly/Matt4:19) · [MRK 5:40](https://ref.ly/Matt5:40) · [MRK 6:56](https://ref.ly/Matt6:56) · [MRK 7:15](https://ref.ly/Matt7:15) · [MRK 7:18](https://ref.ly/Matt7:18) · [MRK 7:19](https://ref.ly/Matt7:19) · [MRK 11:2](https://ref.ly/Matt11:2) · [LUK 8:16](https://ref.ly/Mark8:16) · [LUK 11:33](https://ref.ly/Mark11:33) · [LUK 18:24](https://ref.ly/Mark18:24) · [LUK 19:30](https://ref.ly/Mark19:30) · [LUK 22:10](https://ref.ly/Mark22:10) · [ACT 3:2](https://ref.ly/John3:2) · [ACT 8:3](https://ref.ly/John8:3) · [ACT 28:30](https://ref.ly/John28:30)

41\.24 · Behavior and Related States · Behavior, Conduct

**Collocation:** εἰσπορεύομαι καὶ ἐκπορεύομαι

to live with, to live among (idiom, literally: to go in and to go out) to live or to conduct oneself in relationship to some community or group The semantic focus of this idiom in [ACT 9:28](https://ref.ly/John9:28) appears to be the manner in which a person conducts himself in relationship to others, but it is possible to imply a certain amount of movement. For example, in [ACT 9:28](https://ref.ly/John9:28) one could translate ‘Paul stayed with them and went about in Jerusalem.’

[ACT 9:28](https://ref.ly/John9:28)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 15:17; Mark 1:21; Mark 4:19; Mark 5:40; Mark 6:56; Mark 7:15; Mark 7:18; Mark 7:19; Mark 11:2; Luke 8:16; Luke 11:33; Luke 18:24; Luke 19:30; Luke 22:10; Acts 3:2; Acts 8:3; Acts 28:30; Acts 9:28


## εἰστρέχω (id: 001544000000000)

εἰστρέχω
========

εἰστρέχω (G1532)

τρέχω

15\.232 · Linear Movement · Run

to run in, to run into

[ACT 12:14](https://ref.ly/John12:14)

## εἰσφέρω (id: 001545000000000)

εἰσφέρω
=======

εἰσφέρω (G1533)

φέρω

15\.194 · Linear Movement · Carry, Bear

to bring in, to carry in to carry or bring something into an area or structure

[LUK 5:18](https://ref.ly/Mark5:18) · [LUK 5:19](https://ref.ly/Mark5:19) · [LUK 12:11](https://ref.ly/Mark12:11) · [1TI 6:7](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:7) · [HEB 13:11](https://ref.ly/Phlm13:11)

90\.93 · Case · To Cause To Experience

to cause to, to bring in to, to lead to to cause someone to enter into a particular event or state

[MAT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Matt6:13) · [LUK 11:4](https://ref.ly/Mark11:4)

24\.55 · Sensory Events and States · Hear

**Collocation:** εἰσφέρω εἰς τὰς ἀκοάς

to cause to hear, to make hear (an idiom, literally: to bring into the ears) to cause someone to hear In a number of languages, however, it is awkward to speak of ‘to cause to hear’; rather, it seems far better to use an expression of ‘speaking,’ for example, ‘what we hear you say is strange.’ For another interpretation of this idiom in [ACT 17:20](https://ref.ly/John17:20), see [33\.92](#33.92).

[ACT 17:20](https://ref.ly/John17:20)

33\.92 · Communication · Speak, Talk

**Collocation:** εἰσφέρω εἰς τὰς ἀκοάς

to bring to the attention of, to speak about to (an idiom, literally: to bring into the ears) to bring something to the attention of people by means of speech, but probably not in a formal or open manner For another interpretation of this idiom in [ACT 17:20](https://ref.ly/John17:20), see [24\.55](#24.55).

[ACT 17:20](https://ref.ly/John17:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 5:18; Luke 5:19; Luke 12:11; 1 Timothy 6:7; Hebrews 13:11; Matthew 6:13; Luke 11:4; Acts 17:20


## εἶτα (id: 001546000000000)

εἶτα
====

εἶτα (G1534)

adv · μετέπειτα

67\.44 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

then, afterwards, later a point of time following another point1

[MRK 4:17](https://ref.ly/Matt4:17) · [MRK 4:28](https://ref.ly/Matt4:28) · [MRK 4:28](https://ref.ly/Matt4:28) · [MRK 8:25](https://ref.ly/Matt8:25) · [LUK 8:12](https://ref.ly/Mark8:12) · [JHN 13:5](https://ref.ly/Luke13:5) · [JHN 19:27](https://ref.ly/Luke19:27) · [JHN 20:27](https://ref.ly/Luke20:27) · [1CO 15:5](https://ref.ly/Rom15:5) · [1CO 15:7](https://ref.ly/Rom15:7) · [1CO 15:24](https://ref.ly/Rom15:24) · [1TI 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:13) · [1TI 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:10) · [HEB 12:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:9) · [JAS 1:15](https://ref.ly/Heb1:15)

89\.100 · Relations · Addition

besides this, furthermore a marker of an addition which has just been specified in the context

[HEB 12:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:9)

1 It is possible that μετέπειτα suggests a somewhat longer interval of time or it may be merely a stylistic variant of ἔπειτα.

90\.12 · Case · Instrument

by, as a result of (with the genitive) a marker of instrument, with the added implication of result

[JHN 1:13](https://ref.ly/Luke1:13) · [1TH 2:3](https://ref.ly/Col2:3) · [REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Jude3:18) · [REV 8:11](https://ref.ly/Jude8:11)

89\.85 · Relations · Manner

with, from a marker of manner, often with the implication of source

[2CO 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:4) · [2CO 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:17) · [2CO 9:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:7) · [2CO 9:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:7) · [2CO 13:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:4) · [COL 3:23](https://ref.ly/Phil3:23) · [1PE 1:22](https://ref.ly/Jas1:22)

90\.16 · Case · Source of Event or Activity

from, by (with the genitive) a marker of the source of an activity or state, with the implication of something proceeding from or out of the source

89\.121 · Relations · Dissociation

from, free from, apart from, independent of a marker of dissociation in the sense of being independent from someone or something In [1CO 9:19](https://ref.ly/Rom9:19) it may be valuable to relate the first part of the verse to what immediately follows, for example, ‘for I am not a slave of anyone, but I make myself a slave to everyone’ or ‘though I am not anyone’s slave, I serve everyone.’

68\.54 · Aspect · Cease, Stop

from a marker of the aspect of cessation

[JHN 12:27](https://ref.ly/Luke12:27) · [ACT 7:10](https://ref.ly/John7:10) · [ROM 11:15](https://ref.ly/Acts11:15) · [ROM 13:11](https://ref.ly/Acts13:11) · [REV 14:13](https://ref.ly/Jude14:13)

67\.33 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

when, at the time of a marker of a point of time which is simultaneous to or overlaps with another point of time3

[MAT 26:42](https://ref.ly/Matt26:42) · [MAT 26:44](https://ref.ly/Matt26:44) · [MRK 14:72](https://ref.ly/Matt14:72) · [LUK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:15) · [JHN 9:24](https://ref.ly/Luke9:24) · [ACT 10:15](https://ref.ly/John10:15) · [ACT 11:9](https://ref.ly/John11:9) · [HEB 9:28](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:28)

67\.131 · Time · Duration of Time with Reference to Some Point of Time: Until, Delay, Still, From

since, from a marker of the extent of time from a point in the past

[MAT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Matt19:12) · [MRK 9:21](https://ref.ly/Matt9:21) · [MRK 10:20](https://ref.ly/Matt10:20) · [LUK 18:21](https://ref.ly/Mark18:21) · [LUK 23:8](https://ref.ly/Mark23:8) · [JHN 6:64](https://ref.ly/Luke6:64) · [JHN 6:66](https://ref.ly/Luke6:66) · [JHN 9:1](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1) · [JHN 16:4](https://ref.ly/Luke16:4) · [JHN 19:12](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12) · [ACT 3:2](https://ref.ly/John3:2) · [ACT 9:33](https://ref.ly/John9:33) · [ACT 14:8](https://ref.ly/John14:8) · [ACT 15:21](https://ref.ly/John15:21) · [ACT 24:10](https://ref.ly/John24:10) · [ACT 26:4](https://ref.ly/John26:4) · [ROM 5:16](https://ref.ly/Acts5:16) · [GAL 1:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:15)

57\.163 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Pay, Price, Cost

with, by means of a marker of price

[MAT 20:2](https://ref.ly/Matt20:2) · [MAT 27:7](https://ref.ly/Matt27:7) · [ACT 1:18](https://ref.ly/John1:18)

89\.142 · Relations · Substance

of, consisting of, out of, made of a marker of the substance of which something consists or out of which it is made

[MAT 27:29](https://ref.ly/Matt27:29) · [JHN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Luke2:15) · [JHN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Luke9:6) · [JHN 12:3](https://ref.ly/Luke12:3) · [JHN 18:3](https://ref.ly/Luke18:3) · [JHN 18:3](https://ref.ly/Luke18:3) · [JHN 19:2](https://ref.ly/Luke19:2) · [ROM 9:21](https://ref.ly/Acts9:21) · [1JN 2:21](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:21) · [1JN 3:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:19)

87\.34 · Status · High Status or Rank \[including persons of high status]

**Collocation:** ἐκ δεξιῶν καθίζω

to sit on the right side of, to be granted high position (an idiom, literally: to sit on the right side of) to be in a position of high status Compare the use of ἀριστερός in [87\.35](#87.35) and see the discussion of [MRK 10:37](https://ref.ly/Matt10:37) there. For another interpretation of δεξιός in [MRK 10:37](https://ref.ly/Matt10:37), see [82\.8](#82.8).

[MAT 20:21](https://ref.ly/Matt20:21) · [MAT 20:23](https://ref.ly/Matt20:23) · [MAT 22:44](https://ref.ly/Matt22:44) · [MAT 26:64](https://ref.ly/Matt26:64) · [MRK 10:37](https://ref.ly/Matt10:37) · [MRK 10:40](https://ref.ly/Matt10:40) · [MRK 12:36](https://ref.ly/Matt12:36) · [MRK 14:62](https://ref.ly/Matt14:62) · [MRK 16:19](https://ref.ly/Matt16:19) · [LUK 20:42](https://ref.ly/Mark20:42) · [LUK 22:69](https://ref.ly/Mark22:69) · [ACT 2:34](https://ref.ly/John2:34) · [HEB 1:13](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:13)

63\.15 · Whole, Unite, Part, Divide · Part

**Collocation:** ἐκ μέρους

being part of, as a part of, in part, partially (an idiom, literally: from a part) the state of being part of something

[1CO 12:27](https://ref.ly/Rom12:27) · [1CO 13:9](https://ref.ly/Rom13:9) · [1CO 13:9](https://ref.ly/Rom13:9) · [1CO 13:10](https://ref.ly/Rom13:10) · [1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12)

78\.12 · Degree · Much, Little \[Positive\-Negative Degree]

**Collocation:** ἐκ μέτρου

sparingly, in a limited way (an idiom, literally: from measure) a scalar point marking considerable limitation in the extent of some activity In a number of languages this expression in [JHN 3:34](https://ref.ly/Luke3:34) may be appropriately rendered as ‘he doesn’t hold back at all when he gives the Spirit’ or ‘he gives the Spirit in an abundant way.’

[JHN 3:34](https://ref.ly/Luke3:34)

78\.20 · Degree · Much, Little \[Positive\-Negative Degree]

**Collocation:** ἐκ περισσοῦ

exceedingly, extremely, decidedly, excessively (an idiom, literally: from excess) an extremely high point on a scale of extent and implying excess In [MRK 6:51](https://ref.ly/Matt6:51) both λίαν ([78\.1](#78.1)) and ἐκ περισσοῦ are expressions of degree and they reinforce one another.

[MRK 6:51](https://ref.ly/Matt6:51)

67\.133 · Time · Duration of Time with Reference to Some Point of Time: Until, Delay, Still, From

**Collocation:** ἐκ τοῦ αἰῶνος

since all time, from all ages past, from the beginning of time an exceedingly long period of time from an assumed beginning up to the present

[JHN 9:32](https://ref.ly/Luke9:32)

78\.48 · Degree · Completely, Enough \[Completive Degree]

**Collocation:** ἐκ … εἰς …

completely, entirely, exclusively a matter of (an idiomatic frame, literally: from ... to ...) a degree of totality emphasizing exclusiveness or the elimination of other possibilities

[LUK 17:24](https://ref.ly/Mark17:24) · [ROM 1:17](https://ref.ly/Acts1:17) · [2CO 2:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:16)

87\.35 · Status · High Status or Rank \[including persons of high status]

**Collocation:** ἐξ ἀριστερῶν καθίζω

to be given somewhat less high status, to sit on the left side of (an idiom, literally: to sit on the left side of) to be in a position of high status, but less than in the case of the right side (compare [87\.34](#87.34)) Compare the use of δεξιός in [87\.34](#87.34).\|In a number of languages the equivalent of this entire expression in [MRK 10:37](https://ref.ly/Matt10:37) would be ‘in order that one of us may be number one and the other may be number two in your kingdom’ or ‘in order that one of us may be your first officer and the other your second officer in your kingdom.’ For another interpretation of ἀριστερός in [MRK 10:37](https://ref.ly/Matt10:37), see [82\.7](#82.7).

[MAT 20:23](https://ref.ly/Matt20:23) · [MRK 10:37](https://ref.ly/Matt10:37) · [MRK 10:40](https://ref.ly/Matt10:40)

83\.42 · Spacial Positions · Opposite, Over Against, Across From, Offshore From

**Collocation:** ἐξ ἐναντίας

opposite, in front of, before, across from, in the presence of a position over against an object or other position

[MRK 15:39](https://ref.ly/Matt15:39)

39\.6 · Hostility, Strife · Opposition, Hostility

**Collocation:** ἐξ ἐναντίας

hostile, one who is hostile pertaining to being hostile toward

[TIT 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:8)

1 MAT 16:1 In [MRK 8:11](https://ref.ly/Matt8:11) (see [84\.3](#84.3)) and [MAT 16:1](https://ref.ly/Matt16:1) (see [84\.4](#84.4)) there is a clear overlap of reference between ἀπό\[c] and ἐκ\[a]. 2 No doubt the meanings in this series differ somewhat, but it is not possible on the basis of the contexts now available to determine precisely what designative or associative features of meaning may be involved. 3 The difference in meaning between the set κατά\[g], ἐπί\[s], ἐν\[s], ἐκ\[k], and καθώς\[d] and the set πότε, ποτέ\[b], ὁπότε, ὅτε\[a], and ὅταν\[b] ([67\.30](#67.30)) is largely a matter of class meanings, but there may be certain subtle rhetorical differences as well.

## ἕκαστος (id: 001550000000000)

ἕκαστος
=======

ἕκαστος (G1538)

adj

59\.27 · Quantity · All, Any, Each, Every \[Totality]

each each one of a totality in a distributive sense

## ἐκβάλλω (id: 001557000000000)

ἐκβάλλω
=======

ἐκβάλλω (G1544)

βάλλω

15\.220 · Linear Movement · Throw, Hurl

to throw out, to jettison (from a boat) to throw out of an area or object It may be that ἐκβάλλω in [MAT 21:39](https://ref.ly/Matt21:39) is a type of hyperbole for causing to go out, but at the same time it is possible that the term ἐκβάλλω is to be understood in a literal sense.

[MAT 21:39](https://ref.ly/Matt21:39) · [MRK 9:47](https://ref.ly/Matt9:47) · [MRK 12:8](https://ref.ly/Matt12:8) · [LUK 4:29](https://ref.ly/Mark4:29) · [LUK 13:28](https://ref.ly/Mark13:28) · [LUK 20:12](https://ref.ly/Mark20:12) · [LUK 20:15](https://ref.ly/Mark20:15) · [ACT 27:38](https://ref.ly/John27:38)

15\.44 · Linear Movement · Leave, Depart, Flee, Escape, Send

to send away, to drive out, to expel to cause to go out or leave, often, but not always, involving force In referring to exorcism of evil spirits, it may be necessary in some languages to use figurative expressions, for example, ‘to pull out,’ ‘to cause to to dismount,’ or ‘to cause one’s heart to return.’
