## ἐναγκαλίζομαι (id: 001736000000000)

ἐναγκαλίζομαι
=============

ἐναγκαλίζομαι (G1723)

ἀγκαλίζομαι · ἀγκάλη

34\.63 · Association · Kiss, Embrace

to embrace, to hug, to put one’s arms around to put one’s arms around someone as an expression of affection and concern It may be important in some instances to indicate the symbolic value of an embrace by some type of classifying phrase, for example, ‘to show his love for by putting his arms around’ or ‘to embrace to show affection.’

[MRK 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36) · [MRK 10:16](https://ref.ly/Matt10:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 9:36; Mark 10:16


## ἐνάλιος (id: 001737000000000)

ἐνάλιος
=======

ἐνάλιος (G1724) — ἐνάλιον

adj · ἁλίζω

4\.58 · Animals · Fishes and Other Sea Creatures

**Collocation:** ἐνάλιον

sea creature, fish any creature living in the sea In [JAS 3:7](https://ref.ly/Heb3:7) one may, of course, simply employ ‘fish,’ since these are the most prominent examples of creatures in the sea.

[JAS 3:7](https://ref.ly/Heb3:7)

* **Associated Passages:** James 3:7


## ἔναντι (id: 001738000000000)

ἔναντι
======

ἔναντι (G1725)

prep

83\.33 · Spacial Positions · In Front Of, Face To Face, In Back Of, Behind

in front of, before a position in front of an object, whether animate or inanimate, which is regarded as having a spacial orientation of front and back1

[LUK 1:8](https://ref.ly/Mark1:8)

90\.20 · Case · Viewpoint Participant

in the sight of, in the opinion of, in the judgment of (with the genitive) marking a participant whose viewpoint is relevant to an event2

[ACT 8:21](https://ref.ly/John8:21)

1 There are no doubt certain slight differences of meaning in this series ([83\.33](#83.33)), but such distinctions cannot be readily determined from existing contexts. 2 There are undoubtedly certain subtle distinctions of meaning associated with the various markers in [90\.20](#90.20), but it is impossible to determine those differences on the basis of existing contexts.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 1:8; Acts 8:21


## ἐναντίον (id: 001739000000000)

ἐναντίον
========

ἐναντίον (G1726) — τοὐναντίον

prep

83\.33 · Spacial Positions · In Front Of, Face To Face, In Back Of, Behind

in front of, before a position in front of an object, whether animate or inanimate, which is regarded as having a spacial orientation of front and back1

[LUK 20:26](https://ref.ly/Mark20:26) · [ACT 8:32](https://ref.ly/John8:32)

90\.20 · Case · Viewpoint Participant

in the sight of, in the opinion of, in the judgment of marking a participant whose viewpoint is relevant to an event2

[LUK 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mark1:6) · [LUK 24:19](https://ref.ly/Mark24:19) · [ACT 7:10](https://ref.ly/John7:10)

89\.134 · Relations · Contrast

on the contrary, rather, instead a marker of an alternative serving as an emphatic contrast

[2CO 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:7) · [GAL 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:7) · [1PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/Jas3:9)

1 There are no doubt certain slight differences of meaning in this series ([83\.33](#83.33)), but such distinctions cannot be readily determined from existing contexts. 2 There are undoubtedly certain subtle distinctions of meaning associated with the various markers in [90\.20](#90.20), but it is impossible to determine those differences on the basis of existing contexts.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 20:26; Acts 8:32; Luke 1:6; Luke 24:19; Acts 7:10; 2 Corinthians 2:7; Galatians 2:7; 1 Peter 3:9


## ἐναντιόομαι (id: 001740000000000)

ἐναντιόομαι
===========

ἐναντιόομαι

39\.1 · Hostility, Strife · Opposition, Hostility

to oppose, to be hostile toward, to show hostility to oppose someone, involving not only a psychological attitude but also a corresponding behavior1 This word is only found as a variant reading: ἐναντιούμενοι καὶ βλασφημοῦντες ‘they opposed him and insulted him’ [ACT 13:45](https://ref.ly/John13:45) (apparatus).

[ACT 13:45](https://ref.ly/John13:45)

1 There are probably certain significant distinctions in meaning in the various verbs listed in [39\.1](#39.1), but on the basis of NT usage it is not possible to determine precisely what these distinctions may be.

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 13:45


## ἐναντίος (id: 001741000000000)

ἐναντίος
========

ἐναντίος (G1727)

adj

82\.11 · Spacial Orientations · Left, Right, Straight Ahead, Opposite

against pertaining to being oriented in the direction opposite to a movement

[MAT 14:24](https://ref.ly/Matt14:24) · [MRK 6:48](https://ref.ly/Matt6:48) · [ACT 27:4](https://ref.ly/John27:4)

39\.6 · Hostility, Strife · Opposition, Hostility

hostile, one who is hostile pertaining to being hostile toward1

[ACT 26:9](https://ref.ly/John26:9) · [ACT 28:17](https://ref.ly/John28:17) · [1TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/Col2:15) · [TIT 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:8)

83\.42 · Spacial Positions · Opposite, Over Against, Across From, Offshore From

**Collocation:** ἐξ ἐναντίας

opposite, in front of, before, across from, in the presence of a position over against an object or other position

[MRK 15:39](https://ref.ly/Matt15:39)

1 It is possible that ὑπεναντίος is somewhat stronger in meaning than ἐναντίος\[b], but this cannot be shown from NT contexts.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 14:24; Mark 6:48; Acts 27:4; Acts 26:9; Acts 28:17; 1 Thessalonians 2:15; Titus 2:8; Mark 15:39

## ἐνδεής (id: 001744000000000)

ἐνδεής
======

ἐνδεής (G1729)

adj · δέομαι

57\.51 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Be Poor, Be Needy, Poverty

poor, needy pertaining to lacking what is needed or necessary for existence ἐνδεής is similar in meaning to πτωχός\[a] ([57\.53](#57.53)), but the focus seems to be more upon a severe lack of needed resources rather than upon a state of poverty and destitution.

[ACT 4:34](https://ref.ly/John4:34)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 4:34


## ἔνδειγμα (id: 001745000000000)

ἔνδειγμα
========

ἔνδειγμα (G1730)

noun · δείκνυμι

28\.52 · Know · Well Known, Clearly Shown, Revealed

proof, evidence, verification, indication (derivative of ἐνδείκνυμαι\[a] ‘to show, to demonstrate,’ [28\.51](#28.51)) the means by which one knows that something is a fact1

[2TH 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:5)

1 As in the case of βέβαιος\[c] ([28\.43](#28.43)), βέβαιοω\[b] ([28\.43](#28.43)), and βεβαίωσις ([28\.44](#28.44)), there are two distinct sets of semantic features in the series ἔνδειξις, ἔνδειγμα, and ἀπόδειξις. The element of showing or demonstrating implies clearly making something known, but that which is made known is done in a clear, convincing, and confirming manner and therefore shown to be certain or true. This double set of semantic features is contained in such terms as proof, evidence and confirmation.

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Thessalonians 1:5


## ἐνδείκνυμι (id: 001746000000000)

ἐνδείκνυμι
==========

ἐνδείκνυμι (G1731) — ἐνδείκνυμαι

δείκνυμι

28\.51 · Know · Well Known, Clearly Shown, Revealed

to cause to be known, to show, to demonstrate to cause to be made known, with possible emphasis upon the means

[ROM 2:15](https://ref.ly/Acts2:15) · [ROM 9:17](https://ref.ly/Acts9:17) · [ROM 9:22](https://ref.ly/Acts9:22) · [2CO 8:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:24) · [EPH 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7) · [1TI 1:16](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:16) · [TIT 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:10) · [TIT 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:2) · [HEB 6:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm6:10) · [HEB 6:11](https://ref.ly/Phlm6:11)

13\.131 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Happen

to do to to do something to someone, normally with the implication of something bad or unfortunate

[2TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 2:15; Romans 9:17; Romans 9:22; 2 Corinthians 8:24; Ephesians 2:7; 1 Timothy 1:16; Titus 2:10; Titus 3:2; Hebrews 6:10; Hebrews 6:11; 2 Timothy 4:14


## ἔνδειξις (id: 001747000000000)

ἔνδειξις
========

ἔνδειξις (G1732)

noun · δείκνυμι

28\.52 · Know · Well Known, Clearly Shown, Revealed

proof, evidence, verification, indication (derivative of ἐνδείκνυμαι\[a] ‘to show, to demonstrate,’ [28\.51](#28.51)) the means by which one knows that something is a fact1

[ROM 3:25](https://ref.ly/Acts3:25) · [ROM 3:26](https://ref.ly/Acts3:26) · [2CO 8:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:24) · [PHP 1:28](https://ref.ly/Eph1:28)

1 As in the case of βέβαιος\[c] ([28\.43](#28.43)), βέβαιοω\[b] ([28\.43](#28.43)), and βεβαίωσις ([28\.44](#28.44)), there are two distinct sets of semantic features in the series ἔνδειξις, ἔνδειγμα, and ἀπόδειξις. The element of showing or demonstrating implies clearly making something known, but that which is made known is done in a clear, convincing, and confirming manner and therefore shown to be certain or true. This double set of semantic features is contained in such terms as proof, evidence and confirmation.

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 3:25; Romans 3:26; 2 Corinthians 8:24; Philippians 1:28

## ἐνδέχομαι (id: 001750000000000)

ἐνδέχομαι
=========

ἐνδέχομαι (G1735) — ἐνδέχεται

δέχομαι

71\.4 · Mode · Possible, Impossible

to be possible, to be thinkable to be possible, in the sense of being fully in accord with human experience

[LUK 13:33](https://ref.ly/Mark13:33)1

1 LUK 13:33 Even though one may translate [LUK 13:33](https://ref.ly/Mark13:33) as ‘it is unthinkable,’ it does not necessarily follow that one should classify this in Domain 30 Think. The meaning of ‘unthinkable’ is simply a derivative from the concept of impossibility.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 13:33


## ἐνδημέω (id: 001751000000000)

ἐνδημέω
=======

ἐνδημέω (G1736)

δῆμος

85\.20 · Existence in Space · Be in a Place

to be at home, to fit in a place to be in a place where one rightfully or normally belongs

[2CO 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:8) · [2CO 5:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:9)

23\.91 · Physiological Processes and States · Live, Die

**Collocation:** ἐνδημέω ἐν τῷ σώματι

to be alive (an idiom, literally: to be at home in the body) to be alive, with special emphasis upon physical existence on earth Most languages have rather generic expressions for ‘life’ or ‘to live,’ but sometimes there are idiomatic expressions which may readily fit the types of contexts illustrated by [2CO 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:6) and [2PE 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:13), for example, ‘to have strength,’ ‘to have one’s eyes,’ and ‘to walk about on the earth.’

[2CO 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:6)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 5:8; 2 Corinthians 5:9; 2 Corinthians 5:6


## ἐνδιδύσκω (id: 001752000000000)

## ἐνδώμησις (id: 001761000000000)

ἐνδώμησις
=========

ἐνδώμησις (G1739a)

noun · δῶμα

7\.77 · Constructions · Building Materials

building material material used in the construction of buildings It is also possible to understand ἐνδώμησις in the sense of ‘foundation’ (see ἐνδώμησις\[b], [7\.41](#7.41)).

[REV 21:18](https://ref.ly/Jude21:18)

7\.41 · Constructions · Parts and Areas of Buildings

foundation that on which a structure is built In some languages it is possible to describe a typical foundation in ancient times as ‘large stones underneath the walls.’ In other languages, however, this may seem to be quite a meaningless type of expression, since foundations are only made secure by driving stakes deep into the ground. Therefore, it may be best to describe the function of a foundation by ‘what keeps the walls firm’ or ‘how the walls are made not to move’ or ‘what goes beneath the walls.’\|A number of scholars, however, prefer to understand ἐνδώμησις in the context of [REV 21:18](https://ref.ly/Jude21:18) as the material of which the wall was made, rather than as the foundation of the wall (see ἐνδώμησις\[a], [7\.77](#7.77)).

[REV 21:18](https://ref.ly/Jude21:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Revelation 21:18


## ἐνέδρα (id: 001762000000000)

ἐνέδρα
======

ἐνέδρα (G1749)

noun · ἑδραῖος

39\.51 · Hostility, Strife · Ambush

ambush, planning a secret attack concealing oneself or proceeding secretly, while waiting for an appropriate opportunity to attack Since ambushing is such a widespread method of warfare and private attack, there is usually little or no difficulty involved in finding an appropriate term and using it in what might be called an anticipatory sense, that is to say, the employment of an ambush despite the fact that it was not finally successful.

[ACT 23:16](https://ref.ly/John23:16) · [ACT 25:3](https://ref.ly/John25:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 23:16; Acts 25:3

## ἐνεργέω (id: 001770000000000)

ἐνεργέω
=======

ἐνεργέω (G1754)

ἐργάζομαι

42\.3 · Perform, Do · Function

to function, to work, to be at work, practice to be engaged in some activity or function, with possible focus upon the energy or force involved

[MAT 14:2](https://ref.ly/Matt14:2) · [MRK 6:14](https://ref.ly/Matt6:14) · [ROM 7:5](https://ref.ly/Acts7:5) · [2CO 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:6) · [2CO 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:12) · [GAL 5:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:6) · [EPH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Gal1:11) · [EPH 2:2](https://ref.ly/Gal2:2) · [EPH 3:20](https://ref.ly/Gal3:20) · [PHP 2:13](https://ref.ly/Eph2:13) · [COL 1:29](https://ref.ly/Phil1:29) · [1TH 2:13](https://ref.ly/Col2:13) · [2TH 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:7) · [JAS 5:16](https://ref.ly/Heb5:16)

42\.4 · Perform, Do · Function

to cause to function, to grant the ability to do to cause or make possible a particular function In many instances it is important to specify the references of πάντα and πᾶσιν in [1CO 12:6](https://ref.ly/Rom12:6), so that one may translate ‘the same God gives the ability to everyone for his or her particular service.’

[1CO 12:6](https://ref.ly/Rom12:6) · [1CO 12:11](https://ref.ly/Rom12:11) · [2CO 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:6) · [GAL 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:8) · [GAL 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:8)

13\.9 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · State

to cause to be, to make to be, to make, to result in, to bring upon, to bring about to cause a state to be1

[2CO 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:6) · [GAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:5) · [PHP 2:13](https://ref.ly/Eph2:13)

1 Any meaning involving ‘to cause to be’ implies some change in state, and therefore one could classify these meanings under Subdomain B Change of State in Domain 13 Be, Become, Exist, Happen. However, the focus seems to be primarily upon the changed state rather than upon the process of moving from one state to another. The fact that a change of state has taken place seems to be semantically incidental.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 14:2; Mark 6:14; Romans 7:5; 2 Corinthians 1:6; 2 Corinthians 4:12; Galatians 5:6; Ephesians 1:11; Ephesians 2:2; Ephesians 3:20; Philippians 2:13; Colossians 1:29; 1 Thessalonians 2:13; 2 Thessalonians 2:7; James 5:16; 1 Corinthians 12:6; 1 Corinthians 12:11; Galatians 2:8; Galatians 3:5

## ἐνέχω (id: 001774000000000)

ἐνέχω
=====

ἐνέχω (G1758) — ἐνέχομαι

ἔχω

88\.169 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Resentful, Hold a Grudge Against

to be resentful against, to have a grudge against to feel resentful because of what someone has done

[MRK 6:19](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19)

39\.4 · Hostility, Strife · Opposition, Hostility

to be against, to be hostile toward to maintain a state of antagonism or hostility In a number of languages hostility is expressed largely in psychological terms, for example, ‘to be angry at’ or ‘to have one’s heart curdle against,’ but hostility may also be expressed in terms of overt behavior, for example, ‘to turn against,’ ‘to face away from,’ or ‘to turn one’s back on.’

[LUK 11:53](https://ref.ly/Mark11:53)

37\.4 · Control, Rule · Control, Restrain

to be subject to, to be under the control of to be under the control of or to be subject to someone or something In a number of languages it is difficult or impossible to maintain the figure of ‘yoke’ in [GAL 5:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:1). An equivalent expression may be ‘do not become slaves again’ or ‘do not let people make you slaves again.’ Such a bold metaphor, however, must sometimes be changed to a simile: ‘do not, as it were, let yourselves be made slaves.’

[GAL 5:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 6:19; Luke 11:53; Galatians 5:1

θυμός · θυμόομαι

30\.1 · Think · To Think, Thought

to think about, to consider to process information by giving consideration to various aspects1 In a number of languages the concept of thinking is closely related to terms referring to perception. For example, in some languages one may speak of thinking as ‘to see with the heart’ or ‘to look at with the liver.’

1 It is possible that ἐμβλέπω\[b] differs somewhat in meaning from βλέπω\[d] in focusing the thought processes on some particular object or matter, but it is difficult to determine this on the basis of existing contexts.

[JHN 11:49](https://ref.ly/Luke11:49) · [JHN 11:51](https://ref.ly/Luke11:51) · [JHN 18:13](https://ref.ly/Luke18:13) · [ACT 11:26](https://ref.ly/John11:26) · [ACT 18:11](https://ref.ly/John18:11) · [GAL 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:10) · [HEB 9:7](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:7) · [HEB 9:25](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:25) · [HEB 10:1](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:1) · [HEB 10:3](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:3) · [JAS 4:13](https://ref.ly/Heb4:13) · [JAS 5:17](https://ref.ly/Heb5:17) · [REV 9:15](https://ref.ly/Jude9:15)

67\.146 · Time · Indefinite Units of Time: Age, Lifetime, Interval, Period

age, era, time a unit of time of indefinite length, possibly somewhat shorter than αἰών\[a] ‘age’ ([67\.143](#67.143)) but of a relatively uncertain reference

[LUK 4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:19)

## ἐνισχύω (id: 001782000000000)

ἐνισχύω
=======

ἐνισχύω (G1765)

ἰσχύω

79\.66 · Features of Objects · Strong, Weak

to strengthen again, to cause strength to return to cause someone to regain strength after a temporary loss

[LUK 22:43](https://ref.ly/Mark22:43)

79\.65 · Features of Objects · Strong, Weak

to regain strength to regain one’s physical strength after a temporary loss of it It may, however, be impossible to say in some languages ‘his strength came back,’ because this would seem to imply a kind of personification of ‘strength.’ Therefore, it may be necessary to translate this phrase in [ACT 9:19](https://ref.ly/John9:19) as ‘after he had eaten, he became strong again’ or ‘... he was no longer weak.’

[ACT 9:19](https://ref.ly/John9:19)

## ἐννέα (id: 001783000000000)

ἐννέα
=====

ἐννέα (G1767)

adj · ἔνατος

60\.18 · Number · One, Two, Three, Etc. \[Cardinals]

nine cardinal number, where there are nine instances of a countable entity In [LUK 17:17](https://ref.ly/Mark17:17) there is a significant ellipsis, and so in some languages one may wish to translate ‘where are the other nine persons?’ or ‘... nine men?’

[MAT 18:12](https://ref.ly/Matt18:12) · [MAT 18:13](https://ref.ly/Matt18:13) · [LUK 15:4](https://ref.ly/Mark15:4) · [LUK 15:7](https://ref.ly/Mark15:7) · [LUK 17:17](https://ref.ly/Mark17:17)

## ἐννεύω (id: 001784000000000)

ἐννεύω
======

ἐννεύω (G1770)

νεύω

33\.485 · Communication · Non\-Verbal Communication

to gesture, to motion, to nod, to beckon to signal to someone by means of part of the body, especially by means of the head or hands1 In a number of languages it is necessary to be quite specific with regard to types of gestures or motions of the head or hands and arms. For example, in [JHN 13:24](https://ref.ly/Luke13:24) there is an obvious question involved, and this may require in some languages a particular signal by means of the hands or face. The same would be true in the case of [LUK 1:62](https://ref.ly/Mark1:62). In [LUK 1:22](https://ref.ly/Mark1:22) the gesture would have to be something which would explain or indicate Zechariah’s inability to speak, while in [LUK 5:7](https://ref.ly/Mark5:7) quite another gesture would no doubt be called for, since the persons involved were at quite a distance from one another.

[LUK 1:62](https://ref.ly/Mark1:62)

1 There are no doubt certain subtle differences of meaning between νεύω, ἐννεύω, διανεύω, and κατανεύω, but such distinctions are not evident from existing contexts.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 1:62


## ἔννοια (id: 001785000000000)

ἔννοια
======

ἔννοια (G1771)

noun · νοέω

30\.5 · Think · To Think, Thought

way of thinking, disposition, manner of thought, attitude a particular manner or way of thinking In a number of languages it may be necessary to render ‘way of thinking’ by a clause, for example, ‘how people think.’ In the case of [1PE 4:1](https://ref.ly/Jas4:1), it may be necessary to translate ‘you too must strengthen yourselves by thinking just like Christ thought.’

[1PE 4:1](https://ref.ly/Jas4:1)

30\.66 · Think · To Intend, To Purpose, To Plan

intention, purpose that which is intended or purposed as the result of thinking

[HEB 4:12](https://ref.ly/Phlm4:12)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Peter 4:1; Hebrews 4:12


## ἔννομος (id: 001786000000000)

ἔννομος
=======

ἔννομος (G1772)

adj · νόμος

33\.336 · Communication · Law, Regulation, Ordinance

legal, in accordance with law (derivative of νόμος\[a] ‘law, rule,’ [33\.333](#33.333)) pertaining to being in accordance with law In some languages the equivalent of ‘legal meeting’ would be ‘regular meeting’ or ‘a meeting called by an official’ or ‘a meeting authorized by an official.’

[ACT 19:39](https://ref.ly/John19:39)

33\.342 · Communication · Law, Regulation, Ordinance

subject to law, under law pertaining to being under obligation imposed by law In some languages there is a difficulty involved in rendering ἔννομος by a phrase such as ‘under law,’ since this may be understood in a sense of ‘beneath the law’ or ‘illegal.’ A more common expression would be ‘tied to the law’ or ‘obligated by the law.’

[1CO 9:21](https://ref.ly/Rom9:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 19:39; 1 Corinthians 9:21


## ἔννυχος (id: 001787000000000)

ἔννυχος
=======

ἔννυχος (G1773) — ἔννυχα

adj · νύξ

67\.193 · Time · Definite Units of Time: Year, Month, Week, Day, Hour

at night, in the night pertaining to the period between sunset and sunrise In some languages it may be necessary to translate ἔννυχα λίαν in [MRK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Matt1:35) as ‘before sunrise’ or ‘long before daylight’ or ‘before the heavens open.’

[MRK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Matt1:35)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 1:35

=======

ἐνοικέω (G1774)

οἰκέω

85\.73 · Existence in Space · Dwell, Reside

to be in, to live in, to dwell in, to reside in (a figurative extension of meaning of ἐνοικέω ‘to live in,’ not occurring in the NT) to remain in a place defined psychologically or spiritually

[2CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:16) · [COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Phil3:16) · [2TI 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:5) · [2TI 1:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:14)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 6:16; Colossians 3:16; 2 Timothy 1:5; 2 Timothy 1:14


## ἐνορκίζω (id: 001789000000000)

ἐνορκίζω
========

ἐνορκίζω (G3726a)

ὁρκίζω

33\.467 · Communication · Swear, Put Under Oath, Vow

to put under oath, to insist that one take an oath, to require that one swear to demand that a person take an oath as to the truth of what is said or as to the certainty that one will carry out the request or command1 It is extremely difficult to translate ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω in a literal manner, though in some contexts one can say ‘I put you under oath,’ but in a number of passages the person speaking is imploring and not necessarily in a position to command or insist. Therefore, in a passage such as [MRK 5:7](https://ref.ly/Matt5:7), one may render the meaning idiomatically as ‘for God’s sake, I ask you, do not punish me.’ In [1TH 5:27](https://ref.ly/Col5:27) one may translate ‘in the name of the Lord, I ask you to read this letter to all the Christian brothers,’ and in [MAT 26:63](https://ref.ly/Matt26:63) one may translate ‘in the name of the living God, I charge you, Tell us who you are.’\|In most languages one may translate ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω by simply adding a causative component to terms meaning ‘to swear’ or ‘to take an oath.’ In other words, ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω may be rendered as ‘to cause a person to say under oath.’

[1TH 5:27](https://ref.ly/Col5:27)

1 It is possible that ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω differ slightly in meaning, but this cannot be determined on the basis of available contexts.

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Thessalonians 5:27

## ἔνοχος (id: 001792000000000)

ἔνοχος
======

ἔνοχος (G1777)

adj · ἔχω

88\.312 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Sin, Wrongdoing, Guilt

guilty, liable pertaining to being guilty for having done wrong (primarily a legal term) In some languages the equivalent of ‘being guilty’ is expressed in terms of the justification for an accusation. Accordingly, this expression in [1CO 11:27](https://ref.ly/Rom11:27) may be rendered as ‘he can rightly be accused of sinning against the Lord’s body and blood.’

37\.5 · Control, Rule · Control, Restrain

controlled by, under the control of, subject to pertaining to being subject to the control of someone or of some institution In this context one may render ἔνοχοι ἦσαν δουλείας as ‘slaves’ and then render the whole expression as ‘as many as were slaves all their lives because of their fear of death’ or ‘... because they were afraid to die.’

[HEB 2:15](https://ref.ly/Phlm2:15)

prep

83\.9 · Spacial Positions · Among, Between, In, Inside

among, with a position within an area determined by other objects and distributed among such objects1 For another interpretation of ἐντός in [LUK 17:21](https://ref.ly/Mark17:21), see discussion at [26\.1](#26.1).

[LUK 17:21](https://ref.ly/Mark17:21)

83\.17 · Spacial Positions · Among, Between, In, Inside

the contents of, that which is inside pertaining to being within an area It is also possible, though not probable, to understand ἐντός in [MAT 23:26](https://ref.ly/Matt23:26) as the inside surface itself rather than the contents.

[MAT 23:26](https://ref.ly/Matt23:26) · [LUK 17:21](https://ref.ly/Mark17:21)

1 There are no doubt certain subtle distinctions in the meanings of the terms in [83\.9](#83.9). For example, πρός\[d] may suggest some kind of interaction or response and παρά\[b] may focus upon association, but it is difficult, if not impossible, on the basis of existing contexts to define precisely the differences in meaning.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 17:21; Matthew 23:26


## ἐντρέπω (id: 001803000000000)

ἐντρέπω
=======

ἐντρέπω (G1788) — ἐντρέπομαι

τροπή

25\.196 · Attitudes and Emotions · Shame, Disgrace, Humiliation

to shame, to embarrass to cause someone to be embarrassed or ashamed

[1CO 4:14](https://ref.ly/Rom4:14) · [2TH 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:14) · [TIT 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:8)

87\.11 · Status · Honor or Respect in Relation to Status

to respect, to show respect to show respect to a person on the basis of his high status In a number of languages the showing of respect is referred to by a number of figurative expressions, for example, ‘to bow before,’ ‘to kneel before,’ ‘to crouch before,’ or ‘to stand appalled in the presence of.’

[MAT 21:37](https://ref.ly/Matt21:37) · [MRK 12:6](https://ref.ly/Matt12:6) · [LUK 18:2](https://ref.ly/Mark18:2) · [LUK 18:4](https://ref.ly/Mark18:4) · [LUK 20:13](https://ref.ly/Mark20:13) · [HEB 12:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:9)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 4:14; 2 Thessalonians 3:14; Titus 2:8; Matthew 21:37; Mark 12:6; Luke 18:2; Luke 18:4; Luke 20:13; Hebrews 12:9

## ἔντρομος (id: 001805000000000)

ἔντρομος
========

ἔντρομος (G1790)

adj · τρέμω

16\.6 · Non\-Linear Movement

trembling, quivering, shaking shaking or trembling, often with the implication of fear and/or consternation In a number of languages there are two distinct terms for trembling, one relating to illness (as in the case of epileptic seizures or the chills and fever associated with malaria) and the other associated with fear.

[ACT 7:32](https://ref.ly/John7:32)

25\.261 · Attitudes and Emotions · Fear, Terror, Alarm

trembling with fear, extremely fearful pertaining to extreme terror or fear, often accompanied by trembling In [HEB 12:21](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:21) the meanings of ἔκφοβος ([25\.256](#25.256)) and ἔντρομος serve to reinforce each other.

[ACT 16:29](https://ref.ly/John16:29) · [HEB 12:21](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 7:32; Acts 16:29; Hebrews 12:21


## ἐντροπή (id: 001806000000000)

ἐντροπή
=======

ἐντροπή (G1791)

noun · τροπή

25\.195 · Attitudes and Emotions · Shame, Disgrace, Humiliation

embarrassment, shame a state of embarrassment resulting from what one has done or failed to do1

[1CO 6:5](https://ref.ly/Rom6:5) · [1CO 15:34](https://ref.ly/Rom15:34)

1 ἐντροπή and ἐντρέπω ([25\.196](#25.196)) differ from αἰσχύνη\[a] ([25\.189](#25.189)) and αἰσχύνω ([25\.190](#25.190)) in seeming to focus upon the embarrassment which is involved in the feeling of shame.

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 6:5; 1 Corinthians 15:34

## ἐνύπνιον (id: 001813000000000)

ἐνύπνιον
========

ἐνύπνιον (G1798)

noun · ὕπνος

33\.486 · Communication · Non\-Verbal Communication

dream a dream as a means of communication1 In a number of languages there are different terms for ‘dream’ depending upon the nature of the dream. A frightening dream without special significance or a dream with erotic implications may be referred to by terms which are quite different from a term referring to a dream which carries important meaning, in other words, a dream which constitutes a type of vision or symbol of some reality.

[ACT 2:17](https://ref.ly/John2:17)

1 The meaning of ὄναρ and ἐνύπνιον could be classified as a psychological state, but their direct connection with communication seems to justify their being included in this domain.

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 2:17

=========

ἀγγέλλω

33\.204 · Communication · Inform, Announce

to proclaim throughout, to announce, to speak out about to announce, with focus upon the extent to which the announcement or proclamation extends1

[MRK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Matt16:8) · [1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/Jas2:9)

## ἐξαγοράζω (id: 001820000000000)

ἐξαγοράζω
=========

ἐξαγοράζω (G1805) — ἐξαγοράζομαι

ἀγοράζω

37\.131 · Control, Rule · Release, Set Free

to redeem, to set free (figurative extension of meaning of ἐξαγοράζω ‘to pay a price,’ not occurring in the NT) to cause the release or freedom of someone by a means which proves costly to the individual causing the release A literal rendering of ἀγοράζω\[b] or ἐξαγοράζω as ‘to release by means of paying a price’ can be misinterpreted in the sense that Christ actually engaged in some kind of monetary transaction. A literal translation may also lead to the mistaken interpretation, which was widespread in the Middle Ages, that in redeeming the believers God actually paid a price to the Devil. Obviously, ἀγοράζω in [1CO 6:20](https://ref.ly/Rom6:20) and ἐξαγοράζω in [GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:13) must be understood in a figurative sense.

[GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:13) · [GAL 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:5)

65\.42 · Value · Advantageous, Not Advantageous

**Collocation:** ἐξαγοράζομαι τὸν καιρόν

to make good use of every opportunity, to take advantage of every chance (an idiom, literally: to buy out the time) to take full advantage of any opportunity The implication of this statement in [COL 4:5](https://ref.ly/Phil4:5) is taking advantage of every opportunity to manifest the reality of one’s faith.

[EPH 5:16](https://ref.ly/Gal5:16) · [COL 4:5](https://ref.ly/Phil4:5)

68\.73 · Aspect · Do Intensely or Extensively

**Collocation:** ἐξαγοράζομαι τὸν καιρόν

to work urgently, to redeem the time (an idiom, literally: to redeem the time) to do something with intensity and urgency (used absolutely) It is also possible to interpret ἐξαγοράζομαι τὸν καιρόν in [EPH 5:16](https://ref.ly/Gal5:16) as ‘to take full advantage of every opportunity’ (see [65\.42](#65.42)).

[EPH 5:16](https://ref.ly/Gal5:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Galatians 3:13; Galatians 4:5; Ephesians 5:16; Colossians 4:5

## ἐξαίφνης (id: 001825000000000)

ἐξαίφνης
========

ἐξαίφνης (G1810)

adv · ἄφνω

67\.113 · Time · Duration of Time without Reference to Points or Units of Time: Time, Spend Time, Always, Eternal, Old, Immediately, Young

suddenly, at once, immediately pertaining to an extremely short period of time between a previous state or event and a subsequent state or event In a number of contexts there is the implication of unexpectedness, but this seems to be a derivative of the context as a whole and not a part of the meaning of the lexical items.

[MRK 13:36](https://ref.ly/Matt13:36) · [LUK 2:13](https://ref.ly/Mark2:13) · [LUK 9:39](https://ref.ly/Mark9:39) · [ACT 9:3](https://ref.ly/John9:3) · [ACT 22:6](https://ref.ly/John22:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 13:36; Luke 2:13; Luke 9:39; Acts 9:3; Acts 22:6


## ἐξακολουθέω (id: 001826000000000)

ἐξακολουθέω
===========

ἐξακολουθέω (G1811)

ἀκολουθέω

36\.33 · Guide, Discipline, Follow · Follow, Be a Disciple

to follow, to conform to to conform as a follower in a detailed or dependent manner In some instances it may be necessary to translate ἐξακολουθήσαντες in [2PE 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:16) as ‘to use’ or ‘to repeat,’ since such expressions may fit the context more satisfactorily.

[2PE 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:16)

41\.48 · Behavior and Related States · Imitate Behavior

to imitate to imitate behavior closely (in the NT the imitation involves wrong behavior) An equivalent of this expression in [2PE 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2) may be in some instances ‘many will live immoral lives just as they did.’

[2PE 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2) · [2PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:15)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Peter 1:16; 2 Peter 2:2; 2 Peter 2:15


## ἑξακόσιοι (id: 001827000000000)

ἑξακόσιοι
=========

ἑξακόσιοι (G1812)

adj · ἕξ

60\.38 · Number · One, Two, Three, Etc. \[Cardinals]

six hundred cardinal number, where there are six hundred instances of a countable entity The symbolic number in [REV 13:18](https://ref.ly/Jude13:18) would result from the total of the numerical values of the letters of the person’s name.

[REV 13:18](https://ref.ly/Jude13:18) · [REV 14:20](https://ref.ly/Jude14:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Revelation 13:18; Revelation 14:20

to wipe away to remove a liquid by wiping off

13\.102 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Exist

to eliminate, to do away with, to wipe out (a figurative extension of meaning of ἐξαλείφω\[a] ‘to wipe off, to wipe away,’ [47\.18](#47.18)) to cause something to cease by obliterating any evidence

[ACT 3:19](https://ref.ly/John3:19) · [COL 2:14](https://ref.ly/Phil2:14) · [REV 3:5](https://ref.ly/Jude3:5)

## ἐξάλλομαι (id: 001829000000000)

ἐξάλλομαι
=========

ἐξάλλομαι (G1814)

ἅλλομαι

15\.240 · Linear Movement · Jump, Leap

to jump up, to leap up to leap up to a standing position It is possible that in [ACT 3:8](https://ref.ly/John3:8) the paralyzed man actually jumped up from the ground, but it is more likely that the reference is to the rapidity with which he got onto his feet.

[ACT 3:8](https://ref.ly/John3:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 3:8


## ἐξανάστασις (id: 001830000000000)

ἐξανάστασις
===========

ἐξανάστασις (G1815)

noun · ἵστημι

23\.93 · Physiological Processes and States · Live, Die

to come back to life, to live again, to be resurrected, resurrection to come back to life after having once died In a number of languages there is a difficulty involved in formulating some expression for ‘resurrection’ or ‘living again,’ since such a phrase may refer to what is technically known as metempsychosis, that is to say, the rebirth of the soul in another existence, a belief which is widely held in a number of areas of south Asia. This problem may be avoided in some languages by speaking of ‘his body will live again’ or ‘his body will come back to life’ or ‘he will be the same person when he lives again.’\|The phrase ‘the resurrection from among the dead’ may be rendered as ‘to live again’ or ‘to live again after having died.’

[PHP 3:11](https://ref.ly/Eph3:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Philippians 3:11


## ἐξανατέλλω (id: 001831000000000)

ἐξανατέλλω
==========

ἐξανατέλλω (G1816)

ἀνατέλλω

23\.195 · Physiological Processes and States · Grow, Growth

to sprout, to sprout leaves to begin vegetative growth, with special emphasis upon the sprouting of leaves The initial stages of growth, either of a plant as a whole or in the renewal of foliage after a winter or dry season, may be expressed in a number of ways, for example, ‘to become green again,’ ‘to show leaves,’ ‘to see leaves begin,’ or ‘when leaves unfold.’

[MAT 13:5](https://ref.ly/Matt13:5) · [MRK 4:5](https://ref.ly/Matt4:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 13:5; Mark 4:5


## ἐξανίστημι (id: 001832000000000)

ἐξανίστημι
==========

ἐξανίστημι (G1817) — ἐξανίσταμαι

ἵστημι

17\.11 · Stances and Events Related to Stances · Stand

to stand up to stand up in a manner distinct from someone else

[ACT 15:5](https://ref.ly/John15:5)

23\.59 · Physiological Processes and States · Birth, Procreation

**Collocation:** ἐξανίστημι σπέρμα

to beget, to procreate, to become the father of (an idiom, literally: to raise up seed) the male role in begetting children In contexts such as [MAT 22:24](https://ref.ly/Matt22:24), [MRK 12:19](https://ref.ly/Matt12:19), and [LUK 20:28](https://ref.ly/Mark20:28) it may be important to add a note concerning levirate responsibilities, that is to say, the requirement for a younger brother to beget children by a deceased older brother’s wife and the reckoning of such an offspring as being the descendant of the older deceased brother.

[MRK 12:19](https://ref.ly/Matt12:19) · [LUK 20:28](https://ref.ly/Mark20:28)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 15:5; Mark 12:19; Luke 20:28


## ἐξαπατάω (id: 001833000000000)

ἐξαπατάω
========

ἐξαπατάω (G1818)

ἀπατάω

31\.12 · Hold a View, Believe, Trust · Hold a Wrong View, Be Mistaken

to mislead, to deceive, deception to cause someone to have misleading or erroneous views concerning the truth1

[ROM 7:11](https://ref.ly/Acts7:11) · [ROM 16:18](https://ref.ly/Acts16:18) · [1CO 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rom3:18) · [2CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3) · [2TH 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:3) · [1TI 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:14)

1 This series of meanings containing the stem ἀπατ overlaps considerably in meaning with the previous series containing the stem πλαν ([31\.8](#31.8)\-[31\.11](#31.11)). Both sets of terms involve deception and erroneous views. It is difficult to determine the precise implications of differences in meaning, but it may be that terms with the stem πλαν are somewhat more related to general deceptive behavior rather than primarily to misconceptions.

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 7:11; Romans 16:18; 1 Corinthians 3:18; 2 Corinthians 11:3; 2 Thessalonians 2:3; 1 Timothy 2:14

16:17](https://ref.ly/Jude16:17) · [REV 18:4](https://ref.ly/Jude18:4) · [REV 19:5](https://ref.ly/Jude19:5) · [REV 19:21](https://ref.ly/Jude19:21) · [REV 20:8](https://ref.ly/Jude20:8)

13\.93 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Exist

to cease to exist, to pass away, to cease to go out of existence

[ACT 16:19](https://ref.ly/John16:19)

23\.60 · Physiological Processes and States · Birth, Procreation

**Collocation:** ἐξέρχομαι ἐκ τῆς ὀσφύος

to be born of, to be begotten by (an idiom, literally: to come out from the loins, genitals) to be born as the result of the male role in begetting an offspring In a number of languages it is more satisfactory to speak of the male role in procreation in terms of ‘being the father of’ rather than employing a term which may have undesirable connotations of sexual relations.

[HEB 7:5](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:5)

41\.24 · Behavior and Related States · Behavior, Conduct

**Collocation:** εἰσέρχομαι καὶ ἐξέρχομαι

to live with, to live among (idiom, literally: to go in and to go out) to live or to conduct oneself in relationship to some community or group The semantic focus of these idioms, especially in [ACT 1:21](https://ref.ly/John1:21) and [ACT 9:28](https://ref.ly/John9:28), appears to be the manner in which a person conducts himself in relationship to others, but it is possible to imply a certain amount of movement. For example, in [ACT 1:21](https://ref.ly/John1:21) it is possible to translate ‘during the whole time the Lord Jesus travelled about with us,’ and in [ACT 9:28](https://ref.ly/John9:28) one could translate ‘Paul stayed with them and went about in Jerusalem.’

[ACT 1:21](https://ref.ly/John1:21)

## ἔξεστι(ν) (id: 001846000000000)

ἔξεστι(ν)
=========

ἔξεστι(ν) (G1832) — ἔξεστι, ἔξεστι \| ἔξεστιν, ἔξεστιν

εἰμί

71\.1 · Mode · Possible, Impossible

to be possible to mark an event as being possible in a highly generic sense In [ACT 2:29](https://ref.ly/John2:29) the possibility of speaking confidently about David evidently is based upon well\-known facts.

[MAT 20:15](https://ref.ly/Matt20:15) · [ACT 2:29](https://ref.ly/John2:29) · [ACT 21:37](https://ref.ly/John21:37) · [1CO 6:12](https://ref.ly/Rom6:12) · [1CO 6:12](https://ref.ly/Rom6:12) · [1CO 10:23](https://ref.ly/Rom10:23) · [1CO 10:23](https://ref.ly/Rom10:23)

71\.32 · Mode · Should, Ought

must, ought to, to be lawful, to be permitted, (with neg.) ought not to to be obligatory; to be within the law

[MAT 12:2](https://ref.ly/Matt12:2) · [MAT 12:4](https://ref.ly/Matt12:4) · [MAT 12:10](https://ref.ly/Matt12:10) · [MAT 12:12](https://ref.ly/Matt12:12) · [MAT 14:4](https://ref.ly/Matt14:4) · [MAT 19:3](https://ref.ly/Matt19:3) · [MAT 22:17](https://ref.ly/Matt22:17) · [MAT 27:6](https://ref.ly/Matt27:6) · [MRK 2:24](https://ref.ly/Matt2:24) · [MRK 2:26](https://ref.ly/Matt2:26) · [MRK 3:4](https://ref.ly/Matt3:4) · [MRK 6:18](https://ref.ly/Matt6:18) · [MRK 10:2](https://ref.ly/Matt10:2) · [MRK 12:14](https://ref.ly/Matt12:14) · [LUK 6:2](https://ref.ly/Mark6:2) · [LUK 6:4](https://ref.ly/Mark6:4) · [LUK 6:9](https://ref.ly/Mark6:9) · [LUK 14:3](https://ref.ly/Mark14:3) · [LUK 20:22](https://ref.ly/Mark20:22) · [JHN 5:10](https://ref.ly/Luke5:10) · [JHN 18:31](https://ref.ly/Luke18:31) · [ACT 16:21](https://ref.ly/John16:21) · [ACT 22:25](https://ref.ly/John22:25) · [2CO 12:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:4)

=======

ἐτάζω

to try to find out, to make a diligent effort to learn to engage in a careful search in order to acquire information, though primarily by inquiry

[MAT 2:8](https://ref.ly/Matt2:8) · [MAT 10:11](https://ref.ly/Matt10:11)

33\.182 · Communication · Question, Answer

to inquire, to ask to inquire intently, with the implication of careful examination

## ἐξηγέομαι (id: 001848000000000)

ἐξηγέομαι
=========

ἐξηγέομαι (G1834)

ἄγω

33\.201 · Communication · Inform, Announce

to inform, to relate, to tell fully to provide detailed information in a systematic manner1 For another interpretation of ἐξηγέομαι in [JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18), see [28\.41](#28.41).

[LUK 24:35](https://ref.ly/Mark24:35) · [JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18) · [ACT 10:8](https://ref.ly/John10:8) · [ACT 15:12](https://ref.ly/John15:12) · [ACT 21:19](https://ref.ly/John21:19)

28\.41 · Know · Well Known, Clearly Shown, Revealed

to make fully and clearly known to make something fully known by careful explanation or by clear revelation For another interpretation of ἐξηγέομαι in [JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18), see [33\.201](#33.201).

[JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18) · [ACT 15:14](https://ref.ly/John15:14)

1 It is possible that ἐξηγέομαι\[a], διηγέομαι, and ἐκδιηγέομαι differ somewhat in emphasis or connotation, but it is impossible to determine this from NT usage.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 24:35; John 1:18; Acts 10:8; Acts 15:12; Acts 21:19; Acts 15:14


## ἑξήκοντα (id: 001849000000000)

ἑξήκοντα
========

ἑξήκοντα (G1835)

adj · ἕξ

60\.29 · Number · One, Two, Three, Etc. \[Cardinals]

sixty cardinal number, where there are sixty instances of a countable entity Note that ἑξήκοντα in [MAT 13:8](https://ref.ly/Matt13:8) is not ἑξηκόντακις ‘sixty times.’ The evident meaning here is that some of the seeds grew into plants which produced as many as a hundred seeds; others grew into plants which produced sixty seeds; and still others, only thirty seeds.

[MAT 13:8](https://ref.ly/Matt13:8) · [MAT 13:23](https://ref.ly/Matt13:23) · [MRK 4:8](https://ref.ly/Matt4:8) · [MRK 4:20](https://ref.ly/Matt4:20) · [LUK 24:13](https://ref.ly/Mark24:13) · [1TI 5:9](https://ref.ly/2Thess5:9) · [REV 11:3](https://ref.ly/Jude11:3) · [REV 12:6](https://ref.ly/Jude12:6) · [REV 13:18](https://ref.ly/Jude13:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 13:8; Matthew 13:23; Mark 4:8; Mark 4:20; Luke 24:13; 1 Timothy 5:9; Revelation 11:3; Revelation 12:6; Revelation 13:18

**Collocation:** τῇ ἑξῆς

the next day, on the next day a day immediately following a previous day1

## ἕξις (id: 001852000000000)

ἕξις
====

ἕξις (G1838)

noun · ἔχω

42\.10 · Perform, Do · Do, Perform

practice, doing again and again, doing repeatedly a repeated activity In a number of languages, however, it may be necessary to restructure this second part of [HEB 5:14](https://ref.ly/Phlm5:14) and translate as follows: ‘since they have often judged between good and evil, their abilities to do so are well\-trained’ or ‘... they are well qualified to do so’ or ‘... they can easily and correctly do so.’

ἵστημι

25\.220 · Attitudes and Emotions · Surprise, Astonish

to astonish greatly, to greatly astound, to astound completely cause someone to be so astounded as to be practically overwhelmed

[MAT 12:23](https://ref.ly/Matt12:23) · [MRK 2:12](https://ref.ly/Matt2:12) · [MRK 5:42](https://ref.ly/Matt5:42) · [LUK 8:56](https://ref.ly/Mark8:56) · [LUK 24:22](https://ref.ly/Mark24:22) · [ACT 2:7](https://ref.ly/John2:7) · [ACT 2:12](https://ref.ly/John2:12) · [ACT 8:9](https://ref.ly/John8:9) · [ACT 8:11](https://ref.ly/John8:11) · [ACT 8:13](https://ref.ly/John8:13) · [ACT 9:21](https://ref.ly/John9:21) · [ACT 10:45](https://ref.ly/John10:45) · [ACT 12:16](https://ref.ly/John12:16)

25\.218 · Attitudes and Emotions · Surprise, Astonish

to be greatly astonished, to be astounded to be so astonished as to almost fail to comprehend what one has experienced

[MRK 6:51](https://ref.ly/Matt6:51) · [LUK 2:47](https://ref.ly/Mark2:47)

30\.24 · Think · To Think, Thought

to not be in one’s right mind, to be insane, to be mad, to be out of one’s mind, insanity, madness to think or reason in a completely irrational manner

## ἔξοδος (id: 001855000000000)

ἔξοδος
======

ἔξοδος (G1841)

noun · ὁδός

15\.42 · Linear Movement · Leave, Depart, Flee, Escape, Send

departure, the Exodus, the departure (of Israel from Egypt) motion from or out of a region (in [HEB 11:22](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:22), the only occurrence of ἔξοδος\[a] in the NT, the reference is to Israels departure from Egypt)

[HEB 11:22](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:22)

23\.101 · Physiological Processes and States · Live, Die

to leave this life, to die, death, departure to depart from life, as a euphemistic expression for death1

[LUK 9:31](https://ref.ly/Mark9:31) · [2PE 1:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:15)

1 Expressions occurring in numbered items [23\.101](#23.101)\-[23\.105](#23.105) involve euphemistic expressions for death. These have been subdivided on the basis of certain significant features of the underlying bases, as will be noted in the discussions.

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 11:22; Luke 9:31; 2 Peter 1:15


## ἐξολεθρεύω (id: 001856000000000)

ἐξολεθρεύω
==========

ἐξολεθρεύω (G1842)

ὀλοθρεύω

20\.35 · Violence, Harm, Destroy, Kill · Destroy

to destroy, to destroy and remove to destroy and thus eliminate It is possible in [ACT 3:23](https://ref.ly/John3:23) that ἐξολεθρεύω refers to a type of severe ostracism.

[ACT 3:23](https://ref.ly/John3:23)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 3:23


## ἐξομολογέω (id: 001857000000000)

ἐξομολογέω
==========

ἐξομολογέω (G1843) — ἐξομολογέομαι

33\.278 · Communication · Agree

to agree to to indicate agreement with a proposition or offer In some languages one may render ‘he agreed to it’ by simply saying ‘he said, I will do it.’

[LUK 22:6](https://ref.ly/Mark22:6)

33\.274 · Communication · Profess Allegiance

to profess, to confess, confession to express openly one’s allegiance to a proposition or person It is often extremely difficult, if not impossible, to translate ὁμολογέω\[a], ὁμολογία, and ἐξομολογέω\[b] by the usual expression for ‘confess,’ since this would usually imply that one has done something wrong. It is normally necessary, therefore, to employ quite a different type of relationship, usually involving a public utterance and an expression of confidence or allegiance. For example, in [MAT 10:32](https://ref.ly/Matt10:32) it may be necessary to translate ‘whoever tells people publicly that he is loyal to me, I will tell my Father that I am loyal to that person.’ Similarly, in [HEB 10:23](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:23) one may translate ‘let us hold on to the hope in which we have told people we have such confidence.’ Likewise, in [ROM 15:9](https://ref.ly/Acts15:9) one may translate ‘therefore I will tell the Gentiles how I have put my confidence in you.’\|For another interpretation of ἐξομολογέομαι in [ROM 15:9](https://ref.ly/Acts15:9), see [33\.359](#33.359).

[ROM 14:11](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11)1 · [ROM 15:9](https://ref.ly/Acts15:9)

33\.275 · Communication · Admit, Confess, Deny

to admit, to confess to acknowledge a fact publicly, often in reference to previous bad behavior In [PHP 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eph2:11) the statement πᾶσα γλῶσσα ἐξομολογήσηται ὅτι κύριος Ἰησοῦς Χριστός (‘that everyone may confess that Jesus Christ is Lord’) means simply to acknowledge a fact publicly, and in this instance there is no implication of previous bad behavior.\|In translating [MAT 3:6](https://ref.ly/Matt3:6) in some languages, it may be useful to restructure the expression somewhat, for example, ‘they admitted to people that they had sinned’ or ‘they admitted publicly to God ...’

[MAT 3:6](https://ref.ly/Matt3:6) · [MRK 1:5](https://ref.ly/Matt1:5) · [ACT 19:18](https://ref.ly/John19:18) · [PHP 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eph2:11) · [JAS 5:16](https://ref.ly/Heb5:16)

33\.351 · Communication · Thanks

to thank, to give thanks to to acknowledge one’s thankfulness, restricted in NT usage to contexts in which God is the one being thanked

[MAT 11:25](https://ref.ly/Matt11:25) · [LUK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21)

33\.359 · Communication · Praise

to praise to express praise or honor, with a possible implication of acknowledging the nature of someone or something (occurring in the NT only in quotations from the Septuagint) This meaning of ἐξομολογέομαι is almost guaranteed by the parallelism of the second line of the quotation in [ROM 15:9](https://ref.ly/Acts15:9), but for another interpretation of [ROM 15:9](https://ref.ly/Acts15:9), see [33\.274](#33.274).

[LUK 22:6](https://ref.ly/Mark22:6) · [ROM 14:11](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11)1 · [ROM 15:9](https://ref.ly/Acts15:9)

1 ROM 14:11 It is also possible that ἐξομολογέομαι in [ROM 14:11](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11) may mean ‘to praise,’ but many scholars assume that καὶ πᾶσα γλῶσσα ἐξομολογήσεται τῷ θεῷ should be understood in the sense of ‘and everyone will confess God’ (see [33\.274](#33.274) and [33\.275](#33.275)).

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 22:6; Romans 14:11; Romans 15:9; Matthew 3:6; Mark 1:5; Acts 19:18; Philippians 2:11; James 5:16; Matthew 11:25; Luke 10:21


## ἐξορκίζω (id: 001858000000000)

ἐξορκίζω
========

ἐξορκίζω (G1844)

ὁρκίζω

33\.467 · Communication · Swear, Put Under Oath, Vow

to put under oath, to insist that one take an oath, to require that one swear to demand that a person take an oath as to the truth of what is said or as to the certainty that one will carry out the request or command1 It is extremely difficult to translate ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω in a literal manner, though in some contexts one can say ‘I put you under oath,’ but in a number of passages the person speaking is imploring and not necessarily in a position to command or insist. Therefore, in a passage such as [MRK 5:7](https://ref.ly/Matt5:7), one may render the meaning idiomatically as ‘for God’s sake, I ask you, do not punish me.’ In [1TH 5:27](https://ref.ly/Col5:27) one may translate ‘in the name of the Lord, I ask you to read this letter to all the Christian brothers,’ and in [MAT 26:63](https://ref.ly/Matt26:63) one may translate ‘in the name of the living God, I charge you, Tell us who you are.’\|In most languages one may translate ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω by simply adding a causative component to terms meaning ‘to swear’ or ‘to take an oath.’ In other words, ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω may be rendered as ‘to cause a person to say under oath.’

[MAT 26:63](https://ref.ly/Matt26:63)

1 It is possible that ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω differ slightly in meaning, but this cannot be determined on the basis of available contexts.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 26:63

## ἐξουδενέω (id: 001861000000000)

ἐξουδενέω
=========

ἐξουδενέω (G1847)

88\.133 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Treat Badly

to ill\-treat, to ill\-treat and look down upon to ill\-treat someone with contempt Because of the complex nature of the meaning of ἐξουδενέω, it is often possible to translate the term with a semantic doublet, for example, ‘to look down upon and to ill\-treat.’ This double meaning can be readily derived from the literal rendering ‘to regard as nothing.’

[MRK 9:12](https://ref.ly/Matt9:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 9:12

37\.13 · Control, Rule · Control, Restrain

control a state of control over someone or something

[ACT 5:4](https://ref.ly/John5:4) · [ACT 26:18](https://ref.ly/John26:18) · [1CO 7:37](https://ref.ly/Rom7:37) · [REV 14:18](https://ref.ly/Jude14:18) · [REV 16:9](https://ref.ly/Jude16:9)

76\.12 · Power, Force

power the power to do something, with or without an added implication of authority It is also possible to understand ἐξουσία in [MRK 3:15](https://ref.ly/Matt3:15) as meaning ‘authority,’ and in a number of instances it is difficult to determine whether the focus is upon the power which an individual has or a granted authority to do something which naturally implies strength or power (see [37\.35](#37.35) \- [37\.38](#37.38)).

[MRK 3:15](https://ref.ly/Matt3:15) · [MRK 6:7](https://ref.ly/Matt6:7) · [LUK 4:6](https://ref.ly/Mark4:6) · [LUK 22:53](https://ref.ly/Mark22:53) · [JHN 5:27](https://ref.ly/Luke5:27) · [ACT 8:19](https://ref.ly/John8:19) · [1CO 15:24](https://ref.ly/Rom15:24) · [HEB 13:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm13:10) · [REV 9:3](https://ref.ly/Jude9:3) · [REV 9:3](https://ref.ly/Jude9:3) · [REV 9:10](https://ref.ly/Jude9:10) · [REV 9:19](https://ref.ly/Jude9:19) · [REV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Jude20:6)

12\.44 · Supernatural Beings and Powers · Supernatural Powers

power, authority, lordship, ruler, wicked force (title for supernatural forces and powers) a supernatural power having some particular role in controlling the destiny and activities of human beings Some scholars have believed that it is possible to reconstruct at least in part some of the hierarchy represented by these various supernatural forces and powers, on the basis of the neoplatonic system of nine such powers arranged in three orders of three each. NT terminology and usage does not, however, lend itself to such a classification, and it is difficult, if not impossible, to determine what are the significant differences between these supernatural powers and forces.\|In the case of [EPH 1:21](https://ref.ly/Gal1:21) some scholars apply the terms in question both to the supernatural and to the human sphere, thus emphasizing the totality of Christ’s rule. This has a considerable measure of justification in view of the concepts held by persons in the ancient world, since various earthly powers were supposed to reflect corresponding supernatural powers.\|For the most part, translators have endeavored to render the various terms in somewhat different ways, for example, ἐξουσία as ‘authority’; δύναμις as ‘power’; κυριότης as ‘ruler’; and phrases such as τὰ πνευματικὰ τῆς πονηρίας ἐν τοῖς ἐπουρανίοις as ‘wicked spiritual forces in the heavenly world.’ In a number of languages, however, there are simply not enough terms to designate various kinds of powers, authorities, and forces which could be employed in speaking of such supernatural impersonal powers. Accordingly, some translators have simply used a phrase such as ‘any and all kinds of supernatural powers.’ Where it is possible to make certain lexical distinctions between terms and phrases, this, of course, should be done, but in a number of contexts this may be impossible, and accordingly, some explanation in a marginal note may be required if the reader is to understand satisfactorily the intent of the passages in question.

[1CO 15:24](https://ref.ly/Rom15:24) · [EPH 1:21](https://ref.ly/Gal1:21) · [EPH 2:2](https://ref.ly/Gal2:2) · [EPH 3:10](https://ref.ly/Gal3:10) · [EPH 6:12](https://ref.ly/Gal6:12) · [COL 1:16](https://ref.ly/Phil1:16) · [COL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Phil2:10) · [COL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Phil2:15) · [1PE 3:22](https://ref.ly/Jas3:22)

30\.122 · Think · To Distinguish, To Evaluate, To Judge

right, freedom of choice, freedom of action, power to evaluate the right to judge on the basis of having the potential to evaluate

body, physical form (an idiom, literally: the outside person) the physical nature or aspect of a person The phrase ὁ ἔξω ἄνθρωπος is to be understood in direct contrast with ὁ ἔσω (ἄνθρωπος) in the same verse, which refers to the psychological or spiritual nature of human personality (see [26\.1](#26.1)).

## ἔξωθεν (id: 001870000000000)

ἔξωθεν
======

ἔξωθεν (G1855)

prep

84\.15 · Spacial Extensions · Extension From a Source

from outside extension from a source which is outside

[MRK 7:15](https://ref.ly/Matt7:15) · [MRK 7:18](https://ref.ly/Matt7:18)

83\.20 · Spacial Positions · Around, About, Outside

outside, apart from a position not contained within a particular area

[2CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:5) · [REV 11:2](https://ref.ly/Jude11:2) · [REV 11:2](https://ref.ly/Jude11:2) · [REV 14:20](https://ref.ly/Jude14:20)

83\.21 · Spacial Positions · Around, About, Outside

on the outside, outside of the outside surface of an object The manner in which ἔξωθεν\[c], ἐκτός\[b], and ὄπισθεν\[c] may be rendered in particular contexts depends very largely upon what is being spoken about. For example, ἔξωθεν in [1PE 3:3](https://ref.ly/Jas3:3) refers to the outward aids employed in making oneself beautiful, but instead of speaking of such objects as being on the surface or on the outside, it may be necessary to speak of those things which one puts on the body or what one wears.

[MAT 23:25](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25) · [MAT 23:27](https://ref.ly/Matt23:27) · [MAT 23:28](https://ref.ly/Matt23:28) · [LUK 11:39](https://ref.ly/Mark11:39) · [LUK 11:40](https://ref.ly/Mark11:40) · [1PE 3:3](https://ref.ly/Jas3:3)

11\.10 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · General

**Collocation:** ὁ ἔξωθεν

outsider (a figurative extension of meaning of ἔξωθεν\[a] ‘from outside,’ [83\.20](#83.20)) a person who is not a member of a particular in\-group In a number of languages it may be appropriate to render [1TI 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:7) as ‘he should be a man who is respected by those who are not believers’ or ‘... by those who are not followers of Christ.’

[1TI 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 7:15; Mark 7:18; 2 Corinthians 7:5; Revelation 11:2; Revelation 14:20; Matthew 23:25; Matthew 23:27; Matthew 23:28; Luke 11:39; Luke 11:40; 1 Peter 3:3; 1 Timothy 3:7

adj

outer darkness, darkness outside (an idiom, literally: the outer darkness) a place or region which is both dark and removed (presumably from the abode of the righteous) and serving as the abode of evil spirits and devils In a number of languages this expression in [MAT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Matt8:12) must be rendered as ‘they will be thrown outside where it is dark.’

## ἑορτή (id: 001875000000000)

ἑορτή
=====

ἑορτή (G1859)

noun · ἑορτάζω

51\.2 · Festivals

festival, feast, celebration the events associated with the celebration of a festival or feast Though various celebrations of the Jewish religious year have traditionally been called ‘feasts,’ it would be wrong to use a term which would suggest gluttony or wasteful abundance of food. The emphasis should be upon the ceremonial character of the eating rather than upon the extent of what was eaten. Therefore, it may be better in a number of languages to use terms referring to ‘celebrations’ or ‘festivals,’ since the festivities of such gatherings were far more important than the feasting.

[MAT 26:5](https://ref.ly/Matt26:5) · [MAT 27:15](https://ref.ly/Matt27:15) · [MRK 14:2](https://ref.ly/Matt14:2) · [MRK 15:6](https://ref.ly/Matt15:6) · [LUK 2:41](https://ref.ly/Mark2:41) · [LUK 2:42](https://ref.ly/Mark2:42) · [LUK 22:1](https://ref.ly/Mark22:1) · [JHN 2:23](https://ref.ly/Luke2:23) · [JHN 4:45](https://ref.ly/Luke4:45) · [JHN 4:45](https://ref.ly/Luke4:45) · [JHN 5:1](https://ref.ly/Luke5:1) · [JHN 6:4](https://ref.ly/Luke6:4) · [JHN 7:2](https://ref.ly/Luke7:2) · [JHN 7:8](https://ref.ly/Luke7:8) · [JHN 7:8](https://ref.ly/Luke7:8) · [JHN 7:10](https://ref.ly/Luke7:10) · [JHN 7:11](https://ref.ly/Luke7:11) · [JHN 7:14](https://ref.ly/Luke7:14) · [JHN 7:37](https://ref.ly/Luke7:37) · [JHN 11:56](https://ref.ly/Luke11:56) · [JHN 12:12](https://ref.ly/Luke12:12) · [JHN 12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:20) · [JHN 13:1](https://ref.ly/Luke13:1) · [JHN 13:29](https://ref.ly/Luke13:29) · [COL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Phil2:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 26:5; Matthew 27:15; Mark 14:2; Mark 15:6; Luke 2:41; Luke 2:42; Luke 22:1; John 2:23; John 4:45; John 5:1; John 6:4; John 7:2; John 7:8; John 7:10; John 7:11; John 7:14; John 7:37; John 11:56; John 12:12; John 12:20; John 13:1; John 13:29; Colossians 2:16

33\.280 · Communication · Agree

agreement, approval the content of one’s agreement or approval In ἐπαγγελία\[b] the focus is upon the communication of agreement or approval. One may, therefore, translate 'and are waiting for your approval ́ in [ACT 23:21](https://ref.ly/John23:21) as 'and are waiting for a message from you concerning your agreement ́ or 'and are waiting for word from you as to your approval. ́

[ACT 23:21](https://ref.ly/John23:21) · [HEB 11:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:9) · [HEB 11:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:9)

1 The meaning of ἐπάγγελμα and ἐπαγγελία\[a] focuses primarily upon the content of what is promised, that is to say, what God or someone will do, but the fact of promising is always a relevant element.

## ἐπάγω (id: 001879000000000)

ἐπάγω
=====

ἐπάγω (G1863)

ἄγω

13\.9 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · State

to cause to be, to make to be, to make, to result in, to bring upon, to bring about to cause a state to be1

[ACT 5:28](https://ref.ly/John5:28) · [2PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:1) · [2PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5)

1 Any meaning involving ‘to cause to be’ implies some change in state, and therefore one could classify these meanings under Subdomain B Change of State in Domain 13 Be, Become, Exist, Happen. However, the focus seems to be primarily upon the changed state rather than upon the process of moving from one state to another. The fact that a change of state has taken place seems to be semantically incidental.

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 5:28; 2 Peter 2:1; 2 Peter 2:5

## ἐπαινέω (id: 001883000000000)

ἐπαινέω
=======

ἐπαινέω (G1867)

αἰνέω

33\.354 · Communication · Praise

to praise, praise to speak of the excellence of a person, object, or event In a number of languages praise can only be expressed by direct discourse, and this requires some content which provides the basis for praise. For example, in [LUK 2:13](https://ref.ly/Mark2:13) instead of a literal rendering of ‘praising God,’ it may be necessary to have ‘they said, God is wonderful,’ and in [LUK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Mark16:8) it may be necessary to translate ‘the master of this dishonest manager said, You are remarkable for having done such a clever thing.’

[LUK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Mark16:8) · [ROM 15:11](https://ref.ly/Acts15:11) · [1CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/Rom11:2) · [1CO 11:17](https://ref.ly/Rom11:17) · [1CO 11:22](https://ref.ly/Rom11:22) · [1CO 11:22](https://ref.ly/Rom11:22)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 16:8; Romans 15:11; 1 Corinthians 11:2; 1 Corinthians 11:17; 1 Corinthians 11:22

to praise, praise to speak of the excellence of a person, object, or event In a number of languages praise can only be expressed by direct discourse, and this requires some content which provides the basis for praise. ## ἐπαίρω (id: 001885000000000)

ἐπαίρω
======

ἐπαίρω (G1869) — ἐπαίρομαι

αἴρω

15\.105 · Linear Movement · Come/Go Up, Ascend

to raise to cause to move up In a number of languages one cannot speak of ‘raising hands,’ since this would imply something that was detached from the body. It may therefore be necessary to say ‘extending hands upwards.’

[LUK 6:20](https://ref.ly/Mark6:20) · [LUK 24:50](https://ref.ly/Mark24:50) · [ACT 1:9](https://ref.ly/John1:9) · [ACT 27:40](https://ref.ly/John27:40) · [1TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:8)

25\.160 · Attitudes and Emotions · Courage, Boldness

**Collocation:** ἐπαίρω τὴν κεφαλήν

to have courage, to lift the head (an idiom, literally: to raise up the head) to demonstrate courage in the face of danger or adversity A literal rendering of ἐπαίρω τὴν κεφαλήν ‘to lift up the head’ can be misleading in some languages, since the expression may be an idiom meaning ‘to be proud’ or ‘to try to take precedence over others.’ A more satisfactory equivalent in some languages may be ‘to look up.’

[LUK 21:28](https://ref.ly/Mark21:28)

39\.3 · Hostility, Strife · Opposition, Hostility

**Collocation:** ἐπαίρω τὴν πτέρναν

to turn against, to oppose (an idiom, literally: to lift one’s heel against) to oppose someone by turning against such a person, possibly with focus upon the initial aspect of becoming opposed to In a number of languages a convenient way of rendering ‘to turn against’ may be ‘to become an enemy of.’

[JHN 13:18](https://ref.ly/Luke13:18)

24\.34 · Sensory Events and States · See

**Collocation:** ἐπαίρω τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς

to notice, to look (an idiom, literally: to lift up the eyes) to direct one’s attention to something by looking closely at

[MAT 17:8](https://ref.ly/Matt17:8) · [LUK 16:23](https://ref.ly/Mark16:23) · [LUK 18:13](https://ref.ly/Mark18:13) · [JHN 4:35](https://ref.ly/Luke4:35) · [JHN 6:5](https://ref.ly/Luke6:5) · [JHN 17:1](https://ref.ly/Luke17:1)

33\.78 · Communication · Speak, Talk

**Collocation:** ἐπαίρω φωνήν

to raise the voice, to cry out, to speak loudly (an idiom, literally: to raise the voice) to increase the volume with which one speaks, so as to overcome existing noise or the speech of someone else

[LUK 11:27](https://ref.ly/Mark11:27) · [ACT 2:14](https://ref.ly/John2:14) · [ACT 14:11](https://ref.ly/John14:11) · [ACT 22:22](https://ref.ly/John22:22)

39\.38 · Hostility, Strife · Rebellion

to rise up against (a figurative extension of meaning of ἐπαίρομαι ‘to rise up,’ not occurring in the NT) to rise up in opposition against

[2CO 10:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:5)

88\.212 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Arrogance, Haughtiness, Pride

to be haughty, to be arrogant to become haughty in one’s attitude toward others

[2CO 11:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20)

============

31\.83 · Hold a View, Believe, Trust · Trust, Rely

to rely on, to depend on, to put one’s trust in (a figurative extension of meaning of ἐπαναπαύομαι ‘to remain upon something or someone,’ not occurring in the NT) to continue to believe firmly in someone or something and thus to rely upon The concept of dependence or reliance upon something may be expressed in a number of different ways. For example, the phrase ἐπαναπαύω νόμῳ in [ROM 2:17](https://ref.ly/Acts2:17) may be rendered in some languages as ‘you believe that all you need is the Law’ or ‘you believe the Law will help you in every way.’

## ἔπαυλις (id: 001903000000000)

ἔπαυλις
=======

ἔπαυλις (G1886)

noun · αὐλίζομαι

7\.4 · Constructions · Buildings

homestead, house, residence property in which a person was expected to reside, either as the result of ownership or legal contract In the NT ἔπαυλις occurs only in [ACT 1:20](https://ref.ly/John1:20) as a quotation of [PSA 69:25](https://ref.ly/Ps69:25). The immediate reference is to a person’s traditional residence, but the entire passage must be interpreted figuratively as a reference to a person’s expected position or place of service. The phrase γενηθήτω ἡ ἔπαυλις αὐτοῦ ἔρημος may be rendered as ‘may his house become empty’ or ‘may his home be deserted.’

[ACT 1:20](https://ref.ly/John1:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 1:20

## ἐπαφρίζω (id: 001906000000000)

ἐπαφρίζω
========

ἀφρίζω

14\.29 · Physical Events and States · Events Involving Liquids and Dry Masses

to cause to foam up to cause foam to occur on the surface of a liquid ἐπαφρίζω occurs in the NT only in [JUD 1:13](https://ref.ly/3John1:13) in a figurative context. It probably differs from ἀφρίζω ([14\.28](#14.28)) in emphasizing the greater degree or extent of foam on the surface.\|In some languages the type of foam which would be referred to in [JUD 1:13](https://ref.ly/3John1:13) is called ‘the saliva of the waves’ or ‘the whiteness of the waves.’ Since foaming at the mouth is also regarded as a sign of anger, it is possible to speak in some languages of the foam of the waves as being ‘the anger of the waves.’ In other languages one may speak simply of ‘the whiteness of the waves.’

[JUD 1:13](https://ref.ly/3John1:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Jude 1:13

## ἐπεκτείνομαι (id: 001918000000000)

ἐπεκτείνομαι
============

ἐπεκτείνομαι (G1901)

τείνω

16\.20 · Non\-Linear Movement

to reach out, to stretch toward to reach out or stretch out toward some goal In [PHP 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eph3:13) ἐπεκτείνομαι is used figuratively to suggest intense effort as well as firm purpose.

[PHP 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eph3:13)1

13\.20 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · State

to seek strongly to, to try hard to to attempt energetically to attain a state or condition

[PHP 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eph3:13)1

1 PHP 3:13 There is no evidence that the figurative meaning of ἐπεκτείνομαι had become conventionalized by NT times. Accordingly, in [PHP 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eph3:13) the occurrence of ἐπεκτείνομαι involves figurative usage rather than figurative meaning. It is rare that one can use a word meaning ‘to stretch out’ or ‘to reach toward’ in this context of [PHP 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eph3:13). It may be far better to use an expression such as ‘to earnestly seek those things that are ahead’ or ‘to do one’s best to reach what is ahead’ or ‘to strongly desire to experience what is ahead of me’ or ‘… what lies in the future.’

* **Associated Passages:** Philippians 3:13

ἔρχομαι

15\.83 · Linear Movement · Come, Come To, Arrive

to come to, to arrive to move to or on to, generally with the implication of having arrived In the rendering of [LUK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Mark1:35) it is important to avoid the kind of literal translation which might suggest sexual relations.

[LUK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Mark1:35) · [LUK 21:26](https://ref.ly/Mark21:26) · [ACT 14:19](https://ref.ly/John14:19) · [EPH 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7)

39\.47 · Hostility, Strife · Attack

to attack, to assault to use sudden physical force against someone as the outgrowth of a hostile attitude In a number of languages the equivalent of ‘to attack’ is simply ‘to start fighting against.’ In some languages, however, the equivalent is ‘to jump on’ or ‘to grab to do harm to.’

[LUK 11:22](https://ref.ly/Mark11:22)

13\.119 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Happen

to happen to, to come upon, to overtake to happen to someone or something, with the implication of an event which is undesirable

[ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/John1:8) · [ACT 8:24](https://ref.ly/John8:24) · [ACT 13:40](https://ref.ly/John13:40) · [JAS 5:1](https://ref.ly/Heb5:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 1:35; Luke 21:26; Acts 14:19; Ephesians 2:7; Luke 11:22; Acts 1:8; Acts 8:24; Acts 13:40; James 5:1


## ἐπερωτάω (id: 001922000000000)

ἐπερωτάω
========

ἐπερωτάω (G1905)

ἐρωτάω

33\.180 · Communication · Question, Answer

to ask, to ask a question to ask for information1 In a number of languages a term such as ἐρωτάω\[a] or ἐπερωτάω\[a] implies an introduction of direct discourse, so that in [LUK 9:45](https://ref.ly/Mark9:45), for example, it may be necessary to translate ‘they were afraid to ask him, What do you mean by what you said?’

[MAT 12:10](https://ref.ly/Matt12:10) · [MAT 17:10](https://ref.ly/Matt17:10) · [MAT 22:23](https://ref.ly/Matt22:23) · [MAT 22:35](https://ref.ly/Matt22:35) · [MAT 22:41](https://ref.ly/Matt22:41) · [MAT 22:46](https://ref.ly/Matt22:46) · [MRK 5:9](https://ref.ly/Matt5:9) · [MRK 7:5](https://ref.ly/Matt7:5) · [MRK 7:17](https://ref.ly/Matt7:17) · [MRK 8:23](https://ref.ly/Matt8:23) · [MRK 8:27](https://ref.ly/Matt8:27) · [MRK 8:29](https://ref.ly/Matt8:29) · [MRK 9:11](https://ref.ly/Matt9:11) · [MRK 9:16](https://ref.ly/Matt9:16) · [MRK 9:21](https://ref.ly/Matt9:21) · [MRK 9:28](https://ref.ly/Matt9:28) · [MRK 9:32](https://ref.ly/Matt9:32) · [MRK 9:33](https://ref.ly/Matt9:33) · [MRK 10:2](https://ref.ly/Matt10:2) · [MRK 10:10](https://ref.ly/Matt10:10) · [MRK 10:17](https://ref.ly/Matt10:17) · [MRK 11:29](https://ref.ly/Matt11:29) · [MRK 12:18](https://ref.ly/Matt12:18) · [MRK 12:28](https://ref.ly/Matt12:28) · [MRK 12:34](https://ref.ly/Matt12:34) · [MRK 13:3](https://ref.ly/Matt13:3) · [MRK 15:44](https://ref.ly/Matt15:44) · [LUK 2:46](https://ref.ly/Mark2:46) · [LUK 3:10](https://ref.ly/Mark3:10) · [LUK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14) · [LUK 6:9](https://ref.ly/Mark6:9) · [LUK 8:9](https://ref.ly/Mark8:9) · [LUK 8:30](https://ref.ly/Mark8:30) · [LUK 9:18](https://ref.ly/Mark9:18) · [LUK 17:20](https://ref.ly/Mark17:20) · [LUK 18:18](https://ref.ly/Mark18:18) · [LUK 18:40](https://ref.ly/Mark18:40) · [LUK 20:21](https://ref.ly/Mark20:21) · [LUK 20:27](https://ref.ly/Mark20:27) · [LUK 20:40](https://ref.ly/Mark20:40) · [LUK 21:7](https://ref.ly/Mark21:7) · [LUK 22:64](https://ref.ly/Mark22:64) · [LUK 23:6](https://ref.ly/Mark23:6) · [JHN 18:7](https://ref.ly/Luke18:7) · [ACT 23:34](https://ref.ly/John23:34) · [ROM 10:20](https://ref.ly/Acts10:20) · [1CO 14:35](https://ref.ly/Rom14:35)

33\.161 · Communication · Ask For, Request

to ask for, to request to ask for, usually with the implication of an underlying question2

[MAT 16:1](https://ref.ly/Matt16:1)

56\.14 · Courts and Legal Procedures · Judicial Hearing, Inquiry

to question, to interrogate, to try to learn to attempt in a legal or semi\-legal procedure to know the truth about a matter, normally by interrogation

[MAT 27:11](https://ref.ly/Matt27:11) · [MRK 14:60](https://ref.ly/Matt14:60) · [MRK 14:61](https://ref.ly/Matt14:61) · [MRK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Matt15:2) · [MRK 15:4](https://ref.ly/Matt15:4) · [LUK 23:9](https://ref.ly/Mark23:9) · [JHN 9:23](https://ref.ly/Luke9:23) · [ACT 5:27](https://ref.ly/John5:27)

1 There may be some slight difference of meaning between ἐρωτάω\[a] and ἐπερωτάω\[a], but this cannot be determined from existing contexts. 2 ἐπερωτάω\[b] may be somewhat more intensive in meaning than ἐρωτάω\[b], but both seem to be somewhat weaker in urgency than αἰτέω and παραιτέομαι\[a] ([33\.163](#33.163)).

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 12:10; Matthew 17:10; Matthew 22:23; Matthew 22:35; Matthew 22:41; Matthew 22:46; Mark 5:9; Mark 7:5; Mark 7:17; Mark 8:23; Mark 8:27; Mark 8:29; Mark 9:11; Mark 9:16; Mark 9:21; Mark 9:28; Mark 9:32; Mark 9:33; Mark 10:2; Mark 10:10; Mark 10:17; Mark 11:29; Mark 12:18; Mark 12:28; Mark 12:34; Mark 13:3; Mark 15:44; Luke 2:46; Luke 3:10; Luke 3:14; Luke 6:9; Luke 8:9; Luke 8:30; Luke 9:18; Luke 17:20; Luke 18:18; Luke 18:40; Luke 20:21; Luke 20:27; Luke 20:40; Luke 21:7; Luke 22:64; Luke 23:6; John 18:7; Acts 23:34; Romans 10:20; 1 Corinthians 14:35; Matthew 16:1; Matthew 27:11; Mark 14:60; Mark 14:61; Mark 15:2; Mark 15:4; Luke 23:9; John 9:23; Acts 5:27


## ἐπερώτημα (id: 001923000000000)

ἐπερώτημα
=========

ἐπερώτημα (G1906)

noun · ἐρωτάω

33\.162 · Communication · Ask For, Request

request, appeal (derivative of ἐπερωτάω\[b] ‘to ask for,’ [33\.161](#33.161)) that which is asked for It is also possible to interpret ἐπερώτημα in [1PE 3:21](https://ref.ly/Jas3:21) as meaning ‘pledge’ or ‘promise,’ in which case it may be classified under [33\.288](#33.288). Accordingly, the phrase συνειδήσεως ἀγαθῆς ἐπερώτημα εἰς θεόν may be rendered as ‘a promise made to God from a good conscience.’

[1PE 3:21](https://ref.ly/Jas3:21)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Peter 3:21


## ἐπέχω (id: 001924000000000)

ἐπέχω
=====

ἐπέχω (G1907)

ἔχω

27\.59 · Learn · Be Ready To Learn, Pay Attention

to pay attention to, to keep on the lookout for, to be alert for, to be on one’s guard against to be in a continuous state of readiness to learn of any future danger, need, or error, and to respond appropriately

[1TI 4:16](https://ref.ly/2Thess4:16)

31\.47 · Hold a View, Believe, Trust · Believe To Be True

to hold firmly to, to continue to believe to hold firmly to a particular belief The equivalent of ἐπέχω\[b] and προσέχω\[c] may be simply ‘to continue to strongly believe’ or ‘to not give up at all to believe.’\|It is also possible to understand ἐπέχω in [PHP 2:16](https://ref.ly/Eph2:16) as meaning ‘to offer.’

[PHP 2:16](https://ref.ly/Eph2:16)

24\.33 · Sensory Events and States · See

to notice, to watch to direct one’s attention to a particular object or event

[LUK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Mark14:7) · [ACT 3:5](https://ref.ly/John3:5)

85\.59 · Existence in Space · Remain, Stay

to stay on, to remain to stay or remain in a place beyond some point of time

[ACT 19:22](https://ref.ly/John19:22)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Timothy 4:16; Philippians 2:16; Luke 14:7; Acts 3:5; Acts 19:22


## ἐπηρεάζω (id: 001925000000000)

ἐπηρεάζω
========

ἐπηρεάζω (G1908)

88\.129 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Treat Badly

to mistreat to mistreat, with the implication of threats and abuse

[LUK 6:28](https://ref.ly/Mark6:28) · [1PE 3:16](https://ref.ly/Jas3:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 6:28; 1 Peter 3:16
