67\.33 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

when, at the time of a marker of a point of time which is simultaneous to or overlaps with another point of time3

[MAT 1:11](https://ref.ly/Matt1:11) · [MRK 2:26](https://ref.ly/Matt2:26) · [LUK 10:35](https://ref.ly/Mark10:35) · [JHN 4:27](https://ref.ly/Luke4:27) · [ACT 3:1](https://ref.ly/John3:1) · [ACT 11:10](https://ref.ly/John11:10) · [ACT 11:28](https://ref.ly/John11:28) · [EPH 4:26](https://ref.ly/Gal4:26) · [PHP 1:3](https://ref.ly/Eph1:3) · [1TH 1:2](https://ref.ly/Col1:2) · [HEB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:2) · [HEB 9:26](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:26) · [1PE 1:20](https://ref.ly/Jas1:20)

67\.136 · Time · Duration of Time with Reference to Some Unit of Time: During, In, While, Throughout

during, in the course of, within, for a marker of the extent of time within a unit

[MAT 9:15](https://ref.ly/Matt9:15) · [LUK 3:2](https://ref.ly/Mark3:2) · [LUK 4:25](https://ref.ly/Mark4:25) · [LUK 4:27](https://ref.ly/Mark4:27) · [LUK 18:4](https://ref.ly/Mark18:4) · [ACT 4:5](https://ref.ly/John4:5) · [ACT 13:31](https://ref.ly/John13:31) · [ACT 16:18](https://ref.ly/John16:18) · [ACT 17:2](https://ref.ly/John17:2) · [ACT 18:20](https://ref.ly/John18:20) · [ACT 19:8](https://ref.ly/John19:8) · [ACT 19:10](https://ref.ly/John19:10) · [ACT 19:34](https://ref.ly/John19:34) · [ACT 27:20](https://ref.ly/John27:20) · [ROM 1:10](https://ref.ly/Acts1:10) · [ROM 7:1](https://ref.ly/Acts7:1) · [1CO 7:39](https://ref.ly/Rom7:39) · [2CO 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:4) · [2CO 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:14) · [2CO 7:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:4) · [GAL 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:1) · [EPH 1:16](https://ref.ly/Gal1:16) · [1TH 3:7](https://ref.ly/Col3:7) · [PHM 1:4](https://ref.ly/Titus1:4) · [HEB 9:15](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:15) · [HEB 11:30](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:30)

90\.5 · Case · Agent, Personal or Nonpersonal, Causative or Immediate, Direct or Indirect

by (with the accusative) a marker of agent, with the added implication of effect upon the agent It is possible that in [2TH 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:10) there is a double relation marked by ἐπί, for it may be construed as marking the affected participant with τὸ μαρτύριον and at the same time the agent participant with ἐπιστεύθη. It is this double role which may account for the use of ἐπί rather than ὑπό, which would clearly mark a relation with ἐπιστεύθη, or πρός, which would indicate clearly a relation with μαρτύριον.

[2TH 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:10)

67\.58 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

**Collocation:** ἐπὶ θύραις

soon, very soon (an idiom, literally: at the doors) a point of time subsequent to another point of time and indicating imminence, that is to say, the subsequent event is regarded as almost begun

[MAT 24:33](https://ref.ly/Matt24:33) · [MRK 13:29](https://ref.ly/Matt13:29)

67\.89 · Time · Duration of Time without Reference to Points or Units of Time: Time, Spend Time, Always, Eternal, Old, Immediately, Young

**Collocation:** ἐπὶ πολύ

long, long time, for some time pertaining to a relatively long duration of time

[ACT 20:9](https://ref.ly/John20:9) · [ACT 28:6](https://ref.ly/John28:6)

37\.102 · Control, Rule · Assign to a Role or Function

**Collocation:** ἐπὶ τὴν κεφαλήν

upon someone’s head, responsibility (an idiom, literally: upon someone’s head) to accept responsibility for some action, often with the implication of blame This statement in [ACT 18:6](https://ref.ly/John18:6) is no doubt an adage meaning that anything bad which may happen to a person, including one’s death, will be one’s own responsibility, and therefore no one else is to blame.\|It is also possible to classify this idiom in Domain 90D. Note that in [37\.102](#37.102) the focus is on the assignment of responsibility.

[ACT 18:6](https://ref.ly/John18:6)

37\.44 · Control, Rule · Exercise Authority

**Collocation:** ἐπὶ τῆς Μωϋσέως καθέδρας καθίζω

to be an authority concerning the Law of Moses (an idiom, literally: to sit upon the seat of Moses) to have the capacity to interpret the Law of Moses with authority4

[MAT 23:2](https://ref.ly/Matt23:2)

28\.64 · Know · Able To Be Known

**Collocation:** ἐπὶ τῶν δωμάτων

in public, publicly (an idiom, literally: on the housetops) pertaining to being widely known in view of the events in question having taken place in public5

[MAT 10:27](https://ref.ly/Matt10:27) · [LUK 12:3](https://ref.ly/Mark12:3)

67\.91 · Time · Duration of Time without Reference to Points or Units of Time: Time, Spend Time, Always, Eternal, Old, Immediately, Young

**Collocation:** ἐφ’ ἱκανόν

during a considerable period of time, for a long time (an idiom, literally: upon enough) duration of time for a considerable period, with the focus upon sufficiency of time

[ACT 20:11](https://ref.ly/John20:11) · [ACT 20:11](https://ref.ly/John20:11)

1 There are no doubt certain subtle distinctions in the meanings of the terms in [83\.9](#83.9). For example, πρός\[d] may suggest some kind of interaction or response and παρά\[b] may focus upon association, but it is difficult, if not impossible, on the basis of existing contexts to define precisely the differences in meaning. 2 In the meaning of extension toward a goal, εἰς\[a] ([84\.16](#84.16)) and ἐπί\[e] ([84\.17](#84.17)) overlap considerably. It is, of course, possible to argue that ἐπί implies being on or at a point, while εἰς defines entering into an area related to some point, but such a distinction would seem to be arbitrary. 3 The difference in meaning between the set κατά\[g], ἐπί\[s], ἐν\[s], ἐκ\[k], and καθώς\[d] and the set πότε, ποτέ\[b], ὁπότε, ὅτε\[a], and ὅταν\[b] ([67\.30](#67.30)) is largely a matter of class meanings, but there may be certain subtle rhetorical differences as well. 4 Since the concept of authority expresses only the manner in which the interpretation of the Law takes place, one may be justified in classifying this idiom under Domain 33 Communication. 5 It is very possible that the two idioms in [28\.64](#28.64) differ somewhat in meaning, or at least in connotation, but it is difficult, if not impossible, to show this from existing contexts.

## ἐπιβαίνω (id: 001927000000000)

ἐπιβαίνω
========

ἐπιβαίνω (G1910)

βαίνω

15\.83 · Linear Movement · Come, Come To, Arrive

to come to, to arrive to move to or on to, generally with the implication of having arrived

[ACT 20:18](https://ref.ly/John20:18) · [ACT 25:1](https://ref.ly/John25:1)

15\.97 · Linear Movement · Come/Go Onto

to embark, to go onto, to mount to move up onto some object In a high percentage of languages it is necessary to use quite distinct terms in speaking of boarding a ship in contrast with mounting a donkey. A wrong choice in such a context can be seriously misleading.

[MAT 21:5](https://ref.ly/Matt21:5) · [ACT 21:2](https://ref.ly/John21:2) · [ACT 21:4](https://ref.ly/John21:4) · [ACT 27:2](https://ref.ly/John27:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 20:18; Acts 25:1; Matthew 21:5; Acts 21:2; Acts 21:4; Acts 27:2


## ἐπιβάλλω (id: 001928000000000)

ἐπιβάλλω
========

ἐπιβάλλω (G1911)

βάλλω

15\.218 · Linear Movement · Throw, Hurl

to throw on to throw something on1

[MRK 11:7](https://ref.ly/Matt11:7)

85\.51 · Existence in Space · Put, Place

to put on, to place on, to lay on to place something on something

[MAT 9:16](https://ref.ly/Matt9:16) · [LUK 5:36](https://ref.ly/Mark5:36) · [1CO 7:35](https://ref.ly/Rom7:35)

14\.21 · Physical Events and States · Events Involving Liquids and Dry Masses

to splash into to strike upon and into (with special reference to the action of waves)

[MRK 4:37](https://ref.ly/Matt4:37)2

57\.3 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Have, Possess, Property, Owner

to belong to to belong to or to come to belong to, with the possible implication of by right or by inheritance

[LUK 15:12](https://ref.ly/Mark15:12)

30\.7 · Think · To Think, Thought

to reflect on, to think about seriously, to think deeply about to give careful consideration to various implications of an issue For another interpretation of ἐπιβάλλω in [MRK 14:72](https://ref.ly/Matt14:72), see [68\.5](#68.5).

[MRK 14:72](https://ref.ly/Matt14:72)

68\.5 · Aspect · Begin, Start

to begin to begin an activity, with special emphasis upon the inception or possibly the suddenness with which the event takes place It would also be possible to understand ἐπιβάλλω in [MRK 14:72](https://ref.ly/Matt14:72) as meaning ‘when he thought of it’ or ‘when he reflected on it’ (see [30\.7](#30.7)).

[MRK 14:72](https://ref.ly/Matt14:72)

37\.110 · Control, Rule · Seize, Take into Custody

**Collocation:** ἐπιβάλλω τὰς χεῖρας ἐπί

to seize, to arrest (an idiom, literally: to lay hands on) to take a person into custody for alleged illegal activity

[MAT 26:50](https://ref.ly/Matt26:50) · [MRK 14:46](https://ref.ly/Matt14:46) · [LUK 20:19](https://ref.ly/Mark20:19) · [LUK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Mark21:12) · [JHN 7:30](https://ref.ly/Luke7:30) · [JHN 7:44](https://ref.ly/Luke7:44) · [ACT 4:3](https://ref.ly/John4:3) · [ACT 5:18](https://ref.ly/John5:18) · [ACT 12:1](https://ref.ly/John12:1) · [ACT 21:27](https://ref.ly/John21:27)

68\.6 · Aspect · Begin, Start

**Collocation:** ἐπιβάλλω τὴν χεῖρα ἐπ᾽ ἄροτρον καὶ βλέπω εἰς τὰ ὀπίσω

to start to do something and then to hesitate, to begin but have second thoughts about continuing (a Semitic idiom, literally: one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back) to begin some activity requiring close attention but then to change one’s mind about proceeding It is probably best to regard the phrase ἐπιβαλὼν τὴν χεῖρα ἐπ ̓ ἄροτρον καὶ βλέπων εἰς τὰ ὀπίσω as a part of a traditional saying referring to anyone who has second thoughts about undertaking some new or different enterprise, but the particular manner in which this is adapted to a statement concerning ‘the kingdom of God’ makes its strictly idiomatic structure somewhat doubtful.

[LUK 9:62](https://ref.ly/Mark9:62)3

1 The meanings of ἐπιβάλλω\[a] ([15\.218](#15.218)) and ἐπιρίπτω ([15\.219](#15.219)) parallel to a certain extent the distinction in βάλλω\[a] ([15\.215](#15.215)) and ῥίπτω\[a] ([15\.217](#15.217)) in that βάλλω\[a] suggests a more deliberate action, while ῥίπτω\[a] suggests an action characterized by being quicker, more energetic, and often somewhat more impetuous. 2 MRK 4:37 It would be possible to analyze the meaning of ἐπιβάλλω\[c] as merely a matter of impact, but it is the evident action of the waves, not only in striking the boat but filling it with water, that would seem to justify setting up a separate meaning. 3 LUK 9:62 The phrases ἐπιβάλλω τὰς χεῖρας and ἐπιβάλλω τὴν χεῖρα ἐπί also occur with events, and as such focus attention on the beginning of an event, for example, οὐδεὶς ἐπιβαλὼν τὴν χεῖρα ἐπ᾽ ἄροτρον ‘no one puts his hand to the plow’ or ‘no one begins to plow’ [LUK 9:62](https://ref.ly/Mark9:62). A treatment of this meaning is in [68\.6](#68.6).

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 11:7; Matthew 9:16; Luke 5:36; 1 Corinthians 7:35; Mark 4:37; Luke 15:12; Mark 14:72; Matthew 26:50; Mark 14:46; Luke 20:19; Luke 21:12; John 7:30; John 7:44; Acts 4:3; Acts 5:18; Acts 12:1; Acts 21:27; Luke 9:62


## ἐπιβαρέω (id: 001929000000000)

ἐπιβαρέω
========

ἐπιβαρέω (G1912)

βαρέω

57\.224 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Be a Financial Burden

to burden, to be a financial burden to to be a financial burden to someone by requiring too much support

[2CO 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:5) · [1TH 2:9](https://ref.ly/Col2:9) · [2TH 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:8)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 2:5; 1 Thessalonians 2:9; 2 Thessalonians 3:8


## ἐπιβιβάζω (id: 001930000000000)

ἐπιβιβάζω
=========

ἐπιβιβάζω (G1913)

βιβάζω

15\.98 · Linear Movement · Come/Go Onto

to cause to mount to cause to mount, as in the case of an animal In [LUK 10:34](https://ref.ly/Mark10:34) it is difficult to know whether the causative aspect of the verb includes ‘helping him get on’ or ‘having him get on.’

[LUK 10:34](https://ref.ly/Mark10:34) · [LUK 19:35](https://ref.ly/Mark19:35) · [ACT 23:24](https://ref.ly/John23:24)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 10:34; Luke 19:35; Acts 23:24


## ἐπιβλέπω (id: 001931000000000)

ἐπιβλέπω
========

ἐπιβλέπω (G1914)

βλέπω

24\.12 · Sensory Events and States · See

to notice especially to notice and pay special attention to1 In the case of ἐπιβλέπω in [JAS 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3), there is obviously a considerable measure of cognitive involvement, for not only sight but evaluation is also involved. For another interpretation of ἐπιβλέπω in [JAS 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3), see [87\.17](#87.17).

[JAS 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3)

30\.45 · Think · To Think About, with the Implied Purpose of Responding Appropriately

to take notice of, to consider, to pay attention to, to concern oneself with to take special notice of something, with the implication of concerning oneself Though the terms ἐπιβλέπω, ἐπεῖδον (ἐφοράω), and ὁράω suggest as a result of their central meanings some measure of visual perception, what is involved in these specific contexts is much more than visual perception. The focus is actually upon intellectual activity and concern.\|For another interpretation of ἐπιβλέπω in [LUK 9:38](https://ref.ly/Mark9:38), see [35\.8](#35.8).

[LUK 1:48](https://ref.ly/Mark1:48) · [LUK 9:38](https://ref.ly/Mark9:38)

35\.8 · Help, Care For · Help

to help, to be concerned with, to look upon and help to provide help, with the implication of having taken special notice of For another interpretation of ἐπιβλέπω in [LUK 9:38](https://ref.ly/Mark9:38), see [30\.45](#30.45).

[LUK 9:38](https://ref.ly/Mark9:38)

87\.17 · Status · Honor or Respect in Relation to Status

to pay special respect to, to look upon as being someone to be honored to regard a person as deserving special respect For another interpretation of ἐπιβλέπω in [JAS 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3), see [24\.12](#24.12).

[JAS 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3)

1 In a number of contexts, ἐπιβλέπω involves not only sight but a considerable focus upon an accompanying mental activity, so that one may classify ἐπιβλέπω not only as meaning sight but as indicating paying attention to or even concerning oneself with (see [30\.45](#30.45)).

* **Associated Passages:** James 2:3; Luke 1:48; Luke 9:38


## ἐπίβλημα (id: 001932000000000)

ἐπίβλημα
========

ἐπίβλημα (G1915)

noun · βάλλω

6\.157 · Artifacts · Cloth, Leather, and Objects Made of Such Materials

patch a piece of cloth sewed on clothing to repair a hole or tear The statement in [MAT 9:16](https://ref.ly/Matt9:16) that ‘no one puts a patch of new cloth on an old garment’ seems almost preposterous or incredible in some societies, since people habitually repair old clothing by putting on patches of new cloth, sometimes to the point where it is difficult to determine what was the original fabric. In [MAT 9:16](https://ref.ly/Matt9:16) the phrase ῥάκους ἀγνάφου, often translated ‘new cloth,’ is literally ‘unshrunken cloth,’ in other words, cloth which has not been washed one or more times and thus has been shrunken in the process. In biblical times there was no technique for preshrinking cloth.

[MAT 9:16](https://ref.ly/Matt9:16) · [MRK 2:21](https://ref.ly/Matt2:21) · [LUK 5:36](https://ref.ly/Mark5:36) · [LUK 5:36](https://ref.ly/Mark5:36)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 9:16; Mark 2:21; Luke 5:36

ἐπιγινώσκω
==========

ἐπιγινώσκω (G1921)

γινώσκω

28\.2 · Know · Know

to know about, to know definitely about, knowledge about to possess more or less definite information about, possibly with a degree of thoroughness or competence

[MAT 11:27](https://ref.ly/Matt11:27) · [MAT 11:27](https://ref.ly/Matt11:27) · [MRK 5:30](https://ref.ly/Matt5:30) · [ACT 19:34](https://ref.ly/John19:34) · [ACT 25:10](https://ref.ly/John25:10) · [ACT 27:39](https://ref.ly/John27:39) · [ROM 1:32](https://ref.ly/Acts1:32) · [2CO 6:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9) · [1TI 4:3](https://ref.ly/2Thess4:3)

32\.16 · Understand · Come To Understand

to come to understand, to perceive, to comprehend to come to an understanding as the result of ability to experience and learn1

[MRK 2:8](https://ref.ly/Matt2:8) · [LUK 1:4](https://ref.ly/Mark1:4) · [LUK 1:22](https://ref.ly/Mark1:22) · [LUK 5:22](https://ref.ly/Mark5:22) · [1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12) · [1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12) · [2CO 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:13) · [2CO 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:13) · [2CO 1:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:14) · [2CO 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:5) · [COL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:6)

27\.8 · Learn · Learn

to learn about, to find out about to acquire information, probably in a somewhat more exact or detailed form and perhaps with focus upon what is learned (compare [27\.2](#27.2)) ἐπιγινώσκω\[c] also occurs in legal contexts referring to careful investigation and interrogation, as in [ACT 22:24](https://ref.ly/John22:24) and [ACT 23:28](https://ref.ly/John23:28).2

[LUK 7:37](https://ref.ly/Mark7:37) · [LUK 23:7](https://ref.ly/Mark23:7) · [ACT 9:30](https://ref.ly/John9:30) · [ACT 22:24](https://ref.ly/John22:24) · [ACT 22:29](https://ref.ly/John22:29) · [ACT 23:28](https://ref.ly/John23:28) · [ACT 24:8](https://ref.ly/John24:8) · [ACT 24:11](https://ref.ly/John24:11) · [ACT 28:1](https://ref.ly/John28:1) · [2PE 2:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:21) · [2PE 2:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:21)

27\.61 · Learn · Recognize

to recognize to identify newly acquired information with what had been previously learned or known

[MAT 7:16](https://ref.ly/Matt7:16) · [MAT 7:20](https://ref.ly/Matt7:20) · [MAT 14:35](https://ref.ly/Matt14:35) · [MRK 6:33](https://ref.ly/Matt6:33) · [MRK 6:54](https://ref.ly/Matt6:54) · [LUK 24:16](https://ref.ly/Mark24:16) · [LUK 24:31](https://ref.ly/Mark24:31) · [ACT 3:10](https://ref.ly/John3:10) · [ACT 4:13](https://ref.ly/John4:13) · [ACT 12:14](https://ref.ly/John12:14) · [1CO 14:37](https://ref.ly/Rom14:37)

31\.27 · Hold a View, Believe, Trust · Acknowledge

to acknowledge to indicate that one does know It may also be possible to translate the expression in [1CO 16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:18) as ‘you should give these people due recognition’ in the sense of ‘causing to be recognized’ or ‘causing to be acknowledged.’

[MAT 17:12](https://ref.ly/Matt17:12) · [1CO 16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:18)

1 It is possible that ἐπιγινώσκω\[b] differs somewhat in meaning from γινώσκω\[d] in focusing attention on what is understood or indicating that the process of understanding is somewhat more emphatic, but such a distinction cannot be determined from existing contexts. 2 Though ἐπιγινώσκω does occur in certain legal contexts, it still means essentially to acquire more exact or detailed information, and therefore it does not seem necessary to set up a distinct meaning for ἐπιγινώσκω merely because of its use in such contexts.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 11:27; Mark 5:30; Acts 19:34; Acts 25:10; Acts 27:39; Romans 1:32; 2 Corinthians 6:9; 1 Timothy 4:3; Mark 2:8; Luke 1:4; Luke 1:22; Luke 5:22; 1 Corinthians 13:12; 2 Corinthians 1:13; 2 Corinthians 1:14; 2 Corinthians 13:5; Colossians 1:6; Luke 7:37; Luke 23:7; Acts 9:30; Acts 22:24; Acts 22:29; Acts 23:28; Acts 24:8; Acts 24:11; Acts 28:1; 2 Peter 2:21; Matthew 7:16; Matthew 7:20; Matthew 14:35; Mark 6:33; Mark 6:54; Luke 24:16; Luke 24:31; Acts 3:10; Acts 4:13; Acts 12:14; 1 Corinthians 14:37; Matthew 17:12; 1 Corinthians 16:18


## ἐπίγνωσις (id: 001938000000000)

ἐπίγνωσις
=========

ἐπίγνωσις (G1922)

noun · γινώσκω

28\.2 · Know · Know

knowing about, knowing definitely about, knowledge about possessing more or less definite information about, possibly with a degree of thoroughness or competence It is also possible to render ἐπίγνωσις in [ROM 1:28](https://ref.ly/Acts1:28) as ‘true knowledge.’ For another interpretation of ἐπίγνωσις as part of an idiom in [ROM 1:28](https://ref.ly/Acts1:28), see [31\.28](#31.28).

[ROM 1:28](https://ref.ly/Acts1:28) · [EPH 1:17](https://ref.ly/Gal1:17) · [EPH 4:13](https://ref.ly/Gal4:13) · [COL 1:9](https://ref.ly/Phil1:9) · [COL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Phil1:10) · [COL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Phil2:2) · [COL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Phil3:10) · [2TI 2:25](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:25) · [TIT 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:1) · [PHM 1:6](https://ref.ly/Titus1:6) · [HEB 10:26](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:26) · [2PE 1:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:2) · [2PE 1:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3) · [2PE 1:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:8) · [2PE 2:20](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:20)

28\.18 · Know · Known \[the content of knowledge]

what is known, definite knowledge, full knowledge, knowledge the content of what is definitely known

[ROM 3:20](https://ref.ly/Acts3:20) · [ROM 10:2](https://ref.ly/Acts10:2) · [PHP 1:9](https://ref.ly/Eph1:9) · [2TI 2:25](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:25) · [HEB 10:26](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:26)

27\.4 · Learn · Learn

**Collocation:** εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἔρχομαι

to learn about, to find out, to come to know (an idiom, literally: to come into knowledge) to acquire information about something, with emphasis upon the process involved It is also possible to treat the phrase εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἔρχομαι as meaning ‘to come to know’ in the sense of ‘to come to understand’ (see [32\.17](#32.17)).

[1TI 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:4)

32\.17 · Understand · Come To Understand

**Collocation:** εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἔρχομαι

to come to understand, to come to know (an idiom, literally: to come into knowledge) to come to understand the meaning of something, with special emphasis upon the process One may also interpret εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἔρχομαι as meaning ‘to come to have knowledge of, to come to know’ (see [27\.4](#27.4)).

[2TI 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:7)

## ἐπιδέχομαι (id: 001942000000000)

ἐπιδέχομαι
==========

ἐπιδέχομαι (G1926)

δέχομαι

34\.53 · Association · Welcome, Receive

to welcome, to receive, to accept, to have as a guest to accept the presence of a person with friendliness1

[3JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/2John1:10)

36\.14 · Guide, Discipline, Follow · Obey, Disobey

to pay attention to and obey to listen or pay attention to a person, with resulting conformity to what is advised or commanded2

[3JN 1:9](https://ref.ly/2John1:9)

1 There are no doubt certain contextual constraints involved in the choice of ἀναδέχομαι\[a], ἀποδέχομαι\[a], ἐπιδέχομαι\[a], εἰσδέχομαι, ὑποδέχομαι, προσδέχομαι\[b], and παραδέχομαι\[b] in the sense that the spacial relationships involved in the welcoming of a guest can be reflected in the prepositional prefixes. For example, in [LUK 8:40](https://ref.ly/Mark8:40) the use of ἀποδέχομαι may focus upon the particular source, while ἐπιδέχομαι in [3JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/2John1:10) may be relevant in view of the fact that the believers are coming to a particular point. προσδέχομαι in [LUK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Mark15:2) may suggest a kind of reciprocal relationship which is established with outcasts, and παραδέχομαι in [ACT 15:4](https://ref.ly/John15:4) may simply point to spacial proximity. However, it would be wrong to insist upon clear distinctions in meaning based on the very limited number of contexts involved. Some of these same distinctions in spacial relations may exist for the set προσλαμβάνω\[e], παραλαμβάνω\[d], ἀπολαμβάνω\[d]. 2 Both ἀκούω\[e] and ἐπιδέχομαι\[b] involve an overlapping of domains, for there is an evident factor of perception in paying attention, but also a clear inference of subsequent obedience.

* **Associated Passages:** 3 John 1:10; 3 John 1:9

## ἐπιθυμία (id: 001955000000000)

ἐπιθυμία
========

ἐπιθυμία (G1939)

noun · θυμόομαι

25\.12 · Attitudes and Emotions · Desire Strongly

longing for, desire greatly desiring to do or have something The phrase ἐπιθυμίᾳ ἐπεθύμησα may be regarded as a type of Semitic idiom.

[MRK 4:19](https://ref.ly/Matt4:19) · [LUK 22:15](https://ref.ly/Mark22:15) · [EPH 4:22](https://ref.ly/Gal4:22) · [PHP 1:23](https://ref.ly/Eph1:23) · [1TH 2:17](https://ref.ly/Col2:17) · [TIT 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:12) · [REV 18:14](https://ref.ly/Jude18:14)

25\.20 · Attitudes and Emotions · Desire Strongly

coveting, lust, desire strongly desiring to have what belongs to someone else and/or to engage in an activity which is morally wrong

[JHN 8:44](https://ref.ly/Luke8:44) · [ROM 1:24](https://ref.ly/Acts1:24) · [ROM 6:12](https://ref.ly/Acts6:12) · [ROM 7:7](https://ref.ly/Acts7:7) · [ROM 7:8](https://ref.ly/Acts7:8) · [ROM 13:14](https://ref.ly/Acts13:14) · [GAL 5:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:16) · [GAL 5:24](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:24) · [EPH 2:3](https://ref.ly/Gal2:3) · [COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Phil3:5) · [1TH 4:5](https://ref.ly/Col4:5) · [1TI 6:9](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:9) · [2TI 2:22](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:22) · [2TI 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:6) · [2TI 4:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:3) · [TIT 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:12) · [TIT 3:3](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:3) · [JAS 1:14](https://ref.ly/Heb1:14) · [JAS 1:15](https://ref.ly/Heb1:15) · [1PE 1:14](https://ref.ly/Jas1:14) · [1PE 2:11](https://ref.ly/Jas2:11) · [1PE 4:2](https://ref.ly/Jas4:2) · [1PE 4:3](https://ref.ly/Jas4:3) · [2PE 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:4) · [2PE 2:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:10) · [2PE 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:18) · [2PE 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:3) · [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:16) · [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:16) · [1JN 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17) · [JUD 1:16](https://ref.ly/3John1:16) · [JUD 1:18](https://ref.ly/3John1:18)

## ἐπικαθίζω (id: 001956000000000)

ἐπικαθίζω
=========

ἐπικαθίζω (G1940)

καθίζω

17\.15 · Stances and Events Related to Stances · Sit

to sit upon, to mount, to get on to sit down upon something In [MAT 21:7](https://ref.ly/Matt21:7) the pronominal form αὐτῶν is ambiguous in reference, for it may refer either to the garments or to the donkey and colt. The closest noun reference is τὰ ἱμάτια ‘the garments,’ but in view of Matthew’s quotation and evident interpretation of the reference to [ZEC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Zech9:9), both the donkey and the colt may be the substantive referents, though being seated upon two animals at the same time poses real problems of understanding.

[MAT 21:7](https://ref.ly/Matt21:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 21:7


## ἐπικαλέω (id: 001957000000000)

ἐπικαλέω
========

ἐπικαλέω (G1941) — ἐπικαλέομαι

καλέω

33\.131 · Communication · Name

to call, to name to use an attribution in speaking of a person It is not always possible to determine from the context whether the act involved is an initial naming of a person or simply calling a person by a particular name. Likewise in the case of attributions or titles, it is not always possible to determine whether it is a matter of giving an attribution or title to a person or simply a matter of speaking of or to a person by means of such an attribution or title. In some instances the giving of a title may be made indirect. For example, in [MAT 1:23](https://ref.ly/Matt1:23) the statement ‘and they will call his name Emmanuel’ suggests the giving of a title, but the process is stated in terms of how people will speak of this person.

[MAT 10:25](https://ref.ly/Matt10:25) · [ACT 1:23](https://ref.ly/John1:23) · [ACT 4:36](https://ref.ly/John4:36) · [ACT 7:59](https://ref.ly/John7:59) · [ACT 9:21](https://ref.ly/John9:21) · [ACT 10:5](https://ref.ly/John10:5) · [ACT 10:18](https://ref.ly/John10:18) · [ACT 10:32](https://ref.ly/John10:32) · [ACT 11:13](https://ref.ly/John11:13) · [ACT 12:12](https://ref.ly/John12:12) · [ACT 12:25](https://ref.ly/John12:25) · [HEB 11:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:16) · [JAS 2:7](https://ref.ly/Heb2:7) · [1PE 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jas1:17)

33\.176 · Communication · Ask For, Request

to call upon, to appeal to, to ask for help to call upon someone to do something, normally implying an appeal for aid ἐπικαλέομαι\[a] also occurs in such expressions as ὅς ἂν ἐπικαλέσηται τὸ ὄνομα κυρίου ([ACT 2:21](https://ref.ly/John2:21)) ‘whoever calls upon the name of the Lord,’ but the meaning is essentially the same as ‘to call upon the Lord,’ since τὸ ὄνομα ‘the name’ may simply be a metonym for ‘the Lord.’

[ACT 2:21](https://ref.ly/John2:21) · [ACT 22:16](https://ref.ly/John22:16) · [ROM 10:12](https://ref.ly/Acts10:12) · [ROM 10:13](https://ref.ly/Acts10:13) · [ROM 10:14](https://ref.ly/Acts10:14) · [1CO 1:2](https://ref.ly/Rom1:2) · [2CO 1:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:23) · [2TI 2:22](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:22) · [1PE 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jas1:17)

56\.15 · Courts and Legal Procedures · Judicial Hearing, Inquiry

to appeal one’s case, to appeal to a higher court to claim one’s legal right to have a case reviewed by a higher tribunal The second clause in [ACT 26:32](https://ref.ly/John26:32) may also be rendered in a number of languages as ‘if he had not asked for the Emperor to listen to his case’ or ‘... to judge the accusations against him.’

[ACT 25:11](https://ref.ly/John25:11) · [ACT 25:12](https://ref.ly/John25:12) · [ACT 25:21](https://ref.ly/John25:21) · [ACT 25:25](https://ref.ly/John25:25) · [ACT 26:32](https://ref.ly/John26:32) · [ACT 28:19](https://ref.ly/John28:19)

11\.28 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · Socio\-Religious

**Collocation:** ἐπικαλέομαι τὸ ὄνομά τινος ἐπί τινα

to be one of (God’s) people, to be the people of (an idiom, literally: to have someone’s name called upon someone) to be acknowledged as belonging to the one whose name is called upon such an individual This interpretation of the expression ἐπικαλέομαι τὸ ὄνομά τινος ἐπί τινα is based upon well attested OT usage, and though it is quite different from traditional interpretations of [ACT 9:14](https://ref.ly/John9:14) and [ACT 15:17](https://ref.ly/John15:17), it should be followed as reflecting a more satisfactory and accurate interpretation of the passive form of ἐπικαλέω.

[ACT 9:14](https://ref.ly/John9:14) · [ACT 15:17](https://ref.ly/John15:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 10:25; Acts 1:23; Acts 4:36; Acts 7:59; Acts 9:21; Acts 10:5; Acts 10:18; Acts 10:32; Acts 11:13; Acts 12:12; Acts 12:25; Hebrews 11:16; James 2:7; 1 Peter 1:17; Acts 2:21; Acts 22:16; Romans 10:12; Romans 10:13; Romans 10:14; 1 Corinthians 1:2; 2 Corinthians 1:23; 2 Timothy 2:22; Acts 25:11; Acts 25:12; Acts 25:21; Acts 25:25; Acts 26:32; Acts 28:19; Acts 9:14; Acts 15:17

ἐπικάλυμμα (G1942)

noun · καλύπτω

## ἐπικατάρατος (id: 001960000000000)

ἐπικατάρατος
============

ἐπικατάρατος (G1944)

adj · ἀρά

33\.475 · Communication · Bless, Curse

cursed, accursed pertaining to being cursed The occurrences of ἐπάρατος in [JHN 7:49](https://ref.ly/Luke7:49) and ἐπικατάρατος in [GAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:10) imply much more than being cursed by some person. In such contexts, the real meaning is that these individuals have already been condemned by God or are under the threat of such a condemnation.

[GAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:10) · [GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Galatians 3:10; Galatians 3:13


## ἐπίκειμαι (id: 001961000000000)

ἐπίκειμαι
=========

ἐπίκειμαι (G1945)

κεῖμαι

85\.4 · Existence in Space · Be in a Place

to be on, to lie on to be in a place on something

[JHN 11:38](https://ref.ly/Luke11:38) · [JHN 21:9](https://ref.ly/Luke21:9)

19\.43 · Physical Impact · Press

to press against, to push against to press or push against

[LUK 5:1](https://ref.ly/Mark5:1) · [ACT 27:20](https://ref.ly/John27:20)

76\.17 · Power, Force

to be in force, to have power over to possess power or sanction In some languages the concept of ‘being in force’ may be expressed simply as ‘to work’ or ‘to do what they are supposed to do’ or ‘to be in control.’ For another interpretation of ἐπίκειμαι in [HEB 9:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:10), see [13\.73](#13.73).

[1CO 9:16](https://ref.ly/Rom9:16) · [HEB 9:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:10)

68\.16 · Aspect · Continue

to keep on, to continue insisting, to persist in to keep on doing something with presumed insistence

[LUK 23:23](https://ref.ly/Mark23:23)

13\.73 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Exist

to exist, to exist for, to be set to exist, with the implication of having been established and thus having continuity and purpose For another interpretation of ἐπίκειμαι in [HEB 9:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:10), see [76\.17](#76.17).

[HEB 9:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:10)

* **Associated Passages:** John 11:38; John 21:9; Luke 5:1; Acts 27:20; 1 Corinthians 9:16; Hebrews 9:10; Luke 23:23

ἐπικέλλω
========

ἐπικέλλω (G2027a)

κέλλω · κελεύω

54\.18 · Maritime Activities

to run aground (a technical, nautical term) to cause a ship to run up onto or against a shore In some instances it may be necessary to translate ἐπικέλλω as ‘they caused the ship to get stuck in the sand’ or ‘... in the mud.’

## Ἐπικούρειος (id: 001963000000000)

Ἐπικούρειος
===========

Ἐπικούρειος (G1946)

noun nom

11\.96 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · Philosophical

Epicurean, a follower of Epicurus pertaining to the philosophical system of the Greek philosopher Epicurus, who taught that the world is a series of fortuitous combinations of atoms and that the highest good is pleasure In most instances translators have found it more satisfactory to simply transliterate the terms Ἐπικούρειος and Στοϊκός ([11\.97](#11.97)), but some supplementary note to identify the teachings of the Epicureans and Stoics is important.

[ACT 17:18](https://ref.ly/John17:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 17:18

## ἐπικρίνω (id: 001965000000000)

ἐπικρίνω
========

ἐπικρίνω (G1948)

κρίνω

30\.75 · Think · To Decide, To Conclude

to come to a conclusion, to decide, to make up one’s mind to come to a conclusion in the process of thinking and thus to be in a position to make a decision1 In some languages the process of ‘deciding’ is expressed idiomatically, for example, ‘to come to the end in one’s thinking’ or ‘to choose in one’s mind’ or ‘the mind sees its goal.’

[LUK 23:24](https://ref.ly/Mark23:24)

1 It is possible that ἐπικρίνω differs from κρίνω\[a] in focusing greater attention upon what is decided.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 23:24


## ἐπιλαμβάνομαι (id: 001966000000000)

ἐπιλαμβάνομαι
=============

ἐπιλαμβάνομαι (G1949)

λαμβάνω

18\.2 · Attachment · Grasp, Hold

to take hold of, to grasp to take hold of or grasp, with focus upon the goal of the motion In a number of languages quite different terms must be employed if one is to take someone by the hand in order to lead him or take someone by the hand in order to rescue him. The difference implied in the presumed force or strength required frequently results in quite different words being used.

[MAT 14:31](https://ref.ly/Matt14:31) · [MRK 8:23](https://ref.ly/Matt8:23) · [LUK 9:47](https://ref.ly/Mark9:47) · [LUK 14:4](https://ref.ly/Mark14:4) · [LUK 23:26](https://ref.ly/Mark23:26) · [ACT 9:27](https://ref.ly/John9:27) · [ACT 17:19](https://ref.ly/John17:19) · [ACT 18:17](https://ref.ly/John18:17) · [ACT 21:30](https://ref.ly/John21:30) · [ACT 23:19](https://ref.ly/John23:19) · [HEB 8:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm8:9)

37\.110 · Control, Rule · Seize, Take into Custody

to seize, to arrest to take a person into custody for alleged illegal activity

[ACT 16:19](https://ref.ly/John16:19) · [ACT 21:33](https://ref.ly/John21:33)

27\.32 · Learn · Learn Something Against Someone

to catch, to trap, to seize, to learn in order to seize (figurative extension of meaning of ἐπιλαμβάνομαι\[a] ‘to grasp,’ [18\.2](#18.2)) to learn something about someone, with the purpose of seizing, arresting, or overcoming

[LUK 20:20](https://ref.ly/Mark20:20) · [LUK 20:26](https://ref.ly/Mark20:26)

35\.1 · Help, Care For · Help

to help to assist in supplying what may be needed1 Though the meaning of ‘help’ is possible for ἐπιλαμβάνομαι in [HEB 2:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm2:16), the more generally accepted interpretation is ‘to be concerned with’ or ‘to take an interest in’ (see [30\.42](#30.42)).

[HEB 2:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm2:16)2 · [HEB 2:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm2:16)2

30\.42 · Think · To Think About, with the Implied Purpose of Responding Appropriately

to be concerned about, to be concerned for to be concerned about, with the implication of possible help For another interpretation of ἐπιλαμβάνομαι in [HEB 2:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm2:16), see [35\.1](#35.1).2

[HEB 2:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm2:16)2 · [HEB 2:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm2:16)2

90\.63 · Case · Experiencer

to undergo, to experience to experience some event or state, often with the implication of something negatively valued3 In the expression in [1TI 6:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:12), ἐπιλαβοῦ no doubt also suggests some activity associated with the state of eternal life.

[1TI 6:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:12) · [1TI 6:19](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:19)

1 The various terms noted in [35\.1](#35.1) (ἀντιλαμβάνω\[a], ἐπιλαμβάνομαι\[d], ὑπολαμβάνω\[c], συμβάλλω\[e], παρίστημι\[l], ἀντέχω\[c], ἐπαρκέω\[a], βοηθέω, βοήθεια\[a]) with a generic meaning of ‘help’ no doubt possess certain contrastive features of meaning, at least of a connotative type, but there is no specific evidence of this in NT usage. 2 HEB 2:16 · HEB 2:16 It is difficult to know whether ἐπιλαμβάνομαι in a context such as [HEB 2:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm2:16) refers principally or even in a significant measure to the intellectual concern rather than the more practical implication of giving help. It is specifically for that reason that ἐπιλαμβάνομαι is classified in both Domain 30 and Domain 35\. 3 There are no doubt certain subtle distinctions of meaning in this series of related expressions in [90\.63](#90.63) (ἀπολαμβάνω\[e], ἐπιλαμβάνομαι\[f], λαμβάνω\[h], μεταλαμβάνω\[b]), but it is difficult to determine the precise distinctions on the basis of existing contexts. As in the case of a number of verbs which mark an experiencer relation, an equivalent translation consists simply of a passive form of a verb related to the noun complement. For example, in the case of [JHN 7:23](https://ref.ly/Luke7:23), one might very well translate ‘if a person is circumcised on the Sabbath.’

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 14:31; Mark 8:23; Luke 9:47; Luke 14:4; Luke 23:26; Acts 9:27; Acts 17:19; Acts 18:17; Acts 21:30; Acts 23:19; Hebrews 8:9; Acts 16:19; Acts 21:33; Luke 20:20; Luke 20:26; Hebrews 2:16; 1 Timothy 6:12; 1 Timothy 6:19

## ἐπιλύω (id: 001974000000000)

ἐπιλύω
======

ἐπιλύω (G1956)

λύω

33\.141 · Communication · Interpret, Mean, Explain

to explain, meaning, explanation to explain the meaning of something, with the implication that the text in question is difficult or complex

[MRK 4:34](https://ref.ly/Matt4:34)

30\.81 · Think · To Decide, To Conclude

to resolve (a dispute), to settle (a problem), to come to a decision (a figurative extension of meaning of ἐπιλύω ‘to untie, to loose,’ not occurring in the NT) to come to a conclusion concerning a presumably difficult or complex matter

[ACT 19:39](https://ref.ly/John19:39)1

1 ACT 19:39 ἐπιλύω\[b] in [ACT 19:39](https://ref.ly/John19:39) involves several different semantic features, but since the focus is upon the final resolution of the issues, the meaning of ἐπιλύω in [ACT 19:39](https://ref.ly/John19:39) can perhaps be best treated in this subdomain.

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 4:34; Acts 19:39

===========

μαρτυρέω

[1PE 5:12](https://ref.ly/Jas5:12)

1 It is possible that ἐπιμαρτυρέω is somewhat more specific or emphatic in meaning than μαρτυρέω\[a], but this cannot be determined from NT contexts.

## ἐπιπίπτω (id: 001985000000000)

========

πίπτω

19\.43 · Physical Impact · Press

to press against, to push against to press or push against

[MRK 3:10](https://ref.ly/Matt3:10) · [ACT 20:10](https://ref.ly/John20:10)

13\.122 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Happen

to happen to, to fall upon to happen suddenly to, with the connotation of something bad and adverse In a number of languages it is difficult to speak of ‘insults happening.’ Therefore, it may be necessary to restructure this expression in [ROM 15:3](https://ref.ly/Acts15:3) as ‘they began insulting me in the same way they used to insult you.’

[LUK 1:12](https://ref.ly/Mark1:12) · [ACT 8:16](https://ref.ly/John8:16) · [ACT 10:44](https://ref.ly/John10:44) · [ACT 11:15](https://ref.ly/John11:15) · [ACT 19:17](https://ref.ly/John19:17) · [ROM 15:3](https://ref.ly/Acts15:3) · [REV 11:11](https://ref.ly/Jude11:11)

34\.64 · Association · Kiss, Embrace

**Collocation:** ἐπιπίπτω ἐπὶ τὸν τράχηλον

to hug, to embrace (an idiom, literally: to fall on the neck) to show special affection for by throwing one’s arms around a person1 A literal translation of ἐπιπίπτω ἐπὶ τὸν τράχηλον ‘to fall on someone’s neck’ can be seriously misunderstood. In fact, in some languages it is so close to an expression meaning ‘to kill’ that one should normally avoid any literal rendering. At the same time, it is very important not to employ terms for ‘embrace’ and ‘kiss’ in [ACT 20:37](https://ref.ly/John20:37) which might suggest improper sexual interests.

[LUK 15:20](https://ref.ly/Mark15:20) · [ACT 20:37](https://ref.ly/John20:37)

1 The idiom ἐπιπίπτω ἐπὶ τὸν τράχηλον would seem to indicate somewhat greater affection and concern than the verb ἐναγκαλίζομαι ([34\.63](#34.63)).

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 3:10; Acts 20:10; Luke 1:12; Acts 8:16; Acts 10:44; Acts 11:15; Acts 19:17; Romans 15:3; Revelation 11:11; Luke 15:20; Acts 20:37

[1PE 5:7](https://ref.ly/Jas5:7)

1 The meanings of ἐπιβάλλω\[a] ([15\.218](#15.218)) and ἐπιρίπτω ([15\.219](#15.219)) parallel to a certain extent the distinction in βάλλω\[a] ([15\.215](#15.215)) and ῥίπτω\[a] ([15\.217](#15.217)) in that βάλλω\[a] suggests a more deliberate action, while ῥίπτω\[a] suggests an action characterized by being quicker, more energetic, and often somewhat more impetuous.

## ἐπισιτισμός (id: 001996000000000)

ἐπισιτισμός
===========

ἐπισιτισμός (G1979)

noun · σῖτος

5\.1 · Foods and Condiments · Food

food any kind of food or nourishment1 Almost all languages have at least a few general terms for food, though these may be derived from verbal expressions meaning essentially ‘that which is eaten.’ In some instances a distinction may be made between ‘that which is chewed’ and ‘that which is drunk,’ and it may therefore be important in certain contexts to speak of food in general by a phrase meaning ‘that which is either chewed or drunk.’ If one fails to include both types of food, the reader might interpret a passage such as [MAT 6:11](https://ref.ly/Matt6:11) as requesting only solid foods.\|In the place of a general term or phrase such as ‘that which is eaten’ or ‘that which is consumed,’ some languages generalize the meaning of a particular term. For example, in certain parts of the Orient the term meaning specifically ‘rice’ also refers to ‘food’ in general. In fact, some persons will declare, ‘I have not eaten!’ if they have not had rice as part of a meal. A similar type of meaning has developed in English with respect to ‘bread.’ For example, in rendering [LUK 4:4](https://ref.ly/Mark4:4) (οὐκ ἐπ’ ἄρτῳ μόνῳ ζήσεται ὁ ἄνθρωπος ‘man shall not live by bread alone’) it would be wrong to employ a strictly literal rendering, since this might mean simply that people should not live on a diet consisting solely of bread. The meaning of [LUK 4:4](https://ref.ly/Mark4:4) is, of course, ‘to live means more than merely eating.’

[LUK 9:12](https://ref.ly/Mark9:12)

1 There are no doubt certain subtle differences of meaning in the various terms listed here as meaning food, but it is impossible to determine such distinctions in meaning from NT contexts. Perhaps τροφή and διατροφή are somewhat more generic in meaning and may suggest a greater emphasis upon nourishment. At the same time, βρῶσις\[c] and βρῶμα\[a] may imply solid substantive food, but it is difficult, if not impossible, to argue for such subtle connotative distinctions in meanings which are essentially generic.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 9:12

35\.39 · Help, Care For · Care For, Take Care Of

## ἐπισκηνόω (id: 001999000000000)

ἐπισκηνόω
=========

ἐπισκηνόω (G1981)

σκηνόω · σκηνή

85\.75 · Existence in Space · Dwell, Reside

to take up residence, to come to reside, to come to dwell to come to dwell in a place defined psychologically or spiritually (with the possible implication in some contexts of a temporary arrangement) In their NT contexts, σκηνόω and ἐπισκηνόω are essentially figurative in meaning, for they deal with spiritual existence and residence rather than human residence or dwelling. In translating one should, in so far as possible, try to preserve this important figurative relationship, since it expresses one of the most significant ways in which spiritual and human existence can be combined. In some languages, however, it may be necessary to employ an expression which means simply ‘to be in’ or ‘to continue with,’ since expressions for ‘living’ or ‘dwelling’ may be based upon terms which are too specific in meaning, for example, ‘to eat from the same pot’ or ‘to enter by the same door.’

[2CO 12:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:9)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 12:9


## ἐπισκιάζω (id: 002000000000000)

ἐπισκιάζω
=========

ἐπισκιάζω (G1982)

σκιάζω · σκιά

14\.62 · Physical Events and States · Darkness

to cast a shadow upon to cause a shadow by interposing something between an object and a source of light In [ACT 5:15](https://ref.ly/John5:15) there is a semantic duplication of the element of ‘shadow’ in view of the noun σκιά and the verb ἐπισκιάζω. In [MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5) the shining cloud would not produce the same kind of shadow as in the case of [ACT 5:15](https://ref.ly/John5:15), but it would be interposed between (1\) the sun and (2\) Jesus and his disciples. In [MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5) the shining cloud may thus be more like a covering than a shadow. The reference to this shining cloud in [MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5) as the Shekinah of God is discussed in [14\.49](#14.49) and [14\.50](#14.50).\|In [LUK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Mark1:35) ἐπισκιάζω is used figuratively in speaking of the agency of the Holy Spirit in causing Mary to conceive: πνεῦμα ἅγιον ἐπελεύσεται ἐπὶ σέκαὶ δύναμις ὑψίστου ἐπισκιάσει σοι the Holy Spirit will come upon you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you.\|In a number of languages it is not possible, however, to translate ἐπισκιάζω literally as ‘overshadow.’ It may therefore be necessary to use some such expression as the power of the Most High will come to you or God’s power will come upon you. A strictly literal rendering of ἐπισκιάζω in [LUK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Mark1:35) could be interpreted in some languages as being a rather crude reference to sexual intercourse, and this interpretation should be avoided.

[MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5) · [MRK 9:7](https://ref.ly/Matt9:7) · [LUK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Mark1:35) · [LUK 9:34](https://ref.ly/Mark9:34) · [ACT 5:15](https://ref.ly/John5:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 17:5; Mark 9:7; Luke 1:35; Luke 9:34; Acts 5:15


## ἐπισκοπέω (id: 002001000000000)

ἐπισκοπέω
=========

ἐπισκοπέω (G1983) — ἐπισκοπεύω

σκοπέω

35\.39 · Help, Care For · Care For, Take Care Of

to look after, to take care of, to see to to care for or look after, with the implication of continuous responsibility For another interpretation of ἐπισκοπέω in [HEB 12:15](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:15), see [30\.46](#30.46).

[HEB 12:15](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:15) · [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/Jas5:2)

30\.46 · Think · To Think About, with the Implied Purpose of Responding Appropriately

to give careful attention to, to consider carefully, to guard against to give careful consideration to something, with the implication of guarding against For another interpretation of ἐπισκοπέω in [HEB 12:15](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:15), see [35\.39](#35.39). Some scholars have seen in the occurrence of ἐπισκοπέω in [HEB 12:15](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:15) a meaning at least somewhat similar to that which occurs in ἐπισκοπέω\[c] ‘to be responsible, to care for’ ([53\.70](#53.70)).

[HEB 12:15](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:15)

53\.70 · Religious Activities · Roles and Functions

to minister unto, to be responsible, to care for to have responsibility for the care of someone, implying a somewhat official responsibility within a congregation It is possible, however, that ἐπισκοπέω in [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/Jas5:2) is not to be understood as designating some official responsibility but merely as a role of helping and serving (see [35\.39](#35.39)).

[1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/Jas5:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 12:15; 1 Peter 5:2


## ἐπισκοπή (id: 002002000000000)

ἐπισκοπή
========

ἐπισκοπή (G1984)

noun · σκοπή · σκοπέω

34\.51 · Association · Visit

coming, visitation the coming of divine power, either for benefit or judgment In [1PE 2:12](https://ref.ly/Jas2:12) the reference may be to God’s coming to rule or his coming in judgment.

[LUK 19:44](https://ref.ly/Mark19:44) · [1PE 2:12](https://ref.ly/Jas2:12)

53\.69 · Religious Activities · Roles and Functions

office, position, ministry as church leader a religious role involving both service and leadership See discussion at [53\.71](#53.71). For a more probable interpretation of ἐπισκοπή in [ACT 1:20](https://ref.ly/John1:20), see [35\.40](#35.40).

[ACT 1:20](https://ref.ly/John1:20) · [1TI 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:1)

35\.40 · Help, Care For · Care For, Take Care Of

position of responsibility, position of oversight (derivative of ἐπισκοπέω\[a] ‘to take care of,’ [35\.39](#35.39)) the position of one who has responsibility for the care of someone Though in some contexts ἐπισκοπή has been regarded traditionally as a position of authority, in reality the focus is upon the responsibility for caring for others, and in the context of [ACT 1:20](https://ref.ly/John1:20) the reference is clearly to the responsibility for caring for the church (see [53\.69](#53.69)).

[ACT 1:20](https://ref.ly/John1:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 19:44; 1 Peter 2:12; Acts 1:20; 1 Timothy 3:1


## ἐπίσκοπος (id: 002003000000000)

ἐπίσκοπος
=========

ἐπίσκοπος (G1985)

noun · σκοπέω

35\.43 · Help, Care For · Care For, Take Care Of

one responsible for, one who cares for, guardian, keeper one who has the responsibility of caring for spiritual concerns In [1PE 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jas2:25) ἐπίσκοπος is applied to Christ, and it no doubt shares certain of the meanings associated with ἐπίσκοπος\[b] in [53\.71](#53.71), but the focus in [1PE 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jas2:25) is not upon leadership but upon the role of caring for the believers.

[1PE 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jas2:25)

53\.71 · Religious Activities · Roles and Functions

church leader one who serves as a leader in a church For ἐπίσκοπος in [1PE 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jas2:25), see [35\.43](#35.43).\|In translating ἐπισκοπή\[b] ([53\.69](#53.69)), ἐπισκοπέω\[c] ([53\.70](#53.70)), or ἐπίσκοπος\[b], it is important to try to combine the concepts of both service and leadership, in other words, the responsibility of caring for the needs of a congregation as well as directing the activities of the membership. In some translations an equivalent may be ‘helper’ and ‘leader.’

[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/John20:28) · [PHP 1:1](https://ref.ly/Eph1:1) · [1TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:2) · [TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:7)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Peter 2:25; Acts 20:28; Philippians 1:1; 1 Timothy 3:2; Titus 1:7


## ἐπισπάομαι (id: 002004000000000)

ἐπισπάομαι
==========

ἐπισπάομαι (G1986)

σπάομαι

53\.52 · Religious Activities · Dedicate, Consecrate

to conceal circumcision, to extend the foreskin (a technical and medical term) to pull the foreskin over the end of the penis as a means of concealing former circumcision A literal and descriptive rendering of ἐπισπάομαι could prove to be quite vulgar in some languages. Furthermore, such a practice might seem to be medically impossible or absurd, so that in general, translators have used some such expression as ‘do not conceal the circumcision’ or ‘do not change the circumcision to appear like uncircumcision,’ but often some additional marginal note is required if people are to understand satisfactorily what is involved.

[1CO 7:18](https://ref.ly/Rom7:18)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 7:18

to sow in addition, to sow on top of to sow in addition to a previous sowing

ἐπίσταμαι

ἐπίσταμαι (G1987)

ἵστημι

28\.3 · Know · Know

to know to possess information about, with the implication of an understanding of the significance of such information1

[ACT 10:28](https://ref.ly/John10:28) · [ACT 15:7](https://ref.ly/John15:7) · [ACT 18:25](https://ref.ly/John18:25) · [ACT 19:15](https://ref.ly/John19:15) · [ACT 19:25](https://ref.ly/John19:25) · [ACT 20:18](https://ref.ly/John20:18) · [ACT 22:19](https://ref.ly/John22:19) · [ACT 24:10](https://ref.ly/John24:10) · [ACT 26:26](https://ref.ly/John26:26) · [HEB 11:8](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:8) · [JAS 4:14](https://ref.ly/Heb4:14)

32\.3 · Understand · Understand

to understand, to be aware of, to really know to have or gain insight, with focus upon the process

[MRK 14:68](https://ref.ly/Matt14:68) · [1TI 6:4](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:4) · [JAS 4:14](https://ref.ly/Heb4:14) · [JUD 1:10](https://ref.ly/3John1:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 10:28; Acts 15:7; Acts 18:25; Acts 19:15; Acts 19:25; Acts 20:18; Acts 22:19; Acts 24:10; Acts 26:26; Hebrews 11:8; James 4:14; Mark 14:68; 1 Timothy 6:4; Jude 1:10


## ἐπίστασις (id: 002007000000000)

ἐπίστασις
=========

ἐπίστασις (G1999a)

noun · ἵστημι

25\.239 · Attitudes and Emotions · Worry, Anxiety, Distress, Peace

pressure, concern, burden a state of prolonged concern and anxiety For another interpretation of ἐπίστασις in [2CO 11:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:28), see [35\.42](#35.42).

[2CO 11:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:28)

39\.34 · Hostility, Strife · Rebellion

revolt, insurrection, rebellion rising up in open defiance of authority, with the presumed intention to overthrow it or to act in complete opposition to its demands1 It is also possible that ἀποστασία differs slightly in meaning from ἐπίστασις\[b] by virtue of the differences in prefixal formation, but there is insufficient contextual evidence to support such a view.

[ACT 24:12](https://ref.ly/John24:12)

35\.42 · Help, Care For · Care For, Take Care Of

responsibility for, concern for, care of responsibility for oversight based upon authority Though one might regard ἐπίστασις as meaning only the position of authority, nevertheless this authority is expressed in terms of the oversight and care of others. In a sense the meaning of ἐπίστασις in [2CO 11:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:28) is defined by the following appositional phrase, ἡ μέριμνα πασῶν τῶν ἐκκλησιῶν ‘concern for all the churches.’ For another interpretation of ἐπίστασις in [2CO 11:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:28), see [25\.239](#25.239).

[2CO 11:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:28)

1 It is possible that ἐπανίστημι and ἐπίστασις\[b], with the prefix ἐπι, differ in some measure from ἀνίστημι\[h] and στάσις\[a] by virtue of the prefixal element which may suggest action directed specifically toward some object, but there is insufficient contextual evidence to support such distinct meanings.

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 11:28; Acts 24:12

## ἐπιστολή (id: 002012000000000)

ἐπιστολή
========

ἐπιστολή (G1992)

noun · στέλλομαι

6\.63 · Artifacts · Instruments Used in Marking and Writing

letter (normally written on papyrus) an object containing writing addressed to one or more persons In some languages it may be necessary to try to distinguish between a letter as an object and a letter as content or message. In [ACT 23:33](https://ref.ly/John23:33) the focus seems to be upon the letter as an object, not necessarily upon the contents of the document. In most contexts, however, it is impossible to distinguish between a letter as a material object and a letter as a message (see [33\.48](#33.48)).

[ACT 9:2](https://ref.ly/John9:2) · [ACT 15:30](https://ref.ly/John15:30) · [ACT 22:5](https://ref.ly/John22:5) · [ACT 23:25](https://ref.ly/John23:25) · [ACT 23:33](https://ref.ly/John23:33) · [ROM 16:22](https://ref.ly/Acts16:22) · [1CO 16:3](https://ref.ly/Rom16:3) · [2CO 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:1) · [1TH 5:27](https://ref.ly/Col5:27) · [2TH 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:2) · [2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:15) · [2TH 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:14)

33\.48 · Communication · Written Language

letter a written communication, usually from one person to one or more persons1 It is also possible to understand ἐπιστολή in [ACT 23:25](https://ref.ly/John23:25) not as a content or message, but as an object (see [6\.63](#6.63))

[ACT 9:2](https://ref.ly/John9:2) · [ACT 23:25](https://ref.ly/John23:25) · [ROM 16:22](https://ref.ly/Acts16:22) · [1CO 5:9](https://ref.ly/Rom5:9) · [1CO 16:3](https://ref.ly/Rom16:3) · [2CO 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:2) · [2CO 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:3) · [2CO 7:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:8) · [2CO 7:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:8) · [2CO 10:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:9) · [2CO 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:10) · [2CO 10:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:11) · [COL 4:16](https://ref.ly/Phil4:16) · [1TH 5:27](https://ref.ly/Col5:27) · [2TH 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:2) · [2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:15) · [2TH 3:17](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:17) · [2PE 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1) · [2PE 3:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:16)

1 ἐπιστολή may occur either in the sense of the physical object (see [6\.63](#6.63)) which is used as a means of communication or as the content of what is written ([33\.48](#33.48)), but in some contexts it is difficult, if not impossible, to distinguish between these two phases of the meaning of ἐπιστολή.

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 9:2; Acts 15:30; Acts 22:5; Acts 23:25; Acts 23:33; Romans 16:22; 1 Corinthians 16:3; 2 Corinthians 3:1; 1 Thessalonians 5:27; 2 Thessalonians 2:2; 2 Thessalonians 2:15; 2 Thessalonians 3:14; 1 Corinthians 5:9; 2 Corinthians 3:2; 2 Corinthians 3:3; 2 Corinthians 7:8; 2 Corinthians 10:9; 2 Corinthians 10:10; 2 Corinthians 10:11; Colossians 4:16; 2 Thessalonians 3:17; 2 Peter 3:1; 2 Peter 3:16

## ἐπιστρέφω (id: 002014000000000)

ἐπιστρέφω
=========

ἐπιστρέφω (G1994) — ἐπιστρέφομαι

στρέφω

15\.90 · Linear Movement · Return

to return to, to go back to, to turn around to return to a point or area where one has been before, with probable emphasis on turning about

[MAT 10:13](https://ref.ly/Matt10:13) · [MAT 12:44](https://ref.ly/Matt12:44) · [MAT 24:18](https://ref.ly/Matt24:18) · [MRK 5:30](https://ref.ly/Matt5:30) · [MRK 8:33](https://ref.ly/Matt8:33) · [MRK 13:16](https://ref.ly/Matt13:16) · [LUK 2:39](https://ref.ly/Mark2:39) · [LUK 8:55](https://ref.ly/Mark8:55) · [LUK 17:4](https://ref.ly/Mark17:4) · [LUK 17:31](https://ref.ly/Mark17:31) · [JHN 21:20](https://ref.ly/Luke21:20) · [ACT 9:40](https://ref.ly/John9:40) · [ACT 15:36](https://ref.ly/John15:36) · [ACT 16:18](https://ref.ly/John16:18) · [2PE 2:22](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:22) · [REV 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jude1:12) · [REV 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jude1:12)

31\.60 · Hold a View, Believe, Trust · Change an Opinion Concerning Truth

to turn to, to come to believe, to come to accept to change one’s belief, with focus upon that to which one turns In analyzing the meaning of στρέφω\[g], ἐπιστρέφω\[b], and ἐπιστροφή\[a] in such passages as [GAL 4:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:9) and [1PE 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jas2:25), it is difficult to determine the extent to which belief or behavior is primarily in focus. In the case of [1PE 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jas2:25), one could certainly argue that the emphasis is upon the interpersonal relationship. It is therefore essential in rendering these terms not to narrow the meaning too much, for in this way one would certainly do violence to certain crucial semantic features.

[ACT 3:19](https://ref.ly/John3:19) · [ACT 9:35](https://ref.ly/John9:35) · [ACT 14:15](https://ref.ly/John14:15) · [ACT 15:19](https://ref.ly/John15:19) · [2CO 3:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:16) · [GAL 4:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:9) · [1TH 1:9](https://ref.ly/Col1:9) · [1PE 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jas2:25)

31\.61 · Hold a View, Believe, Trust · Change an Opinion Concerning Truth

to cause to change belief, to cause to turn to to cause a person to change belief, with focus upon that to which one turns

[LUK 1:16](https://ref.ly/Mark1:16) · [ACT 26:18](https://ref.ly/John26:18) · [JAS 5:19](https://ref.ly/Heb5:19) · [JAS 5:20](https://ref.ly/Heb5:20)

41\.51 · Behavior and Related States · Change Behavior

to change one’s ways, to turn to God, repentance to change one’s manner of life in a particular direction, with the implication of turning back to God In a number of languages it is not sufficient to simply translate ‘to change one’s manner of life,’ for this could either be neutral or even suggest a change for the worse. Therefore, it may be necessary in a number of instances to translate ‘to change one’s way of living as God would want’ or ‘to change and live like God would want one to live.’

[MAT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:15) · [MRK 4:12](https://ref.ly/Matt4:12) · [LUK 22:32](https://ref.ly/Mark22:32) · [ACT 11:21](https://ref.ly/John11:21) · [ACT 26:20](https://ref.ly/John26:20) · [ACT 28:27](https://ref.ly/John28:27)

25\.95 · Attitudes and Emotions · Acceptable To, To Be Pleased With

**Collocation:** ἐπιστρέφω καρδίας ἐπί

to make well\-disposed toward, to make friendly toward, to cause to become acceptable (an idiom, literally: to turn hearts to) to cause a change of attitude in a positive and acceptable direction

[LUK 1:17](https://ref.ly/Mark1:17)

16\.13 · Non\-Linear Movement

to turn around, to turn toward to turn around to or toward

[MRK 5:30](https://ref.ly/Matt5:30) · [MRK 8:33](https://ref.ly/Matt8:33) · [JHN 21:20](https://ref.ly/Luke21:20) · [JAS 5:19](https://ref.ly/Heb5:19) · [JAS 5:20](https://ref.ly/Heb5:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 10:13; Matthew 12:44; Matthew 24:18; Mark 5:30; Mark 8:33; Mark 13:16; Luke 2:39; Luke 8:55; Luke 17:4; Luke 17:31; John 21:20; Acts 9:40; Acts 15:36; Acts 16:18; 2 Peter 2:22; Revelation 1:12; Acts 3:19; Acts 9:35; Acts 14:15; Acts 15:19; 2 Corinthians 3:16; Galatians 4:9; 1 Thessalonians 1:9; 1 Peter 2:25; Luke 1:16; Acts 26:18; James 5:19; James 5:20; Matthew 13:15; Mark 4:12; Luke 22:32; Acts 11:21; Acts 26:20; Acts 28:27; Luke 1:17

order, command, decree, ordinance, instruction (derivative of ἐπιτάσσω ‘to order, to instruct,’ [33\.325](#33.325)) that which has been specifically ordered or commanded

37\.42 · Control, Rule · Exercise Authority

right to command, authority to command, authority the right or authority to command It is also possible to translate this expression in [TIT 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:15) as ‘show that you have every right to command when you rebuke them.’

[TIT 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:15)

ἐπιτελέω
========

ἐπιτελέω (G2005)

τελέω

to complete, to finish, to end, to accomplish to bring an activity to a successful finish1

[ROM 15:28](https://ref.ly/Acts15:28) · [2CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:1) · [2CO 8:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:6) · [2CO 8:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:11) · [2CO 8:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:11) · [GAL 3:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:3) · [PHP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Eph1:6) · [HEB 9:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:6)

13\.108 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Happen

to accomplish, to bring about to cause to happen, with the purpose of some end result

[1PE 5:9](https://ref.ly/Jas5:9)

45\.2 · Building, Constructing

to set up, to erect, to put up to set up or erect a construction, often used in connection with tents

[HEB 8:5](https://ref.ly/Phlm8:5)

1 There are no doubt certain subtle distinctions, particularly of emphasis, in the series τελέω, ἐκτελέω\[a], ἀποτελέω, ἐπιτελέω\[a], συντελέω\[a], and τελειόω\[c], but it is not possible to determine with precision the particular distinctions on the basis of existing contexts, though possibly ἐκτελέω and ἀποτελέω emphasize more finality, while ἐπιτελέω\[a] may focus upon the resulting purpose.

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 15:28; 2 Corinthians 7:1; 2 Corinthians 8:6; 2 Corinthians 8:11; Galatians 3:3; Philippians 1:6; Hebrews 9:6; 1 Peter 5:9; Hebrews 8:5

needed, necessary, essential pertaining to being needed or necessary for some particular purpose1

1 In view of the more general meaning of ἐπιτήδειος in non\-biblical Greek, one could classify ἐπιτήδειος under Domain 71 Mode, since the focus in so many contexts outside of the Scriptures is upon suitability and fitness, but in the one occurrence in the NT, namely [JAS 2:16](https://ref.ly/Heb2:16), the element of lack is focal, namely, poverty as a more or less continuous state of lack of needed possessions.

to put on, to place on, to lay on to place something on something

[MAT 9:18](https://ref.ly/Matt9:18) · [MAT 19:13](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13) · [MAT 19:15](https://ref.ly/Matt19:15) · [MAT 21:7](https://ref.ly/Matt21:7) · [MAT 23:4](https://ref.ly/Matt23:4) · [MAT 27:29](https://ref.ly/Matt27:29) · [MAT 27:37](https://ref.ly/Matt27:37) · [MRK 5:23](https://ref.ly/Matt5:23) · [MRK 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5) · [MRK 7:32](https://ref.ly/Matt7:32) · [MRK 8:23](https://ref.ly/Matt8:23) · [MRK 8:25](https://ref.ly/Matt8:25) · [MRK 16:18](https://ref.ly/Matt16:18) · [LUK 4:40](https://ref.ly/Mark4:40) · [LUK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Mark13:13) · [LUK 15:5](https://ref.ly/Mark15:5) · [LUK 23:26](https://ref.ly/Mark23:26) · [JHN 9:15](https://ref.ly/Luke9:15) · [JHN 19:2](https://ref.ly/Luke19:2) · [ACT 6:6](https://ref.ly/John6:6) · [ACT 8:17](https://ref.ly/John8:17) · [ACT 8:19](https://ref.ly/John8:19) · [ACT 9:12](https://ref.ly/John9:12) · [ACT 9:17](https://ref.ly/John9:17) · [ACT 13:3](https://ref.ly/John13:3) · [ACT 19:6](https://ref.ly/John19:6) · [ACT 28:3](https://ref.ly/John28:3) · [ACT 28:8](https://ref.ly/John28:8) · [ACT 28:10](https://ref.ly/John28:10) · [1TI 5:22](https://ref.ly/2Thess5:22)

59\.72 · Quantity · Add, Subtract

to add to add something to an existing quantity In a number of languages it is not at all difficult to speak of ‘adding things,’ but to add events may provide some special difficulties in restructuring. For example, in [LUK 3:20](https://ref.ly/Mark3:20) it may be important to translate as ‘he had done many other bad things, but he also did this,’ and in [GAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:6) it may be helpful to translate ‘for those who were of repute did not say that I should do anything more than I was doing.’

[REV 22:18](https://ref.ly/Jude22:18)

90\.87 · Case · To Cause To Experience

to subject to to subject someone to a particular experience, normally by the use of force In [REV 22:18](https://ref.ly/Jude22:18), there is obviously a play on two different meanings of ἐπιτίθημι, ‘to add to’ and ‘to subject to.’ In the preceding clause ἐάν τις ἐπιθῇ ἐπ̓ αὐτά, the meaning is clearly ‘if anyone adds to these’ (see ἐπιτίθημι\[b], [59\.72](#59.72)), but in the second clause there is both the addition as well as the subjection of a person to punishment.

[LUK 10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:30) · [ACT 15:28](https://ref.ly/John15:28) · [ACT 16:23](https://ref.ly/John16:23) · [ACT 18:10](https://ref.ly/John18:10) · [REV 22:18](https://ref.ly/Jude22:18) · [REV 22:18](https://ref.ly/Jude22:18)

22\.27 · Trouble, Hardship, Relief, Favorable Circumstances · Cause Trouble, Hardship

**Collocation:** ἐπιτίθημι ζυγὸν ἐπὶ τὸν τράχηλον

to load down with obligations, to place a burden on the backs of (an idiom, literally: to put a yoke upon the neck) to cause difficulty to someone by requiring conformity to rules and regulations

[ACT 15:10](https://ref.ly/John15:10)

33\.128 · Communication · Name

**Collocation:** ἐπιτίθημι ὄνομα

to name, to give a name to (an idiom, literally: to place a name) to give a name to, with the possible implication of an additional name

[MRK 3:16](https://ref.ly/Matt3:16)1 · [MRK 3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:17)

57\.78 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Give

to give, to provide, to grant to place something at the disposal of someone else

[ACT 28:10](https://ref.ly/John28:10)

39\.47 · Hostility, Strife · Attack

to attack, to assault to use sudden physical force against someone as the outgrowth of a hostile attitude In a number of languages the equivalent of ‘to attack’ is simply ‘to start fighting against.’ In some languages, however, the equivalent is ‘to jump on’ or ‘to grab to do harm to.’

[ACT 18:10](https://ref.ly/John18:10)

1 MRK 3:16 In [LUK 6:14](https://ref.ly/Mark6:14), which is parallel to [MRK 3:16](https://ref.ly/Matt3:16), Luke uses ὀνομάζω meaning simply ‘to name’ (see [33\.127](#33.127)).

## ἐπιφώσκω (id: 002039000000000)

ἐπιφώσκω
========

ἐπιφώσκω (G2020)

φαίνω

14\.41 · Physical Events and States · Light

to dawn, to become light to change from darkness to light in the early morning hours In some languages dawning is divided into more than one aspect or period of time. The first may be only a glimmering of light in the east; the second period makes visibility quite easy; and the third period involves shafts of light or colored clouds visible in the sky immediately before sunrise. Usually an expression for one of these periods of time is more generalized in usage, and therefore it is probably satisfactory to adopt such an expression for any transition from darkness to light.

[MAT 28:1](https://ref.ly/Matt28:1) · [LUK 23:54](https://ref.ly/Mark23:54)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 28:1; Luke 23:54

## ἐπουράνιος (id: 002050000000000)

ἐπουράνιος
==========

ἐπουράνιος (G2032)

adj · οὐρανός

1\.8 · Geographical Objects and Features · Regions Above the Earth

in the sky, celestial (derivative of οὐρανός\[a] ‘sky,’ [1\.5](#1.5)) related to or located in the sky For ἐπουράνιος as part of an idiom, see [1\.26](#1.26).

[1CO 15:40](https://ref.ly/Rom15:40) · [1CO 15:40](https://ref.ly/Rom15:40)

1\.12 · Geographical Objects and Features · Regions Above the Earth

heavenly, in heaven, pertaining to heaven (derivative of οὐρανός\[b] heaven, [1\.11](#1.11)) related to or located in heaven The derivatives οὐράνιος and ἐπουράνιος\[b] are essentially equivalent to semantic compounds in that they combine a semantic element of heaven as a celestial abode with another semantic element specifying location or relationship. Frequently such terms are translated as ‘in heaven’ or ‘from heaven’ or ‘belonging to heaven.’

[1CO 15:48](https://ref.ly/Rom15:48) · [1CO 15:48](https://ref.ly/Rom15:48) · [1CO 15:49](https://ref.ly/Rom15:49) · [EPH 1:3](https://ref.ly/Gal1:3) · [EPH 1:20](https://ref.ly/Gal1:20) · [EPH 2:6](https://ref.ly/Gal2:6) · [EPH 3:10](https://ref.ly/Gal3:10) · [EPH 6:12](https://ref.ly/Gal6:12)1 · [PHP 2:10](https://ref.ly/Eph2:10) · [2TI 4:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:18) · [HEB 3:1](https://ref.ly/Phlm3:1) · [HEB 8:5](https://ref.ly/Phlm8:5) · [HEB 9:23](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:23) · [HEB 11:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:16) · [HEB 12:22](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:22)

12\.17 · Supernatural Beings and Powers · Supernatural Beings

from God (derivative of οὐρανός\[c] ‘God,’ [12\.16](#12.16)) pertaining to being derived from God Though it is possible to interpret [HEB 3:1](https://ref.ly/Phlm3:1) as a ‘calling which comes from heaven,’ it seems more appropriate in terms of the frequent references to ‘calling’ to recognize that the use of ἐπουράνιος in this context is simply a substitute for a direct reference to God. Accordingly, the passage may be effectively rendered as ‘those who have been called by God’ or ‘those whom God has called.’

[JHN 3:12](https://ref.ly/Luke3:12) · [HEB 3:1](https://ref.ly/Phlm3:1) · [HEB 6:4](https://ref.ly/Phlm6:4)

1\.26 · Geographical Objects and Features · Heavenly Bodies

**Collocation:** σῶμα ἐπουράνιον

heavenly body (an idiom, literally: heavenly body, occurring in the NT only in the plural2) the luminous objects in the sky: sun, moon, and other planets and stars See also [1\.8](#1.8).\|The phrase σῶμα ἐπουράνιον is a highly generic expression which in the plural is sometimes translatable as ‘lights in the sky,’ but more often than not it may be necessary to identify the various heavenly bodies as ‘sun, moon, and stars.’ In certain contexts one may also wish to identify the planets, often spoken of as ‘wandering stars’ or ‘moving stars’ (reflecting the etymology of the English word planet, which is derived from a Greek word meaning ‘wanderer’). These are, of course, not to be confused with shooting stars and comets in the sky.

[1CO 15:40](https://ref.ly/Rom15:40) · [1CO 15:40](https://ref.ly/Rom15:40)

1 EPH 6:12 It is possible that the adjectival derivative ἐπουράνιος in [EPH 6:12](https://ref.ly/Gal6:12) should be interpreted as a designation for the area of the sky in which wicked spiritual forces were, the supernatural aspects of these wicked forces and the challenge which such forces were regarded as posing for God and his hosts may justify relating such forces to the heavenly world rather than merely to the sky. 2 It is difficult to say whether the phrase σῶμα ἐπουράνιον should be treated as a fixed phrase with a shifted meaning for the head word σῶμα and thus a type of idiom, or whether it should be considered a semantically endocentric expression and thus not a so\-called lexical unit. The treatment of σῶμα ἐπουράνιον as semantically endocentric can certainly be justified by the highly generic meaning of σῶμα as body, object, but one may also argue that the restriction in reference to the sun, moon, planets, and stars provides a basis for regarding it as semantically exocentric.

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 15:40; 1 Corinthians 15:48; 1 Corinthians 15:49; Ephesians 1:3; Ephesians 1:20; Ephesians 2:6; Ephesians 3:10; Ephesians 6:12; Philippians 2:10; 2 Timothy 4:18; Hebrews 3:1; Hebrews 8:5; Hebrews 9:23; Hebrews 11:16; Hebrews 12:22; John 3:12; Hebrews 6:4

[MAT 7:23](https://ref.ly/Matt7:23) · [MAT 26:10](https://ref.ly/Matt26:10) · [MRK 14:6](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6) · [JHN 3:21](https://ref.ly/Luke3:21) · [JHN 6:28](https://ref.ly/Luke6:28) · [JHN 6:30](https://ref.ly/Luke6:30) · [JHN 9:4](https://ref.ly/Luke9:4) · [ACT 10:35](https://ref.ly/John10:35) · [ACT 13:41](https://ref.ly/John13:41) · [ROM 2:10](https://ref.ly/Acts2:10) · [ROM 13:10](https://ref.ly/Acts13:10) · [1CO 9:13](https://ref.ly/Rom9:13) · [JAS 2:9](https://ref.ly/Heb2:9) · [3JN 1:5](https://ref.ly/2John1:5)

13\.9 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · State

to cause to be, to make to be, to make, to result in, to bring upon, to bring about to cause a state to be1 It is also possible to interpret ἐργάζομαι in [MAT 7:23](https://ref.ly/Matt7:23) as ἐργάζομαι\[c] (see [90\.47](#90.47)).

[MAT 7:23](https://ref.ly/Matt7:23) · [2CO 7:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:10) · [GAL 6:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:10) · [HEB 11:33](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:33)

1 Any meaning involving ‘to cause to be’ implies some change in state, and therefore one could classify these meanings under Subdomain B Change of State in Domain 13 Be, Become, Exist, Happen. However, the focus seems to be primarily upon the changed state rather than upon the process of moving from one state to another. The fact that a change of state has taken place seems to be semantically incidental.

## ἐρεθίζω (id: 002060000000000)

ἐρεθίζω
=======

ἐρεθίζω (G2042)

88\.168 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Resentful, Hold a Grudge Against

to make resentful, to make someone bitter to cause someone to feel resentment

[COL 3:21](https://ref.ly/Phil3:21)

90\.55 · Case · Agent in a Causative Role Marked by Verbs

to cause, encouragement a marker of a causative relation, with the implication of stimulating a change in motivation or attitude The relevant action in [2CO 9:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:2)b comes from the immediately preceding statements in [2CO 9:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:1)\-[2CO 9:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:2)a.

[2CO 9:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:2)1

1 2CO 9:2 In the case of [2CO 9:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:2) it is difficult to know precisely how much meaning to attach to ἐρεθίζω in the sense of ‘to encourage’ or ‘to motivate.’ It is possible that ἐρεθίζω retains in this context a considerable amount of its more common meaning (see [88\.168](#88.168)). But in [2CO 9:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:2) it may also represent an instance in which the original meaning of a term has been largely lost or at least reduced to primarily a marker of causative relation. It appears likely that παροξυσμός in [HEB 10:24](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:24) has also been largely reduced to a marker of a causative relation, in other words, ‘to cause people to love and to do good,’ but since in this instance there seems to be some focus upon the psychological factors involved, one may translate παροξυσμός as ‘to encourage.’

* **Associated Passages:** Colossians 3:21; 2 Corinthians 9:2

## ἐρημία (id: 002063000000000)

ἐρημία
======

ἐρημία (G2047)

noun · ἐρημόομαι

1\.86 · Geographical Objects and Features · Areas Which Are Uninhabited or Only Sparsely Populated

desert, wilderness, lonely place a largely uninhabited region, normally with sparse vegetation (in contrast with πόλις\[a] ‘a population center,’ [1\.88](#1.88)) The phrase ἄνυδρος τόπος ([MAT 12:43](https://ref.ly/Matt12:43), [LUK 11:24](https://ref.ly/Mark11:24)), literally ‘waterless place,’ is a set phrase equivalent to ἐρημία and may be translated as ‘wilderness’ or ‘uninhabited country.’\|Throughout the NT ἐρημία and ἔρημος\[b] focus primarily upon the lack of population rather than upon sparse vegetation, though the two features are closely related ecologically in the Middle East.\|In most languages the most satisfactory equivalent of ἐρημία and ἔρημος\[b] is a word or phrase suggesting a place where few if any people live. Such expressions are generally far better than a word meaning ‘a bare place’ or ‘a place of sand,’ since in some languages such expressions could mean only a clearing in the forest or a sandy beach along a river bank. In the case of translations being made for people living in jungle areas, it may, however, be necessary to describe in a footnote the nature of an ἐρημία in NT times.

[MAT 15:33](https://ref.ly/Matt15:33) · [MRK 8:4](https://ref.ly/Matt8:4) · [2CO 11:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:26) · [HEB 11:38](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:38)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 15:33; Mark 8:4; 2 Corinthians 11:26; Hebrews 11:38

ἔρημος
======

ἔρημος (G2048)

adj · ἐρημόομαι

uninhabited, deserted pertaining to an absence of residents or inhabitants in a place

[MAT 23:38](https://ref.ly/Matt23:38) · [ACT 1:20](https://ref.ly/John1:20)

1\.86 · Geographical Objects and Features · Areas Which Are Uninhabited or Only Sparsely Populated

desert, wilderness, lonely place a largely uninhabited region, normally with sparse vegetation (in contrast with πόλις\[a] ‘a population center,’ [1\.88](#1.88)) The phrase ἄνυδρος τόπος ([MAT 12:43](https://ref.ly/Matt12:43) [LUK 11:24](https://ref.ly/Mark11:24)), literally ‘waterless place,’ is a set phrase equivalent to ἐρημία and may be translated as ‘wilderness’ or ‘uninhabited country.’\|Throughout the NT ἐρημία and ἔρημος\[b] focus primarily upon the lack of population rather than upon sparse vegetation, though the two features are closely related ecologically in the Middle East.\|In most languages the most satisfactory equivalent of ἐρημία and ἔρημος\[b] is a word or phrase suggesting a place where few if any people live. Such expressions are generally far better than a word meaning ‘a bare place’ or ‘a place of sand,’ since in some languages such expressions could mean only a clearing in the forest or a sandy beach along a river bank. In the case of translations being made for people living in jungle areas, it may, however, be necessary to describe in a footnote the nature of an ἐρημία in NT times.
